**Internal deps:** `proxybroker.errors (ProxyConnError, ProxyEmptyRecvError, ProxyRecvError, ProxySendError, ProxyTimeoutError, ResolveError)`, `proxybroker.negotiators (NGTRS — dict mapping the 6 protocol names to negotiator classes)`, `proxybroker.resolver (Resolver — uses .host_is_ip staticmethod, .get_ip_info staticmethod, and async .resolve)`, `proxybroker.utils (log — the package logging.Logger; parse_headers — HTTP header parser used by _recv)`
**External deps:** `asyncio (open_connection, wait_for, TimeoutError, IncompleteReadError, StreamReader, StreamWriter, StreamReaderProtocol, get_running_loop, loop.start_tls)`, `ssl (imported as _ssl — create_default_context, CERT_NONE, SSLError)`, `time (time.time for runtime measurement)`, `warnings (warnings.warn for the avgRespTime DeprecationWarning)`, `collections.Counter (error tallies in stat['errors'])`

**Port risks**

- **connect(ssl=True) reaches into CPython asyncio private internals to TLS-upgrade a live proxy tunnel: reads `self._writer['conn'].transport`, constructs a bare `asyncio.StreamReaderProtocol(asyncio.StreamReader())`, awaits `loop.start_tls(...)`, then reads the PRIVATE `protocol._stream_reader` and hand-builds an `asyncio.StreamWriter(ssl_transport, protocol, reader, loop)`.** — There is no Rust equivalent of this pattern because there is no equivalent problem — it exists purely to work around asyncio's lack of a public in-place TLS upgrade for the stream API. It also depends on a private attribute (`_stream_reader`) that CPython can rename in any point release, and this module already churned once (0ce07a7) chasing an asyncio deprecation. A literal transliteration would be porting a workaround for a bug the target language does not have. Compounding it: this exact path has ZERO test coverage. **Approach:** `tokio-rustls`: `TlsConnector::from(Arc<ClientConfig>).connect(server_name, tcp_stream).await` CONSUMES the plaintext `TcpStream` by value and returns `TlsStream<TcpStream>`. That is the whole upgrade — typed, safe, public API, zero private access. Model the conn/ssl slots as `enum Stream { Plain(TcpStream), Tls(Box<TlsStream<TcpStream>>) }` and upgrade with `std::mem::replace` + match. Use `rustls::pki_types::ServerName::IpAddress` since server_hostname is an IP literal here. (`tokio-native-tls` is the fallback if platform trust stores are required.)
- **Proxy.ngtr setter performs runtime class registration + dynamic dispatch + creates a parent/child reference cycle in one expression: `self._ngtr = NGTRS[proto](self)`, where NGTRS is a module-level `dict[str, type]` in negotiators.py. The negotiator stores the Proxy; the Proxy stores the negotiator. `close()` must null `_ngtr` to break it.** — Three Python-only mechanisms fused: (1) a dict of CLASSES indexed by string, instantiated at runtime — no static type known at the call site; (2) an unvalidated `NGTRS[proto]` that raises KeyError on a typo, i.e. protocol validity is a runtime concern; (3) a cycle that only survives because CPython's GC collects cycles. Rust's ownership model rejects (3) outright — `Rc<RefCell<Proxy>>` would leak the cycle, and `Arc<Mutex<>>` would deadlock the moment a negotiator called back into a locked proxy. **Approach:** `Box<dyn Negotiator>` produced by a `match ProxyType { Http => Box::new(HttpNgtr), Socks5 => Box::new(Socks5Ngtr), ... }` factory — matching an enum makes the KeyError a compile-time exhaustiveness check. INVERT the ownership to kill the cycle: make negotiators stateless zero-sized types and pass the proxy in — `#[async_trait] trait Negotiator { fn name(&self) -> &'static str; async fn negotiate(&self, proxy: &mut Proxy) -> Result<(), ProxyError>; }`. The Proxy then stores only `Option<ProxyType>`, no back-reference exists, and `close()`'s `_ngtr = None` cycle-break becomes unnecessary. Use `async-trait` (or native AFIT if the trait need not be object-safe across the boundary).
- **`_ssl_context` holds a duck-typed union: the literal bool `True` when verify_ssl=True, or an `ssl.SSLContext` when False. It is passed straight into `loop.start_tls(...)`, relying on asyncio interpreting `ssl=True` as 'build the default verifying context for me'.** — A bool and an SSLContext are unrelated types sharing one slot, with meaning assigned by a downstream callee's isinstance sniffing. Rust has no such slot. Worse, the False branch needs cert AND hostname verification fully disabled — rustls deliberately provides no `CERT_NONE` equivalent, considering it a footgun, so the 'easy' Python default is the HARD path in Rust. tests/test_proxy.py pins BOTH arms (`assert p._ssl_context is True` and the CERT_NONE assertions), so the union is a tested contract, not an accident. **Approach:** `enum TlsMode { VerifiedDefault, Unverified }` resolved lazily to an `Arc<rustls::ClientConfig>`. Verified: `ClientConfig::builder().with_root_certificates(webpki_roots::TLS_SERVER_ROOTS).with_no_client_auth()`. Unverified: implement `rustls::client::danger::ServerCertVerifier` returning `Ok(ServerCertVerified::assertion())` from every method, installed via `ClientConfig::dangerous().set_certificate_verifier(Arc::new(NoVerify))` under the `dangerous_configuration` feature. Gate behind a `#[cfg(feature)]` and keep it in ONE named module, preserving this file's existing discipline of isolating the unsafe choice.
- **Five distinct proxy error types are raised from `raise err from e` inside try/except/else/finally blocks whose `finally` clauses run telemetry on BOTH the success and the raise path — e.g. connect()'s finally increments `stat['requests'] += 1` and logs even when the connection failed; recv()'s finally logs the first 12 bytes of a partial response before the exception propagates.** — Rust has NO `finally`. The `?` operator returns immediately, skipping any trailing statements, so a naive port silently drops the request counter increment and the failure log line on every error path — changing `error_rate` (whose denominator IS `stat['requests']`) and therefore the entire pool ranking. This is a quiet correctness bug, not a compile error: the code would build and produce subtly wrong priorities forever. Additionally `raise ... from e` builds an explicit cause chain (`__cause__`) that must survive the port. **Approach:** `thiserror`: `#[derive(Error)] enum ProxyError { #[error("Connection: timeout")] Timeout(#[source] Elapsed), #[error("Connection: failed")] Conn(#[source] io::Error), Send(..), Recv(..), #[error("Received: 0 bytes")] EmptyRecv }` — `#[source]` reproduces `raise ... from e` exactly, and `std::error::Error::source()` walks the chain like `__cause__`. For `finally`: use `scopeguard::defer!` (runs on both normal and early return) for the unconditional `stat.requests += 1` + log, OR restructure to bind `let result = inner().await;` then run telemetry and `result?` last. Prefer the explicit-binding form — it is visible at review time, whereas a missing `defer!` is not.
- **`Proxy.log()` decides whether a measurement counts toward `avg_resp_time` by SNIFFING A SUBSTRING of the human-readable message: `if runtime and 'timeout' not in msg: self._runtimes.append(runtime)`. The msg has already been truncated to 60 chars at that point.** — Control flow driven by the text of a log string. Every error message in the module ('Connection: timeout', 'Received: timeout') is load-bearing program logic — rewording one, translating it, or having it truncated past the substring silently corrupts `avg_resp_time`, which feeds `priority`, which orders the whole proxy pool. A Rust port that formats messages via `thiserror`'s `#[error(...)]` attributes will produce the same strings by luck, not by design, and the coupling becomes invisible. **Approach:** Break the coupling at the port boundary — do NOT transliterate. Pass the typed error into the logger and match on the VARIANT: `fn log(&mut self, msg: &str, stime: Option<Instant>, err: Option<&ProxyError>)` with `let is_timeout = matches!(err, Some(ProxyError::Timeout(_)));` and `if runtime > 0.0 && !is_timeout { self.runtimes.push(runtime) }`. Use `tracing::debug!` with structured fields (host, ngtr, runtime) rather than a preformatted string. Also switch `time.time()` -> `std::time::Instant` (monotonic; `time.time()` is wall-clock and can jump backwards under NTP, producing negative runtimes that the `if runtime` truthiness check would then accept).
- **The `reader`/`writer` properties return one of two aliased halves of a single transport (`self._reader.get('ssl') or self._reader.get('conn')`), and `_recv` reads via `self.reader` while `send` writes via `self.writer` — both from `&self`-style access on the same underlying socket.** — Python hands out two independent references to two halves of one connection with no ownership tracking. Rust's borrow checker forbids `&mut self.reader` and `&mut self.writer` simultaneously if both project from the same `&mut self`, and returning `&mut` from a `&self` property is impossible. This is the single most likely place a direct port stalls on borrowck and gets 'fixed' by reaching for `Arc<Mutex<Stream>>`, which then deadlocks or serializes reads against writes. The `or` fallback also encodes an implicit 'TLS shadows plain' state machine as dict-lookup precedence. **Approach:** Use `tokio::io::split(stream)` to obtain owned `ReadHalf<T>`/`WriteHalf<T>` stored in separate fields — they are independently borrowable and designed for exactly this concurrent read/write case. Store as `struct Conn { rd: BufReader<ReadHalf<Stream>>, wr: WriteHalf<Stream> }` so `_recv` gets `&mut self.rd` and `send` gets `&mut self.wr` with no conflict. The `.get('ssl') or .get('conn')` precedence becomes the `Stream` enum's active variant — structural, not a lookup. Make the None case a typed `ProxyError::NotConnected` instead of today's AttributeError panic on a closed proxy.
- **`Proxy.create(cls, host, *args, **kwargs)` relies on positional/keyword punning: it pops `loop` and `resolver` from kwargs (eagerly evaluating `Resolver(loop=loop)` as the default on EVERY call, even when a resolver is passed), forwards `*args, **kwargs` blind to `__init__`, and recovers the port for its error message with `args[0] if args else kwargs.get('port', '?')` — an explicit workaround for the fact that it cannot know how the caller passed it.** — Rust has no *args/**kwargs, no keyword arguments, and no default-argument expressions evaluated per call. The `args[0] if args else kwargs.get('port')` line is a comment-documented scar from exactly this dynamism (an IndexError once masked the real error). `__init__` is additionally a FALLIBLE constructor raising ValueError on three separate conditions — Python constructors can raise, and callers of `Proxy(...)` depend on that. **Approach:** Builder pattern (also the house preference): `ProxyBuilder::new(host: &str).port(u16).types(&[ProxyType]).timeout(Duration).verify_ssl(bool).resolver(Arc<Resolver>).build().await -> Result<Proxy, ProxyError>`. `Option<Arc<Resolver>>` + `unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(Resolver::default()))` fixes the eager-default waste. The port ambiguity vanishes — the builder always knows its own field. `port: u16` makes the `> 65535` check unrepresentable; `host.parse::<IpAddr>()` makes `host_is_ip` structural. Keep the fallible constructor as the natural `Result` return, with `#[derive(Default)]` + `typed-builder` or a hand-rolled builder if compile-time required-field enforcement is wanted.
- **Shared mutable state with no synchronization, assuming GIL atomicity: `self.stat = {'requests': 0, 'errors': Counter()}` is incremented from `connect`/`log` while `error_rate` and `priority` read it concurrently from the pool; `_runtimes` and `_log` are unbounded append-only lists; and the `types` and `get_log` getters return INTERNAL mutable containers by reference so callers can mutate proxy state behind its back (silently defeating the `_schemes` cache invalidation, which only fires in the types setter).** — `Counter[k] += 1` and `list.append` are effectively atomic under the GIL, so this module never needed a lock despite being driven by many concurrent asyncio tasks. Under tokio's multi-threaded work-stealing runtime there is no GIL — a direct port with shared `&mut` access is either a compile error or, if wrapped in `Arc<Mutex<>>` reflexively, a contention point on the hot path (`error_rate` is O(n) over the Counter and is read on every pool sort). The by-reference getters are a genuine aliasing bug that the borrow checker will surface as a compile error — good, but it forces an API decision. **Approach:** `struct Stat { requests: u64, errors: HashMap<String, u64>, errors_total: u64 }` — keep a running `errors_total` so `error_rate` is O(1) instead of scanning the map on every sort. Prefer single-ownership (`&mut Proxy` threaded through negotiators per the ngtr fix) so NO lock is needed; only if the pool must share, use `Arc<Mutex<Proxy>>` at the POOL level, not per-field, or `AtomicU64` for the counters. Getters return `&HashMap`/`&[LogEntry]` — the borrow checker eliminates the aliased-mutation bug for free, and with it the `_schemes` staleness (drop the cache entirely; a 6-element intersection is cheaper than the bug). Bound `_runtimes`/`_log` with a fixed-capacity `VecDeque` — today they grow for the proxy's whole lifetime.
- **`asyncio.wait_for(self._recv(...), timeout=...)` cancels the inner coroutine by throwing `CancelledError` INTO it at an arbitrary await point; `_recv` separately recovers partial data from `asyncio.IncompleteReadError.partial` when `readexactly` falls short. connect() also carries a commented-out CancelledError handler (lines 393-395).** — Two different cancellation/partial-data semantics that do not map 1:1. (a) Python cancellation is EXCEPTION-based and unwinds through `finally` blocks — which is why recv's finally still logs; tokio cancellation is DROP-based (the future is simply dropped at an await point, no unwinding, no finally). The observable behavior happens to coincide here (`resp` is a local and is lost either way) but the mechanism differs, and any future `finally` added to a cancellable path will NOT run in Rust. (b) `IncompleteReadError.partial` returns the bytes read so far — tokio's `AsyncReadExt::read_exact` returns `ErrorKind::UnexpectedEof` and DISCARDS them. A direct port silently loses partial reads. **Approach:** `tokio::time::timeout(dur, fut).await` for the timeout — its drop-based cancel matches the observable outcome here. For the partial-read recovery, do NOT use `read_exact`: loop `AsyncReadExt::read_buf(&mut buf)` into a caller-owned `BytesMut` until `buf.len() == length` or read returns 0, then return whatever accumulated — this preserves `e.partial` semantics AND, because the buffer is owned by the caller rather than the future's locals, it survives cancellation (strictly better than the Python). Delete the dead CancelledError comment block; it has no analogue. If true cleanup-on-cancel is ever needed, that is `scopeguard` in the Drop impl, not a catch.
- **`_recv`'s hand-rolled HTTP framer: `while not self.reader.at_eof()` + `readline()`, with Content-Length counting, a `chunked` flag set only when Content-Length is absent, and chunked termination detected by the literal line `b'0\r\n'` — never decoding chunk-size lines. Plus `int(headers.get('Content-Length', 0))` and no maximum response size.** — Not hard to port mechanically — hard to port CORRECTLY, because the code has three latent bugs a faithful transliteration would enshrine: (1) the chunked path never parses chunk sizes, so a chunk whose DATA happens to be the line `0\r\n` truncates the response early; (2) `int(...)` raises a bare ValueError on a malformed Content-Length, escaping the module's typed-error contract entirely; (3) there is no size cap, so a hostile proxy — and this library connects to untrusted proxies BY DEFINITION — can stream unbounded data and OOM the process. `recv`/`_recv` have test references (14/8) so behavior is partly pinned, but these edges are exactly the untested ones. **Approach:** `httparse::Response::parse` for the header block + `tokio::io::AsyncBufReadExt::read_until(b'\n', &mut buf)` for the line loop, accumulating into `BytesMut` (`bytes` crate). Replace the 3-branch if/elif with an explicit `enum RecvState { Headers, Body { remaining: usize }, Chunked { remaining: usize } }` — carrying `remaining` in the Chunked variant forces the chunk-size decoding the Python omits, fixing bug (1) by construction. Parse Content-Length with `str::parse::<usize>().map_err(|_| ProxyError::Recv(..))?` for bug (2). Wrap the reader in `AsyncReadExt::take(MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES)` for bug (3). DECIDE EXPLICITLY per bug whether to fix or replicate — write a characterization test against the Python first (this is the module's deepest-nested function and its edges are untested).

---

### proxybroker/checker.py

*Validates candidate proxies by negotiating each requested protocol through a randomly-selected judge, verifying the echoed test request, and classifying the resulting anonymity level (Transparent/Anonymous/High).*

**LOC** 390 · **Tags** `HOT: 41 commits touching proxybroker/checker.py — the highest-churn surface in the package; expect the Rust port to keep absorbing changes, so favor testable pure functions over one monolithic check().`, `COMPLEX: no single function exceeds 80 lines (largest is __init__ at ~50, check_judges at ~48), but Checker._check and Checker._check_conn_25 are deeply nested — for-loop wrapping try/except/else/finally with continue-on-timeout, break-on-protocol-error, and a finally that must run proxy.close() on every path including the loop-control paths. Rust has no else/finally, so this is the module's highest-risk restructuring.`, `UNTESTED: partial. tests/test_checker.py covers Checker.__init__ (config/kwarg contracts), _get_anonymity_lvl (9 cases incl. dual-stack, uppercase/expanded IPv6, legacy-str) and _check_test_response (3 cases). NO test references Checker.check_judges, Checker.check, Checker._check, Checker._check_conn_25, Checker._in_DNSBL, Checker._types_passed, _request, _send_test_request, _decompress_content, or ProxyChecker — i.e. every network path and every retry/error branch is unverified. Port these behind a trait-mocked transport and write the tests the Python side never had.`, `STALE: ProxyChecker has been deprecated since the rename recorded in CHANGELOG.md:306/312, is absent from proxybroker/__init__.py __all__, and has zero callers anywhere in the repo outside its own definition. Also dead: the `headers` parameter of _check_test_response is never read, and Checker._real_ext_ip (legacy single-string accessor) has no in-repo consumer.`, `TODO: proxybroker/checker.py:91 — literal '# TODO: need refactoring' on Checker.check_judges, which mixes judge health-checking, global protocol-flag mutation, event signalling, warning emission and error raising in one method.`

**Public API (4)**

| Symbol | Kind | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `Checker` | class | Stateful proxy checker holding judge list, retry/timeout config, requested type filter, DNSBL list and the host's real external IP set; mutates global Judge class state via Judge.clear() at construction. |
| `Checker.__init__` | method | Side effect: calls Judge.clear() wiping the GLOBAL Judge.available registry, then builds judges via get_judges(judges, timeout, verify_ssl); normalizes real_ext_ip (str) / real_ext_ips (iterable, keyword-only, wins if both given) into frozenset, defensively wrapping a bare str so it isn't iterated per-character; sets legacy self._real_ext_ip to an arbitrary member; captures asyncio.get_running_loop() falling back to self._loop = loop (possibly None) on RuntimeError; constructs Resolver; derives _req_http_proto/_req_https_proto/_req_smtp_proto by set-intersecting protocol tuples with types.keys() (all True when types is falsy); _ngtrs = set of keys of types or NGTRS. |
| `Checker.check_judges` | async_method | Concurrently checks all judges via asyncio.gather (no return_exceptions — a single judge raising aborts the whole gather); retains only j.is_working; for each of HTTP/HTTPS/SMTP with zero entries in the GLOBAL Judge.available, disables the corresponding _req_*_proto flag, sets Judge.ev[proto] to unblock already-waiting check() coroutines, and removes disabled protocols from self._ngtrs; emits a UserWarning naming missing judges; raises RuntimeError('Not found judges') if no judge survives. Carries an in-source 'TODO: need refactoring'. Mutates self._judges, self._ngtrs, self._req_*_proto and global Judge state. |
| `Checker.check` | async_method | Main entry point. Returns False early (logging 'Found in DNSBL') if DNSBL configured and host listed. Awaits the global Judge.ev[HTTP/HTTPS/SMTP] events for each required protocol (blocks indefinitely until check_judges sets them). Intersects proxy.expected_types with self._ngtrs (falling back to all _ngtrs). Sequentially (NOT concurrently) checks each protocol, dispatching CONNECT:25 to _check_conn_25 and everything else to _check. Sets proxy.is_working = any(results) (mutation), returns True only if working AND _types_passed(proxy). |

<details><summary><b>Private / internal symbols (11)</b></summary>

| Symbol | Kind | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `Checker._types_passed` | method | Returns True immediately if no type filter configured. Iterates a copy of proxy.types: in non-strict mode returns True on the first protocol whose level matches (or whose required-level set is empty); in strict mode DELETES non-matching protocols from proxy.types (mutating the proxy) and returns True only if any survive. On failure logs 'Protocol or the level of anonymity differs from the requested' to the proxy and returns False. |
| `Checker._in_DNSBL` | async_method | Reverses the dotted host, appends each configured DNSBL domain, resolves all queries concurrently with return_exceptions=True (logging suppressed), and returns True if any response is both truthy and not a ResolveError instance. Quirk: a non-ResolveError exception (OSError/timeout) is a truthy object and therefore counts as a DNSBL hit; an empty-but-successful resolve result is falsy and is discarded. Returns False otherwise. Network I/O via Resolver. |
| `Checker._check_conn_25` | async_method | SMTP-only path. Picks a random judge, retries up to _max_tries: assigns proxy.ngtr = proto (string -> negotiator instance via setter), connects, negotiates to judge.host/judge.ip. ProxyTimeoutError -> continue (retry); ProxyConnError/ProxyRecvError/ProxySendError/ProxyEmptyRecvError/BadStatusError/BadResponseError -> break (give up); success -> sets proxy.types[ngtr.name] = None (no anonymity level for SMTP), result True, break. proxy.close() runs in finally on EVERY path including continue/break. Returns bool. Network I/O; mutates proxy.types, proxy.ngtr. |
| `Checker._check` | async_method | Non-SMTP path. Same retry/connect/negotiate skeleton as _check_conn_25, then sends the test request via _send_test_request, decompresses the body, validates the echo via _check_test_response, and on success computes the anonymity level via _get_anonymity_lvl only when proxy.ngtr.check_anon_lvl is set (True only for HttpNgtr) else None, storing it in proxy.types[ngtr.name]. Same timeout->continue / protocol-error->break policy; proxy.close() in finally on every path. Returns bool. Network I/O; mutates proxy.types, proxy.ngtr. |
| `_request` | function | Pure. Builds a raw HTTP/1.1 request as bytes plus the random verification marker rv from get_headers(rv=True); sets Host, Connection: close, Content-Length (as an int, stringified later by the f-string), and Content-Type: application/octet-stream for POST; uses an absolute URI as the path when fullpath is set. Returns (bytes, rv_str). No I/O. |
| `_send_test_request` | async_function | Sends the built request through the proxy and reads the response. Raises BadStatusError (the CLASS, not an instance) if get_status_code(resp) != 200; raises BadResponseError from a ValueError when the response cannot be split on the b'\r\n\r\n' header/body boundary. The finally block logs 'Get: success/failed' to the proxy and debug-logs judge URL, rv and the full response on every path (including the raising ones) before the exception propagates. Returns (headers_bytes, content_bytes, rv). Network I/O. |
| `_decompress_content` | function | Pure. Parses headers; if Content-Encoding is gzip/deflate, first de-chunks when Transfer-Encoding is chunked by taking a naive stride-2 slice of the b'\r\n' split (content.split(b'\r\n')[1::2]), then zlib.decompress with MAX_WBITS\|32 (auto gzip/zlib detect), swallowing zlib.error into b''. Always returns a str via content.decode('utf-8', 'ignore') — invalid bytes are DROPPED, not replaced. No I/O. |
| `_check_test_response` | function | Pure except for proxy.log side effects. Returns True only if all four hold: the rv marker is echoed in the body, get_headers()['Referer'] is echoed, get_headers()['Cookie'] is echoed, and get_all_ip(content) is non-empty. Logs 'Response: correct' or a per-flag breakdown and returns False otherwise. Note `headers` is accepted but never used. |
| `_get_anonymity_lvl` | function | Pure except for proxy.log. Lowercases the body, extracts all IPs, and normalizes real_ext_ips accepting None (-> empty, Transparent disabled), a single str (legacy contract, canonicalized with fallback to the raw string), or an iterable of strings. Returns 'Transparent' if ANY real ext-IP (v4 or v6) intersects the found IPs, else 'Anonymous' if the body's 'via'/'proxy' counts exceed the judge's baseline judge.marks, else 'High'. Logs the verdict to the proxy. |
| `ProxyChecker` | class | Deprecated backward-compat alias subclass of Checker, adding no behavior. |
| `ProxyChecker.__init__` | method | Emits a DeprecationWarning (stacklevel=2) then forwards all args/kwargs unchanged to Checker.__init__. |

</details>

<details><summary><b>Per-symbol Rust mapping notes (15)</b></summary>

- `Checker` — struct Checker { judges: Vec<Judge>, method: Method, max_tries: u32, real_ext_ips: HashSet<IpAddr>, strict: bool, dnsbl: Vec<String>, types: HashMap<Protocol, HashSet<AnonLvl>>, ngtrs: HashSet<Protocol>, resolver: Arc<Resolver>, req_http/https/smtp_proto: bool }. Do NOT reproduce Judge.clear() global reset — inject an Arc<JudgePool> so instances are isolated (see port_risks).
- `Checker.__init__` — Builder pattern (CheckerBuilder) — 11 params with 10 defaults and a keyword-only arg is exactly the builder case. real_ext_ip/real_ext_ips collapse into one `.real_ext_ips(impl IntoIterator<Item = IpAddr>)`; the str-vs-iterable defensive branch vanishes under the type system. `timeout`/`verify_ssl` are pass-through to get_judges only — keep them on the judge builder, not on Checker. Drop `loop` entirely (tokio has no loop handle to capture).
- `Checker.check_judges` — async fn check_judges(&mut self) -> Result<(), CheckerError>. Use futures::future::join_all (NOT try_join_all) only if you intend to keep abort-on-first-error — current gather() semantics propagate the first exception, so try_join_all is the faithful port. Note SMTP is disabled without being added to `nojudges`, so the warning never names SMTP while still listing it in disable_protocols — preserve or fix deliberately. `warnings.warn` -> tracing::warn!. RuntimeError -> CheckerError::NoJudges.
- `Checker._types_passed` — fn types_passed(&self, proxy: &mut Proxy) -> bool — needs &mut because the strict path prunes proxy.types. The .copy()-then-del dance becomes proxy.types.retain(|proto, lvl| ...) in the strict branch; the non-strict branch is a short-circuiting .any(). Split the two modes into separate branches rather than one interleaved loop — the Python loop conflates them and the strict/non-strict paths never actually share the body.
- `Checker._in_DNSBL` — async fn in_dnsbl(&self, host: &str) -> bool. join_all -> Vec<Result<Vec<IpAddr>, ResolveError>>; faithful predicate is `.any(|r| match r { Ok(ips) => !ips.is_empty(), Err(ResolveError) => false, Err(_) => true })`. The IPv4-only dotted-reverse breaks for IPv6 hosts — decide explicitly. Consider hickory-resolver.
- `Checker.check` — async fn check(&self, proxy: &mut Proxy) -> bool. The per-protocol loop is sequential and must stay so (each iteration reuses the single proxy connection). Judge.ev waits -> tokio::sync::watch::Receiver<bool>::wait_for(|v| *v) — sticky semantics are required (see port_risks). Prefer Result<CheckOutcome, CheckError> over bare bool so DNSBL-reject and all-protocols-failed are distinguishable.
- `Checker._check_conn_25` — async fn check_conn_25(&self, proxy: &mut Proxy, proto: Protocol) -> bool. No else/finally in Rust: run the fallible body in an inner async block, bind the Result, call proxy.close() unconditionally, THEN match to decide continue/break — or use scopeguard::defer. Errors -> matches!(e, CheckError::Timeout) => continue, _ => break. The `None` level maps to Option<AnonLvl>::None, so proxy.types is HashMap<Protocol, Option<AnonLvl>>.
- `Checker._check` — async fn check(&self, proxy: &mut Proxy, proto: Protocol) -> bool. Deepest control flow in the module (for + try/except/else/finally + nested if) — restructure per _check_conn_25's note. check_anon_lvl/use_full_path are per-negotiator constants: model as associated consts on a Negotiator trait or a match on the Protocol enum, not runtime attribute lookup.
- `_request` — fn build_request(method: Method, host: &str, path: &str, fullpath: bool, data: &[u8]) -> (Vec<u8>, String). Hand-rolled serialization — do NOT swap in `http`/`hyper`: the byte layout and header ORDER are load-bearing because _check_test_response greps the echoed page for these exact strings. Keep an IndexMap or a fixed-order array to preserve insertion order (Python dicts are ordered; HashMap is not).
- `_send_test_request` — async fn send_test_request(method, proxy: &mut Proxy, judge: &Judge) -> Result<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>, String), CheckError>. `resp.split(b'\r\n\r\n', maxsplit=1)` + starred unpack -> `resp.split_once(b"\r\n\r\n").ok_or(CheckError::BadResponse)?` — with maxsplit=1 the `*_` is always empty, so the ValueError only ever fires on too-few values. The finally-logs-then-reraises pattern needs an explicit log call on both arms of the match (or scopeguard::defer), since `?` skips any trailing code.
- `_decompress_content` — fn decompress_content(headers: &[u8], content: &[u8]) -> String. Two faithfulness traps: (1) flate2 has no MAX_WBITS|32 auto-detect — sniff the 0x1f 0x8b magic to pick GzDecoder vs ZlibDecoder, and map any decode error to an empty body; (2) String::from_utf8_lossy inserts U+FFFD whereas Python's errors='ignore' DROPS the bytes — use encoding_rs or a manual std::str::from_utf8 loop that skips invalid sequences, or the echo-marker greps in _check_test_response can diverge.
- `_check_test_response` — fn check_test_response(proxy: &Proxy, content: &str, rv: &str) -> bool — drop the dead `headers` param. Depends on get_headers() being PURE for Referer/Cookie (verified: only User-Agent varies, via rv); if the Rust port randomizes any other header, this comparison silently breaks. Hoist Referer/Cookie to `const` rather than re-deriving them.
- `_get_anonymity_lvl` — fn anonymity_lvl(real_ext_ips: &HashSet<IpAddr>, proxy: &Proxy, judge: &Judge, content: &str) -> AnonLvl. Model as `enum AnonLvl { Transparent, Anonymous, High }` (Display for the log's lvl[:4] truncation). The str-or-iterable polymorphism is a Python-only compat shim — collapse to one typed param. Parsing to IpAddr makes canonicalize_ip's normalization structural and free. NB: content.lower() is applied before IP extraction, which is what makes uppercase IPv6 leaks match.
- `ProxyChecker` — Omit from the port, or emit `#[deprecated(note = "use Checker")] pub type ProxyChecker = Checker;`. Not exported from __init__.py's __all__ and has zero in-repo callers — see STALE tag.
- `ProxyChecker.__init__` — No equivalent — Rust deprecation is compile-time via #[deprecated], not a runtime warning. If dropped entirely, note it as a breaking change in the port's migration notes.

</details>

**Internal deps:** `proxybroker.errors (BadResponseError, BadStatusError, ProxyConnError, ProxyEmptyRecvError, ProxyRecvError, ProxySendError, ProxyTimeoutError, ResolveError)`, `proxybroker.judge (Judge, get_judges)`, `proxybroker.negotiators (NGTRS)`, `proxybroker.resolver (Resolver)`, `proxybroker.utils (canonicalize_ip, get_all_ip, get_headers, get_status_code, log, parse_headers)`
**External deps:** `asyncio (gather, get_running_loop, Event via Judge.ev)`, `time (time — judge-check runtime measurement only)`, `warnings (warn — UserWarning for missing judges, DeprecationWarning for ProxyChecker)`, `zlib (decompress, MAX_WBITS, error)`

**Port risks**

- **Judge.available / Judge.ev are class-level mutable dicts acting as a global registry, and Checker.__init__ calls Judge.clear() which wipes them for every other live Checker.** — Python class attributes give free cross-instance shared mutable state. Rust has no equivalent: a `static` needs Mutex/OnceLock, which reproduces the exact test-isolation bug (constructing a second Checker nukes the first one's judges) and forces lock-ordering discipline across the whole check path. **Approach:** Do not port the global. Introduce `struct JudgePool { available: HashMap<Protocol, Vec<Arc<Judge>>>, ready: HashMap<Protocol, watch::Sender<bool>> }` and inject `Arc<JudgePool>` into Checker. Drop Judge.clear() entirely — pool lifetime replaces it. Use arc-swap or tokio::sync::RwLock for the available lists.
- **asyncio.Event semantics in Judge.ev: check_judges calls .set() specifically 'for coroutines, which is already waiting', and Checker.check awaits events that may have been set BEFORE the await is reached.** — asyncio.Event is sticky — set() latches, so a later wait() returns immediately. tokio::sync::Notify is NOT sticky (notify_waiters drops the signal if nobody is parked yet), so a naive swap introduces a rare deadlock where check() hangs forever on a protocol whose judges were already disabled. This is a real missed-wakeup race, not a style issue. **Approach:** Use `tokio::sync::watch::channel(false)` per protocol and `rx.wait_for(|ready| *ready).await` — latched and multi-consumer, matching asyncio.Event exactly. `event-listener::Event` is also non-sticky; `tokio::sync::Semaphore::add_permits` with `acquire` is a workable alternative. Never Notify.
- **`proxy.ngtr = proto` assigns a STRING and the property setter instantiates a negotiator class looked up in the NGTRS dict at runtime; the code then reads proxy.ngtr.name / .check_anon_lvl / .use_full_path / .negotiate() by duck typing.** — This is runtime class registration plus dynamic dispatch through a string key — the protocol set is open at runtime and there is no compile-time guarantee a key exists (a bad proto is a KeyError deep inside the retry loop). **Approach:** `enum Protocol { Http, Https, Connect80, Connect25, Socks4, Socks5 }` (FromStr + Display for the wire/config boundary), with negotiation behind a `trait Negotiator { const CHECK_ANON_LVL: bool; const USE_FULL_PATH: bool; async fn negotiate(&mut self, host: &str, ip: IpAddr) -> Result<(), CheckError>; }`. Use enum_dispatch or a match returning Box<dyn Negotiator> to avoid the dyn-async-trait overhead; the NGTRS dict becomes an exhaustive match the compiler checks.
- **try/except/else/finally inside a retry for-loop, where `finally: proxy.close()` executes on the continue path, the break path, and the success path alike.** — Rust has neither `else` nor `finally`, and `?` early-returns past any trailing cleanup. A direct transliteration silently leaks the connection on the error paths — the exact class of bug that makes a proxy checker report false negatives under load. **Approach:** `for _ in 0..max_tries { let r: Result<_, CheckError> = async { ... }.await; proxy.close(); match r { Err(e) if e.is_timeout() => continue, Err(_) => break, Ok(v) => { ...; break } } }`. Bind the Result before cleanup so `?` can't skip it. scopeguard::defer! is the alternative if the body needs `?` internally; prefer the explicit form here since the cleanup is one call.
- **`err = BadStatusError; raise err` raises the exception CLASS rather than an instance, and the `finally` block reads that same `err` binding for logging before the exception propagates.** — Raising a class (Python auto-instantiates it) combined with a finally-block that inspects a local set by the try-block is a control-flow/data-flow entanglement with no Rust analogue; error identity is also used for the continue-vs-break policy in the callers. **Approach:** `#[derive(thiserror::Error)] enum CheckError { Timeout, Conn, Recv, Send, EmptyRecv, BadStatus(u16), BadResponse }` with an `is_timeout()` helper driving the retry match. Log explicitly on both match arms instead of relying on finally; `anyhow` only at the binary's edges.
- **`content.decode('utf-8', 'ignore')` in _decompress_content DROPS invalid bytes, and `zlib.decompress(content, zlib.MAX_WBITS | 32)` auto-detects gzip vs zlib framing.** — Rust's idiomatic equivalents differ behaviorally on both counts: String::from_utf8_lossy INSERTS U+FFFD replacement characters (changing byte offsets and potentially breaking the rv/Referer/Cookie substring greps that decide whether a proxy passes), and flate2 has no auto-detect wbits mode. **Approach:** Decode with encoding_rs (UTF-8 decoder configured to skip, not replace) or a manual std::str::from_utf8 chunk loop that discards invalid sequences. For decompression, sniff the 0x1f 0x8b gzip magic and pick flate2::read::GzDecoder, else ZlibDecoder, mapping any error to an empty body to mirror the `except zlib.error: content = b''` swallow.
- **Chunked-body handling is `content.split(b'\r\n')[1::2]` — a naive stride-2 slice, not a chunked-transfer parser, and it only runs when the body is ALSO compressed.** — The Python code is subtly wrong (it corrupts any chunk whose payload contains \r\n, and never de-chunks uncompressed chunked bodies). A 'correct' Rust port using a real parser changes which proxies pass the check — a silent behavioral divergence that will look like a port regression in the field. **Approach:** Decide explicitly and document it. Faithful: replicate the stride-2 slice. Correct: use httparse or hyper's chunked decoder and de-chunk regardless of Content-Encoding. Recommend shipping the faithful version first, gated behind a `strict_chunked` feature flag, so the port can be diffed against Python 1:1 before fixing.
- **_in_DNSBL's `any([r for r in responses if not isinstance(r, ResolveError)])` conflates exception objects with successful results under Python truthiness.** — Duck-typed truthiness means a non-ResolveError exception (OSError, timeout) is a truthy object and therefore votes 'proxy is blacklisted', while a successful-but-empty resolve result is falsy and is silently discarded. Rust's Result forces this ambiguity to be resolved at the type level, so the porter must consciously choose a semantic. **Approach:** `join_all` -> `Vec<Result<Vec<IpAddr>, ResolveError>>`, then `.iter().any(|r| match r { Ok(ips) => !ips.is_empty(), Err(ResolveError::NotFound) => false, Err(_) => true })` to preserve current behavior — and add a comment that treating transport errors as a DNSBL hit is inherited, likely unintended. hickory-resolver for the resolver itself.
- **_check_test_response re-derives its expected values by calling get_headers() a second time instead of receiving what _request actually sent.** — This is an implicit purity contract across module boundaries: it works today only because get_headers() randomizes User-Agent alone (via rv) and returns constant Referer/Cookie. Any future randomization of those headers breaks proxy validation silently, with no test catching it (no test covers _request or _send_test_request). **Approach:** Make the coupling explicit: have build_request return a `TestMarkers { rv: String, referer: &'static str, cookie: &'static str }` and thread it into check_test_response. Hoist the constant headers to `const REFERER: &str` / `const COOKIE: &str`. This removes the hidden invariant entirely rather than documenting it.
- **Ordered-dict dependence in _request: headers are serialized in insertion order and the resulting byte layout is what the echo-greps later search for.** — Python dicts preserve insertion order as a language guarantee; Rust's HashMap explicitly randomizes iteration order per process (SipHash with a random seed), so a HashMap-based port produces a different request on every run. **Approach:** Use indexmap::IndexMap, a `Vec<(&str, String)>`, or a fixed const array of header names. Avoid the `http::HeaderMap`/hyper serializer — it normalizes casing and ordering, and this code hand-builds the raw request deliberately.
- **`asyncio.get_running_loop()` captured in __init__ with a RuntimeError fallback that leaves self._loop = None, plus a `loop` parameter threaded to Resolver.** — Explicit event-loop handles are an asyncio-ism with no tokio counterpart; a half-initialized `_loop = None` object is a latent AttributeError that the type system should eliminate rather than reproduce. **Approach:** Delete the concept. Tokio resolves the runtime ambiently at await points; if a handle is genuinely needed use tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current(). Removing `loop` from the public constructor is a breaking API change — record it in the port's migration notes alongside the ProxyChecker removal.
- **Checker.check mutates the proxy it is handed (proxy.is_working, proxy.types, proxy.ngtr) while _types_passed concurrently deletes entries from proxy.types during iteration of a .copy().** — Python's shared-object aliasing lets check(), _check() and _types_passed() all mutate one Proxy freely with no ownership discipline; the .copy() exists purely to dodge a mutation-during-iteration RuntimeError. Rust's borrow checker rejects the whole shape, and the Broker layer likely wants these proxies across tasks. **Approach:** Thread `&mut Proxy` through check/_check/_check_conn_25/_types_passed (single owner, no aliasing — the .copy() disappears in favor of HashMap::retain). If the Broker needs concurrent access, use Arc<Mutex<Proxy>> at the Broker boundary and lock once per check() call, not per mutation, to keep the check path free of interleaving.

---

### proxybroker/providers.py

*Defines the `Provider` scraper base class (HTTP fetch → regex/JS-deobfuscation → (host, port) extraction), 28 site-specific subclasses, and the module-level `PROVIDERS` registry of pre-instantiated providers that `api.Broker` fans out over to harvest free public proxy lists.*

**LOC** 806 · **Tags** `HOT: 94 name-only entries in `git log -- proxybroker/providers.py` out of 662 total repo commits — the single most-churned module in the package (api.py 83, resolver.py 83, proxy.py 73, server.py 67, utils.py 53, provider_utils.py 27). Churn is inherent: every provider site that changes its markup forces an edit here.`, `COMPLEX: strictly, NO function exceeds 80 lines (longest is Provider._get at 27, then Aliveproxy_com._pipe 21, Gatherproxy_com._pipe 19, Provider.__init__ 19, Provider.find_proxies 18 — 48 functions total, measured via AST). Tag applied on the density/nesting axis instead: Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.find_proxies is a 4-stage deobfuscator (sqrt→banker's-round→per-index XOR over code points→unquote→html-unescape→regex), and Spys_ru.find_proxies emulates a JS XOR-variable expression evaluator via a re.sub callback reading order-dependent mutable class state. Also the 112-line PROVIDERS literal (lines 695-806) is the largest single top-level symbol.`, `STALE: heavy. (1) 5 provider classes are fully defined but COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS — Proxyb_net, Proxz_com, Proxynova_com, _50kproxies_com, Free_proxy_cz. (2) ~20 more lines of commented-out dead-site entries (proxydb.net, cybersyndrome, ip-adress.com, marcosbl, go4free, blackstarsecurity, get-proxy.net). (3) Large commented-out logic blocks: Free_proxy_cz._pipe lines 596-619 (abandoned rate-limit batching), Gatherproxy_com.find_proxies lines 360-365 (abandoned gp.dep branch), Spys_ru._pipe lines 563-567 (abandoned country crawl), Aliveproxy_com line 283, Tools_rosinstrument_com_base lines 402-403. (4) Blogspot_com_base._cookies={'NCR':1} is unreachable dead code — shadowed by `self._cookies={}` in Provider.__init__. (5) Provider._loop is assigned and never read. (6) Many registered domains are long defunct (gatherproxy.com ~2018, freeproxylists.com, xseo.in, nntime.com, aliveproxy.com, foxtools.ru, maxiproxies.com). (7) Proxylist_me._pipe carries an inline 'Pre-existing master bug' comment.`, `UNTESTED: tests/test_providers.py covers ONLY the `Provider` base class (construction, the proxies setter, find_proxies incl. the IPv6 masking cases, and _find_on_pages([])). Grep of tests/ finds ZERO references to any of the 28 concrete subclasses, to `PROVIDERS`, or to `ProxyProvider`. Specifically untested: every `_pipe` override, every `find_proxies` override, every `char_js_port_to_num`, all base64/hex/XOR decode paths, Provider.get retry logic, Provider._get error handling, and get_proxies session lifecycle. The only other find_proxies test (tests/test_custom_provider_examples.py) exercises examples/, not this module.`, `TODO: no literal TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK markers exist in the file. Tag applied for the equivalent recorded-but-unfixed work: the 'Pre-existing master bug' note at line 338, the '# # Bad...' disabled-provider block at line 785, and the abandoned commented implementations listed under STALE — each is an unresolved decision the port must explicitly accept or discard.`

**Public API (49)**

| Symbol | Kind | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `Provider` | class | Template-method base for all scrapers; holds url/domain/proto/session/cookies/_proxies set and a per-provider asyncio.Semaphore; subclasses override `_pipe`, `find_proxies`, and/or the `_pattern` class attribute. `domain` is set in __init__ only when url is truthy, otherwise subclasses supply it as a class attr (a bare `Provider()` has NO `domain` and `get_proxies` would raise AttributeError on its first log line). |
| `Provider.__init__` | method | Sets self.domain = urlparse(url).netloc only if url truthy; stores url/proto/_max_tries/_timeout; inits _session=None, _cookies={} (this instance attr SHADOWS and kills Blogspot_com_base._cookies={'NCR':1}), _proxies=set(); constructs asyncio.Semaphore(max_conn); resolves self._loop = loop or asyncio.get_running_loop(), swallowing RuntimeError to None. `self._loop` is assigned and then NEVER read anywhere in the module — dead field. Because PROVIDERS is built at import time (no running loop), _loop is always None for bundled providers. |
| `Provider.proxies` | property | Returns the internal `_proxies` set directly (no copy) — callers can mutate provider state through the returned reference. Elements are (host, port, proto) tuples. |
| `Provider.proxies.setter` | method | MISLEADING NAME: assignment APPENDS, never replaces. Rebuilds each item as (host, port, self.proto), silently DROPS entries whose port is falsy (''/None/0), then `self._proxies.update(new)` for set dedup. Unpacks `for host, port in new` — so any 2-element iterable works (Proxy_list_org feeds it 2-element *lists*). Mutates self._proxies; returns nothing; raises ValueError if any element does not unpack to exactly 2. |
| `Provider.get_proxies` | async_method | Public entry point. Logs debug, opens `aiohttp.ClientSession(headers=get_headers(), cookies=self._cookies)` bound to `self._session` via `async with ... as self._session`, awaits `self._pipe()`, closes the session on exit (leaving self._session as a CLOSED session object, not None), logs count, returns self.proxies. Does NOT reset _proxies — repeat calls accumulate. Exceptions raised by `_pipe` propagate to the caller (api.py); only `find_proxies` errors are swallowed downstream. |
| `Provider.get` | async_method | Retry wrapper: loops up to `self._max_tries`, calling `self._get`, breaking on the first truthy page. Returns the last page (possibly ''). No backoff/sleep between attempts. LATENT BUG: `page` is only bound inside the loop, so `max_tries=0` raises UnboundLocalError. |
| `Provider.find_proxies` | method | Public parse hook (18 lines). Masks every `[v6]:port` span with equal-length spaces BEFORE the IPv4 pass so `[::ffff:192.0.2.1]:8080` cannot also emit a phantom `192.0.2.1:8080`; runs `self._find_proxies(masked)`; then appends `(canonicalize_ip(raw_v6), port)` for each bracketed v6 match, dropping any that fail canonicalization (None). Returns list[tuple[str,str]]. Pure — no I/O, no mutation. Overridden by 8 subclasses which BYPASS the IPv6 path entirely. |
| `Freeproxylists_com` | class | domain = 'freeproxylists.com'. Two-stage scraper: index pages → timestamped load_{type}_{uts}.html endpoints. |
| `Blogspot_com_base` | class | Shared blogspot crawler. Declares `_cookies = {'NCR': 1}` (force no-country-redirect) — DEAD CODE: `Provider.__init__` unconditionally sets `self._cookies = {}`, shadowing it, so the NCR cookie is never sent. Expects subclasses to define `domains` list. |
| `Blogspot_com` | class | domain='blogspot.com'; domains = sslproxies24, proxyserverlist-24, freeschoolproxy, googleproxies24 (.blogspot.com). Registered in PROVIDERS with HTTP/CONNECT:80/HTTPS/CONNECT:25. |
| `Blogspot_com_socks` | class | domain='blogspot.com^socks' (the `^socks` suffix is a synthetic key so two providers can share a real domain without colliding in Broker's unique-provider dict); domains = ['www.socks24.org']. Registered with SOCKS4/SOCKS5. |
| `Webanetlabs_net` | class | domain='webanetlabs.net'. Index → proxylist_at_* paths. |
| `Checkerproxy_net` | class | domain='checkerproxy.net'. Registered in PROVIDERS with NO proto (defaults to `()`), so every proxy it yields carries an empty protocol tuple. |
| `Proxz_com` | class | domain='proxz.com'. COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS (dead registration, ~443 proxies historically). |
| `Proxz_com.find_proxies` | method | URL-decodes the page then runs `self._find_proxies` — overriding the base means it SKIPS all IPv6 bracketed extraction and the v6 masking. Pure. |
| `Proxy_list_org` | class | domain='proxy-list.org'. ACTIVE in PROVIDERS (HTTP/CONNECT:80/HTTPS/CONNECT:25). Base64-obfuscated host:port inside `Proxy('...')` JS calls. |
| `Proxy_list_org.find_proxies` | method | `[b64decode(hp).decode().split(':') for hp in self._find_proxies(page)]` — returns list[LIST[str]], NOT list[tuple], relying on the setter's duck-typed 2-unpack. Raises binascii.Error on bad b64, UnicodeDecodeError on non-utf8 payload, and ValueError in the SETTER if a decoded payload has ≠1 colon — all swallowed by `_find_on_page`'s except (the setter one only if it happens inside... actually the setter runs outside the try, so a 3-part split raises ValueError that ESCAPES to _pipe). Skips IPv6 handling. |
| `Aliveproxy_com` | class | domain='aliveproxy.com'. Registered with NO proto (empty tuple). |
| `Maxiproxies_com` | class | domain='maxiproxies.com'. Registered with NO proto. |
| `_50kproxies_com` | class | domain='50kproxies.com'. Leading underscore is NOT privacy — Python identifiers cannot start with a digit. COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS (~822 proxies historically). |
| `Proxylist_me` | class | domain='proxylist.me'. ACTIVE in PROVIDERS. |
| `Foxtools_ru` | class | domain='foxtools.ru'. Registered with max_conn=1 (the only rate-limited entry besides My_proxy_com's max_conn=2). |
| `Gatherproxy_com` | class | domain='gatherproxy.com'. ACTIVE in PROVIDERS. Hex-encoded ports embedded in JS. Site has been defunct since ~2018. |
| `Gatherproxy_com.find_proxies` | method | Returns `[(host, str(int(port, 16))) for host, port in self._pattern_h.findall(page) if port]` — hex→decimal port conversion, dropping empty ports. Raises ValueError on non-hex port text (swallowed by _find_on_page). 6 lines of commented-out dead code above it show an abandoned gp.dep http-vs-socks branch. |
| `Gatherproxy_com_socks` | class | domain='gatherproxy.com^socks'. Registered with SOCKS4/SOCKS5. Extends Provider directly (NOT Gatherproxy_com), so it uses the default IPv4 `_pattern`, not the hex-port one. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base` | class | domain='tools.rosinstrument.com'. Shared JS-deobfuscation base: sqrt-derived XOR key + hideTxt() payload. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.find_proxies` | method | 4-stage deobfuscator: (1) sqrtPattern.findall → return [] if empty; (2) x = round(sqrt(float(x[0]))) — Python's round() is BANKER'S rounding; (3) `self.bodyPattern.findall(page)[0]` → UNGUARDED IndexError if sqrt matched but hideTxt didn't (caught by _find_on_page); (4) unquote, then XOR every ODD-indexed char's code point with x (`ord(char) ^ (x if i % 2 else 0)`), rebuild via chr(), html.unescape, then `_find_proxies`. Pure but algorithmically dense; bypasses IPv6 handling. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com` | class | domain='tools.rosinstrument.com'. Registered with HTTP/CONNECT:80/HTTPS/CONNECT:25. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_socks` | class | domain='tools.rosinstrument.com^socks'. Registered with SOCKS4/SOCKS5. |
| `Xseo_in` | class | domain='xseo.in'. ACTIVE in PROVIDERS. JS char-variable port obfuscation. Russian site — pages are historically cp1251-encoded. |
| `Xseo_in.char_js_port_to_num` | method | `re.sub` callback: takes group(0) chars, strips '+', maps each char through self.charEqNum, joins into a digit string. Raises KeyError if a char is absent from the map (swallowed by _find_on_page). Reads mutable self state. |
| `Xseo_in.find_proxies` | method | Builds self.charEqNum from `\b(?P<char>[a-z])=(?P<num>\d);` pairs (rebinds the instance attr each call), then `re.sub(r'\(""\+(?P<chars>[a-z+]+)\)', self.char_js_port_to_num, page)` to inline the real port digits, then `_find_proxies`. Mutates self.charEqNum; bypasses IPv6 handling. |
| `Nntime_com` | class | domain='nntime.com'. ACTIVE in PROVIDERS. Same JS char-map obfuscation as Xseo_in but with a `(":"+chars)` port form and its own IP pattern. |
| `Nntime_com.char_js_port_to_num` | method | Byte-identical to Xseo_in.char_js_port_to_num (copy-paste duplication): strips '+', maps chars through self.charEqNum, joins. KeyError on unknown char. |
| `Nntime_com.find_proxies` | method | Same as Xseo_in.find_proxies except the port expression is `\(":"\+(?P<chars>[a-z+]+)\)` (colon-prefixed). Mutates self.charEqNum; bypasses IPv6 handling. |
| `Proxynova_com` | class | domain='proxynova.com'. COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS (~818 proxies historically). |
| `Spys_ru` | class | domain='spys.ru'. ACTIVE in PROVIDERS. The most complex obfuscation: XOR-composed JS variables. Russian site, cp1251 encoding risk. Note `_pipe` actually targets spys.ONE, not spys.ru — `self.domain` and the fetched host disagree. |
| `Spys_ru.char_js_port_to_num` | method | `re.sub` callback emulating a JS expression: splits group(0) on '+', drops the leading '' element, and for each `(var1^var2)` term strips parens, splits on '^', XORs the two mapped ints, appends the digit. Raises KeyError on unknown var, ValueError if a term lacks exactly one '^'. Reads self.charEqNum. |
| `Spys_ru.find_proxies` | method | Extracts `[>;](?P<char>[a-z\d]{4,})=(?P<num>[a-z\d\^]+)` var assignments; for each, if the value contains '^' it resolves `int(digit) ^ self.charEqNum[tochar]` against the ACCUMULATED map (order-dependent — a forward reference raises KeyError), else int(num); writes into the shared class dict. Then `re.sub` of `(?P<js_port_code>(?:\+\([a-z0-9^+]+\))+)` through char_js_port_to_num, then `_find_proxies`. Mutates shared class state; bypasses IPv6 handling. Two dead commented-out expCharNum/charEqNum lines above. |
| `My_proxy_com` | class | domain='my-proxy.com'. Registered with max_conn=2 and NO proto (empty tuple). |
| `Free_proxy_cz` | class | domain='free-proxy.cz'. COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS (~420 proxies). Base64 host AND port. |
| `Free_proxy_cz.find_proxies` | method | `[(b64decode(h).decode(), b64decode(p).decode()) for h, p in self._find_proxies(page)]`. Raises binascii.Error / UnicodeDecodeError on malformed payloads (swallowed by _find_on_page). Bypasses IPv6 handling. |
| `Proxyb_net` | class | domain='proxyb.net'. COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS (~857 proxies). AJAX JSON-ish endpoint with b64 ports. |
| `Proxyb_net.find_proxies` | method | Returns [] on falsy page. Splits the body on the literal `","ports":"` → UNGUARDED ValueError if the marker is absent or repeated (not exactly 2 parts). Walks `</tr><tr>`-separated host chunks taking the first IPPattern hit from each, b64-decodes each stats() blob and takes `[0]` of _port_pattern.findall → IndexError if a payload has no `':NNN'`. Zips hosts/ports with strict=False (silently truncates to the shorter list — a host/port MISALIGNMENT here yields wrong proxies, not an error). Bypasses IPv6 handling. |
| `Proxylistplus_com` | class | domain='list.proxylistplus.com' (note: the full host, unlike most entries which use the bare domain). ACTIVE in PROVIDERS. |
| `Proxylist_download` | class | domain='www.proxy-list.download'. Registered with NO proto — so its socks4/socks5 endpoints yield proxies with an EMPTY protocol tuple, losing the type information the url already states. Live/working provider. |
| `ProxyProvider` | class | Deprecated backwards-compat alias for Provider. Not in __init__.__all__ but importable and part of the historical public API. |
| `ProxyProvider.__init__` | method | Emits `DeprecationWarning` via warnings.warn(stacklevel=2) then delegates to super().__init__(*args, **kwargs). Side effect: a runtime warning (which __init__.py configures as `simplefilter('once', DeprecationWarning)`). |
| `PROVIDERS` | constant | Module-level list of 38 ALREADY-INSTANTIATED provider objects, built at IMPORT time: 18 plain `Provider(url=..., proto=...)` entries plus 20 subclass instances. Imported by api.py, which does `list(PROVIDERS)` — a shallow copy, so every Broker shares the SAME mutable provider instances (accumulated `_proxies` sets and closed `_session`s leak across Broker lifetimes). ~20 further lines of commented-out 'Bad...' providers document dead sites (proxydb.net, cybersyndrome, ip-adress.com, marcosbl, go4free, blackstarsecurity, get-proxy.net) plus the 5 defined-but-disabled classes (Proxyb_net, Proxz_com, Proxynova_com, _50kproxies_com, Free_proxy_cz). BUG: the two proxyscrape socks entries pass `proto=("SOCKS4")` and `proto=("SOCKS5")` — parens WITHOUT a trailing comma, so these are STRINGS, not tuples; the proxies setter stores the bare string as the proto field and any downstream iteration over it yields individual CHARACTERS ('S','O','C','K','S','4'). |

<details><summary><b>Private / internal symbols (43)</b></summary>

| Symbol | Kind | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `Provider._pattern` | constant | Class-level default regex (IPv4 + lookahead port) used by `_find_proxies`; overridden as a class attribute by Proxy_list_org, Gatherproxy_com (via _pattern_h), Tools_rosinstrument_com_base, Nntime_com, Free_proxy_cz. This is the module's dynamic-dispatch hinge: `self._pattern` resolves through the MRO at call time. |
| `Provider._pipe` | async_method | Default crawl strategy: single `await self._find_on_page(self.url)`. This is THE override point — 22 subclasses replace it with multi-page discovery. Side effect: populates self._proxies. |
| `Provider._find_on_pages` | async_method | Fan-out helper. Returns immediately on falsy `urls`. Peeks `urls[0]` to decide the shape: if NOT a dict, dedupes via `set(urls)` (loses order, and would TypeError if handed a set); if dict, splats `self._find_on_page(**url)` with keys url/data/headers/method — dict entries are NOT deduped. Runs everything through a single unbounded `asyncio.gather` (Proxyb_net queues 151, Tools_rosinstrument 102, Nntime 30 at once — only the Semaphore in `_get` throttles actual sockets). gather propagates the first exception while leaving siblings running. |
| `Provider._find_on_page` | async_method | Fetch one page via `self.get`, then call `self.find_proxies(page)` inside a bare `except Exception` that logs and substitutes `received = []` — this is the ONLY guard keeping the ~12 fragile subclass parsers (IndexError on `findall(...)[0]`, ValueError on b64decode/int(x,16)/tuple-unpack) from killing a run. Assigns `self.proxies = received` (append semantics), logs added/received counts. Mutates self._proxies; returns None. |
| `Provider._get` | async_method | Single fetch, longest fn in the file (27 lines). Acquires `self._sem_provider` and issues `self._session.request(method, url, data=data, headers=headers, timeout=ClientTimeout(total=self._timeout))` in one combined `async with`. Reads `resp.text()` FIRST, then raises BadStatusError if status != 200 (logging headers/cookies/body at debug). Catches exactly UnicodeDecodeError, BadStatusError, asyncio.TimeoutError, aiohttp.ClientOSError, aiohttp.ClientResponseError, aiohttp.ServerDisconnectedError → returns ''. Anything else (e.g. aiohttp.ClientConnectorError, ServerTimeoutError, InvalidURL, ssl errors) PROPAGATES and kills the pipe. |
| `Provider._find_proxies` | method | `list(self._pattern.findall(page))`. Returns list of tuples when the pattern has >1 group, list of str when it has 1 group (Proxy_list_org's case) — the return SHAPE is pattern-dependent duck typing. Pure. |
| `Freeproxylists_com._pipe` | async_method | gathers 3 index pages (socks/elite/anonymous .html), concatenates them, regex-extracts (type, 10-digit-uts) pairs, formats `http://www.freeproxylists.com/load_{t}_{uts}.html`, then `_find_on_pages`. Empty regex result → empty urls → `_find_on_pages` no-ops. Site is defunct. |
| `Blogspot_com_base._cookies` | constant | Never read — shadowed by the instance attribute set in Provider.__init__. Mutable class-level dict (would be shared across instances if it ever were read). |
| `Blogspot_com_base._pipe` | async_method | gathers `http://{d}/` for every d in self.domains (attribute defined only on subclasses — AttributeError if instantiated directly), regex-extracts dated post links (`.../YYYY/MM/...`), `_find_on_pages`. |
| `Webanetlabs_net._pipe` | async_method | GETs https://webanetlabs.net/publ/24, regex-extracts hrefs containing `proxylist_at_`, prefixes host, `_find_on_pages`. Empty page → no urls → no-op. |
| `Checkerproxy_net._pipe` | async_method | GETs https://checkerproxy.net/, regex-extracts `/archive/YYYY-MM-DD` hrefs, rewrites them to `/api/archive/...` JSON endpoints, `_find_on_pages`. Parsed by the default IPv4 regex over raw JSON. |
| `Proxz_com._pipe` | async_method | GETs proxy_list_high_anonymous_0.html, regex-extracts sibling `proxy_list_high_anonymous_*` hrefs, appends the seed url, `_find_on_pages`. |
| `Proxy_list_org._pattern` | constant | SINGLE capture group → `_find_proxies` returns list[str] here, not list[tuple]. Class-attr override of Provider._pattern. |
| `Proxy_list_org._pipe` | async_method | GETs english/index.php?p=1, regex-extracts `./index.php?p=N` hrefs, prefixes, appends seed, `_find_on_pages`. |
| `Aliveproxy_com._pipe` | async_method | No discovery — hardcodes 16 list paths (socks5-list, high-anonymity/anonymous/fastest, 6 country lists, 5 port-specific lists) and `_find_on_pages` them all concurrently. 21 lines, the longest _pipe. |
| `Maxiproxies_com._pipe` | async_method | GETs /category/proxy-lists/, regex-extracts `<a href>` links containing the literal substring 'example', `_find_on_pages`. |
| `_50kproxies_com._pipe` | async_method | GETs /category/proxy-list/, regex-extracts `<a href>` links containing '-proxy-list-', `_find_on_pages`. |
| `Proxylist_me._pipe` | async_method | GETs https://proxylist.me/, regex-extracts `?page=N` ids, `max()`es them → UNGUARDED ValueError('max() arg is an empty sequence') if the page is empty or the markup changed; this escapes _pipe (not covered by _find_on_page's except). Then fetches pages 1..lastId. Inline comment records that master's `range(lastId)` was a pre-existing bug (hit ?page=0 → 404, never fetched the last page); this file fixes it to range(1, lastId+1). |
| `Foxtools_ru._pipe` | async_method | No discovery — hardcodes `http://api.foxtools.ru/v2/Proxy.txt?page={1..5}` and `_find_on_pages`. |
| `Gatherproxy_com._pattern_h` | constant | Two-group pattern with a lookahead alternation pairing each IPv4 with the next quoted hex port. Note it is `_pattern_h`, NOT `_pattern` — `_find_proxies` is bypassed; `find_proxies` calls `self._pattern_h.findall` directly. |
| `Gatherproxy_com._pipe` | async_method | BUG: builds `urls` inside a `for t in ['anonymous','elite']` loop but REASSIGNS (not extends) it each iteration — the 'anonymous' page set is silently discarded, only 'elite' is ever fetched. POSTs {Type, PageIdx:1} to /proxylist/anonymity/, `continue`s on empty page, `max()`es `href="#N"` ids (unguarded ValueError on empty match), then POSTs pages 1..lastPageId as dict-form entries. |
| `Gatherproxy_com_socks._pipe` | async_method | Single dict entry: POST http://www.gatherproxy.com/sockslist/ with no body, via `_find_on_pages`. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.sqrtPattern` | constant | Extracts the integer whose square root becomes the XOR key. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.bodyPattern` | constant | Greedy `.*` (no DOTALL on this one) capturing the obfuscated payload passed to hideTxt(). |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base._pattern` | constant | Override accepting hostnames OR IPv4 (the only provider pattern in the file that admits DNS names, not just IPs), paired by lookahead to a 2-5 digit port. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com._pipe` | async_method | Generates 102 urls: `raw_free_db.htm?{pid}&t={t}` for pid in range(51) × t in (1,2); all fanned out at once via `_find_on_pages` (Semaphore(4) is the only throttle). |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_socks._pipe` | async_method | Generates 51 urls: `raw_free_db.htm?{pid}&t=3` for pid in range(51), `_find_on_pages`. |
| `Xseo_in.charEqNum` | constant | MUTABLE CLASS-LEVEL DICT shared by every Xseo_in instance. `find_proxies` REBINDS `self.charEqNum = {...}` (creating an instance attr), so the class dict stays empty in practice — but the declaration is a latent shared-state footgun and identical in form to Spys_ru's, which DOES mutate it. |
| `Xseo_in._pipe` | async_method | Single `_find_on_page(url='http://xseo.in/proxylist', data={'submit': 1}, method='POST')`. Note `data` value is an INT — aiohttp urlencodes it as '1'. |
| `Nntime_com.charEqNum` | constant | Mutable class-level dict; rebound (not mutated) per call by find_proxies, same as Xseo_in. |
| `Nntime_com._pattern` | constant | Override of the global pattern adding a leading `\b` word boundary; otherwise equivalent to IPPortPatternGlobal. |
| `Nntime_com._pipe` | async_method | Generates 30 urls `proxy-updated-{01..30}.htm` (zero-padded via `{:02}`), `_find_on_pages`. |
| `Proxynova_com._pipe` | async_method | GETs the proxy-server-list index, regex-extracts 2-letter ISO codes from `"xx"` literals, excludes 'en', builds per-country urls, `_find_on_pages`. The regex is crude enough to catch any 2-letter quoted token in the page (JS, CSS, anything). |
| `Spys_ru.charEqNum` | constant | MUTABLE CLASS-LEVEL DICT that IS mutated in place (`self.charEqNum[char] = int(num)`) and NEVER rebound — so state accumulates across every page, every call, and every Spys_ru instance process-wide. Under `_find_on_pages`' concurrent gather, two pages' variable maps interleave into one shared dict; the XOR resolution path (`self.charEqNum[tochar]`) reads whatever a sibling page last wrote. Benign under the GIL only because find_proxies contains no await; genuinely racy under real threads. |
| `Spys_ru._pipe` | async_method | GETs http://spys.one/proxies/, extracts a 32-hex session id via `re.findall(expSession, page)[0]` → UNGUARDED IndexError that ESCAPES _pipe (no _find_on_page wrapper here) and aborts the provider whenever the page is empty/changed — the most likely live failure in the module. Then POSTs the same url twice with {xf0: session, xpp: 3 (200/page), xf1: 3\|4 (ANM\|HIA)}. 5 lines of dead commented country-crawl code follow. |
| `My_proxy_com._pipe` | async_method | GETs free-proxy-list.html, regex-extracts `free-*` hrefs, prefixes host, appends the seed url, `_find_on_pages`. |
| `Free_proxy_cz._pattern` | constant | Two groups (b64 host, b64 port) joined by a lazy DOTALL `.*?` — can pair a host with a port from an adjacent row if the markup shifts. |
| `Free_proxy_cz._pipe` | async_method | Generates 14 urls `proxylist/main/date/{1..14}`, `_find_on_pages`. Followed by ~24 lines of commented-out rate-limit-batching (15 urls then sleep 61s) and lastPageId discovery — abandoned, the site 429s without it. |
| `Proxyb_net._port_pattern_b64` | constant | Single group: base64 blob per port. |
| `Proxyb_net._port_pattern` | constant | Single group: extracts the port digits from the DECODED b64 payload. |
| `Proxyb_net._pipe` | async_method | Builds 151 dict-form POSTs to http://proxyb.net/ajax.php with data {action:'getProxy', p: 0..150, page:'/anonimnye_proksi_besplatno.html'} and header X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest; `_find_on_pages` fans ALL 151 out at once (largest fan-out in the module). |
| `Proxylistplus_com._pipe` | async_method | Generates 18 urls: `{i}-List-{n}` for i in ['Fresh-HTTP-Proxy','SSL','Socks'] × n in 1..6. NOTE the f-string interpolates `i` (the NAME) into the position the template calls `{i}-List-{n}` — variable naming is inverted vs intent but the output is correct. |
| `Proxylist_download._pipe` | async_method | Hardcodes 4 API v1 urls (type=http\|https\|socks4\|socks5), `_find_on_pages`. Plain-text ip:port bodies parsed by the default pattern. |

</details>

<details><summary><b>Per-symbol Rust mapping notes (92)</b></summary>

- `Provider` — `trait Provider: Send + Sync` with `#[async_trait]`; default `pipe()`/`find_proxies()` impls. Shared fields → a `ProviderCore` struct each impl owns (composition, not inheritance, since Rust has no attribute inheritance). Registry as `Vec<Box<dyn Provider>>`.
- `Provider._pattern` — No class-attribute override in Rust. Make it a trait method `fn pattern(&self) -> &Regex` with a default returning a `LazyLock<Regex>`; each impl overrides. NOTE: the pattern uses `(?=...)` lookahead — the `regex` crate cannot compile it; needs `fancy-regex`.
- `Provider.__init__` — Builder pattern (`ProviderBuilder::new().url(..).proto(..).max_conn(4).build()`) — 6 params, 5 optional. `tokio::sync::Semaphore` has no runtime affinity, so the get_running_loop/RuntimeError dance disappears entirely. Drop `_loop`. `proto` → `Vec<Proto>` enum, not stringly-typed.
- `Provider.proxies` — `fn proxies(&self) -> &HashSet<(String, String, Vec<Proto>)>`. The tuple is a nominal type — prefer `struct ProxyEntry { host, port, proto }` with Hash+Eq. Returning `&` reproduces the no-copy semantics safely.
- `Provider.proxies.setter` — Rust has no property setters and `x = y` meaning `x.extend(y)` is a footgun. Expose `fn add_proxies(&mut self, new: impl IntoIterator<Item = (String, String)>)`. Filter empty ports with `.filter(|(_, p)| !p.is_empty())`. The 'any 2-element iterable' duck typing collapses to the `(String, String)` tuple type.
- `Provider.get_proxies` — `async fn get_proxies(&mut self) -> Result<&HashSet<ProxyEntry>, ProviderError>`. Build a `reqwest::Client` (with default headers + `Arc<Jar>` cookie store) per call, or better hoist a shared client into the broker — reqwest clients are pooled and cheap to clone, unlike ClientSession. Must reset `self._session = None` semantics: just drop the client.
- `Provider._pipe` — Default trait method `async fn pipe(&mut self) -> Result<(), ProviderError>`. Requires `#[async_trait]` (or Rust 1.75+ AFIT — but AFIT is not dyn-safe, so `async-trait` crate is mandatory for a `Box<dyn Provider>` registry).
- `Provider._find_on_pages` — Kill the heterogeneous list with `enum PageRequest { Url(String), Full { url: String, data: Option<Vec<(String,String)>>, headers: Option<HeaderMap>, method: Method } }` — the `**kwargs` splat has no Rust analog. Dedup via `IndexSet` (indexmap crate) to keep order determinism that `set()` destroys. Use `futures::future::join_all` (collects ALL results, unlike gather's first-error propagation) or `tokio::task::JoinSet` for early abort — pick deliberately, the semantics differ.
- `Provider._find_on_page` — The bare `except Exception` is load-bearing — in Rust the equivalent failures are `Result::Err` (recoverable) NOT panics, so every parser must return `Result<Vec<(String,String)>, ParseError>` and this fn does `.unwrap_or_else(|e| { warn!(...); vec![] })`. Do NOT rely on `catch_unwind` — index-out-of-bounds panics must be refactored into `.get(0).ok_or(...)?` at each site.
- `Provider.get` — `async fn get(&self, req: &PageRequest) -> String` returning empty string on total failure (preserve the '' sentinel, or better `Option<String>`). Rust's scoping makes the UnboundLocalError impossible — init `let mut page = String::new()` before the loop. Consider `backoff` crate if adding sleep, but note the original has none.
- `Provider._get` — `tokio::sync::Semaphore` permit + `reqwest`. The narrow catch list is a bug surface: model it as `enum FetchError { BadStatus(u16), Timeout, Decode, Transport(reqwest::Error) }` via `thiserror` and decide explicitly which map to '' — do not blanket-swallow. `resp.text()` charset behavior differs (see port_risks).
- `Provider.find_proxies` — `fn find_proxies(&self, page: &str) -> Result<Vec<(String,String)>, ParseError>` as a default trait method. Equal-length space masking must be byte-safe — use `regex::Regex::replace_all` with a closure emitting `" ".repeat(m.as_str().len())`, and keep everything in `&str`/char boundaries. `canonicalize_ip` → `std::net::IpAddr::from_str(s).ok().map(|a| a.to_string())`.
- `Provider._find_proxies` — The shape-shifting return is the single nastiest port detail: Python's findall collapses 1-group patterns to `list[str]` and multi-group to `list[tuple]`. Rust must split into two explicit methods (`find_pairs -> Vec<(String,String)>` vs `find_singles -> Vec<String>`) per subclass; there is no polymorphic findall.
- `Freeproxylists_com` — Unit struct + `ProviderCore`; impl Provider with only `pipe` overridden.
- `Freeproxylists_com._pipe` — `join_all` over 3 gets, `concat()`, `fancy-regex` captures_iter. Straightforward.
- `Blogspot_com_base` — Base-class attribute shadowed by instance init is a Python-only hazard; in Rust make cookies an explicit builder field so the bug cannot recur silently. Decide whether to fix (send NCR) or preserve.
- `Blogspot_com_base._cookies` — Drop or lift into the builder. Note the value is an `int` 1, not a str — aiohttp would need to coerce; reqwest's cookie jar requires `&str`.
- `Blogspot_com_base._pipe` — `domains` becomes a required trait method `fn domains(&self) -> &[&str]` — makes the 'abstract base' explicit at compile time instead of failing at runtime.
- `Blogspot_com` — Const `&[&str]` for domains.
- `Blogspot_com_socks` — The `^socks` sentinel-in-a-string is a smell — model as `struct ProviderId { domain: String, variant: Option<Variant> }` or a newtype key with Hash/Eq.
- `Webanetlabs_net` — Trivial.
- `Webanetlabs_net._pipe` — Trivial.
- `Checkerproxy_net` — Empty proto tuple is meaningful downstream — keep `Vec<Proto>` and let it be empty rather than defaulting to all.
- `Checkerproxy_net._pipe` — Trivial; note it regexes JSON rather than parsing it — preserve, don't 'improve' to serde_json (the API shape is unverified/dead).
- `Proxz_com` — Port but gate behind a feature flag / disabled-by-default list.
- `Proxz_com.find_proxies` — `percent_encoding::percent_decode_str(page).decode_utf8_lossy()` (Python's unquote defaults to utf-8/errors='replace' — lossy matches).
- `Proxz_com._pipe` — Trivial.
- `Proxy_list_org` — Needs the single-group findall shape (Vec<String>, not Vec<(String,String)>).
- `Proxy_list_org._pattern` — No lookahead — plain `regex` crate is fine. But this is the concrete case proving `_find_proxies` needs two typed variants in Rust.
- `Proxy_list_org.find_proxies` — `base64` crate with a lenient engine (Python's b64decode default validate=False DISCARDS non-alphabet chars). `split_once(':').ok_or(ParseError::Shape)?` — makes the escaping-ValueError path explicit instead of latent.
- `Proxy_list_org._pipe` — Trivial.
- `Aliveproxy_com` — Trivial.
- `Aliveproxy_com._pipe` — `const PATHS: &[&str]` + `join_all`. Zero logic — pure data. Good candidate to fold into a declarative config table rather than code.
- `Maxiproxies_com` — Trivial.
- `Maxiproxies_com._pipe` — Trivial.
- `_50kproxies_com` — Rust has the same identifier rule — name it `FiftyKProxiesCom` or `P50kProxiesCom`; do not carry the underscore.
- `_50kproxies_com._pipe` — Trivial.
- `Proxylist_me` — Trivial.
- `Proxylist_me._pipe` — `.max().ok_or(ProviderError::NoPagination)?` — the empty-max panic must become a Result. Keep the 1-indexed fix.
- `Foxtools_ru` — max_conn=1 → `Semaphore::new(1)`; preserve, the site 429s otherwise.
- `Foxtools_ru._pipe` — Trivial; `(1..6).map(...)`.
- `Gatherproxy_com` — Port for parity, but flag as dead-on-arrival.
- `Gatherproxy_com._pattern_h` — `(?=...)` lookahead → `fancy-regex` mandatory. The `[\d\w]+` port class admits non-hex chars, so from_str_radix(16) must return Option, not unwrap.
- `Gatherproxy_com.find_proxies` — `u32::from_str_radix(port, 16).ok().map(|p| (host, p.to_string()))` + `filter_map`. Delete the commented branch — it is not a spec.
- `Gatherproxy_com._pipe` — The reassign-vs-extend bug vanishes with `let mut urls = Vec::new(); ... urls.extend(...)` — decide explicitly whether to preserve the bug or fix it (fixing doubles the request count). `.max().ok_or(..)?` for the empty case.
- `Gatherproxy_com_socks` — Confirms composition-over-inheritance: it shares the domain-ish name but none of the parsing.
- `Gatherproxy_com_socks._pipe` — Trivial; `PageRequest::Full { method: Method::POST, .. }`.
- `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base` — The XOR/charcode logic is the highest-risk parser in the file — see port_risks.
- `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.sqrtPattern` — Plain `regex` — no lookaround.
- `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.bodyPattern` — Greedy `.*` without DOTALL = single-line match in both Python and `regex` — preserve the flag difference exactly (this pattern is NOT DOTALL while _pattern IS).
- `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base._pattern` — `(?=...)` → `fancy-regex`. Note downstream code assumes hosts are IPs elsewhere — a DNS name flowing out of here is a real type-model question for `ProxyEntry.host` (`String`, not `IpAddr`).
- `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.find_proxies` — Four separate divergence risks: (a) `round()` half-to-even → use `f64::round_ties_even()` NOT `round()`; (b) `chr(n)` accepts surrogates D800-DFFF, `char::from_u32` returns None → operate on `Vec<u32>` or map to REPLACEMENT_CHARACTER deliberately; (c) `html.unescape` = full HTML5 2231-entity lenient table → `html-escape` crate's `decode_html_entities`, not `htmlescape` (incomplete); (d) index-0 access → `.first().ok_or(ParseError::NoBody)?`.
- `Tools_rosinstrument_com` — Shares the parser, differs only in url generation → same trait impl, different `pipe`.
- `Tools_rosinstrument_com._pipe` — Cartesian product → `(0..51).flat_map(|pid| (1..3).map(move |t| ...))`. 102 concurrent futures is fine for tokio; the semaphore gates sockets.
- `Tools_rosinstrument_com_socks` — Same as above; only `t=3`.
- `Tools_rosinstrument_com_socks._pipe` — Trivial.
- `Xseo_in` — Encoding hazard: `resp.text()` charset sniffing must be replicated (see port_risks).
