| 2 | `api.py:185 Broker.grab` | Sets `self._countries = countries` (api.py:194) and `self._limit = limit` (api.py:195); `task = asyncio.create_task(self._grab(check=False))` (api.py:196); `self._all_tasks.append(task)` (api.py:197). Returns immediately — detached/fire-and-forget. Requires a running loop (create_task). |
| 3 | `api.py:404 Broker._grab(types=None, check=False)` | Entry to the grab loop. No try/except anywhere in the body — this is the root cause of port_risk #1. |
| 4 | `api.py:405 _grab.<locals>._get_tasks(by=MAX_CONCURRENT_PROVIDERS)` | Generator. api.py:406-410 filters providers: `if not types or not pr.proto or bool(pr.proto & types.keys())` — with types=None every provider passes. MAX_CONCURRENT_PROVIDERS = 3 (api.py:26). |
| 5 | `api.py:411-415 _get_tasks chunk loop` | `while providers:` → `tasks = [asyncio.create_task(pr.get_proxies()) for pr in providers[:by]]` (api.py:412), `del providers[:by]` (api.py:413), `self._all_tasks.extend(tasks)` (api.py:414), `yield tasks` (api.py:415). Lazy: chunk N+1 is only created when the generator is resumed, i.e. after chunk N is fully drained. |
| 6 | `api.py:418-423 _grab main loop` | `while True:` → `for tasks in _get_tasks():` → `for task in asyncio.as_completed(tasks):` → `proxies = await task` (api.py:421) → `for proxy in proxies: await self._handle(proxy, check=check)` (api.py:422-423). All 3 chunk tasks are already running concurrently; as_completed only orders consumption. |
| 7 | `providers.py:81 Provider.get_proxies` | `async with aiohttp.ClientSession(headers=get_headers(), cookies=self._cookies) as self._session:` (providers.py:88-90) — assigns the session onto the *shared instance*; `await self._pipe()` (providers.py:91); `return self.proxies` (providers.py:96) — returns the live accumulated set, not a copy. |
| 8 | `providers.py:98 Provider._pipe` | Base: `await self._find_on_page(self.url)` (providers.py:99). Subclasses override with fan-out: Freeproxylists_com (providers.py:189), Blogspot_com_base (providers.py:207), Gatherproxy_com (providers.py:372), Spys_ru (providers.py:547), Proxylist_download (providers.py:675). |
| 9 | `providers.py:101 Provider._find_on_pages` | Builds a coroutine per URL then `await asyncio.gather(*tasks)` (providers.py:112). Unbounded coroutine count (hundreds for Gatherproxy page ranges); actual HTTP concurrency is bounded only by `_sem_provider`. These tasks are NOT in `_all_tasks` — they're reaped transitively when the parent get_proxies task is cancelled. |
| 10 | `providers.py:114 Provider._find_on_page` | `page = await self.get(url, data=data, headers=headers, method=method)` (providers.py:115), snapshot `oldcount`, call find_proxies inside try/except (providers.py:117-123), assign `self.proxies = received` (providers.py:124), log delta. |
| 11 | `providers.py:128 Provider.get` | `for _ in range(self._max_tries): page = await self._get(...); if page: break` then `return page`. No backoff between tries; `page` is unbound if `_max_tries == 0` (UnboundLocalError). |
| 12 | `providers.py:135 Provider._get` | `async with (self._sem_provider, self._session.request(method, url, data=data, headers=headers, timeout=timeout) as resp)` (providers.py:139-144); `page = await resp.text()` (providers.py:145); non-200 → `raise BadStatusError` (providers.py:150); except block (providers.py:151-160) swallows UnicodeDecodeError/BadStatusError/asyncio.TimeoutError/ClientOSError/ClientResponseError/ServerDisconnectedError → `page = ""`. NOTE: aiohttp.ClientPayloadError, InvalidURL, TooManyRedirects are NOT caught and escape. |
| 13 | `providers.py:163 Provider.find_proxies` | Masks bracketed IPv6 spans (providers.py:174) so v4-mapped v6 doesn't emit a phantom v4 entry, runs `self._find_proxies(masked)` (providers.py:175 → providers.py:182 `self._pattern.findall`), then appends canonicalized `[v6]:port` pairs (providers.py:176-179). |
| 14 | `providers.py:76 Provider.proxies setter` | `new = [(host, port, self.proto) for host, port in new if port]` (providers.py:78) — drops falsy ports, attaches provider proto tuple; `self._proxies.update(new)` (providers.py:79). Never cleared for the process lifetime. |
| 15 | `api.py:434 Broker._handle` | `proxy = await Proxy.create(*proxy, timeout=self._timeout, resolver=self._resolver, verify_ssl=self._verify_ssl, loop=self._loop)` (api.py:436-442). The tuple unpacks to (host, port, proto). |
| 16 | `proxy.py:71 Proxy.create` | `_host = await resolver.resolve(host)` (proxy.py:89) then `self = cls(_host, *args, **kwargs)` (proxy.py:90). except (ResolveError, ValueError) → log + re-raise (proxy.py:91-98). |
| 17 | `resolver.py:409 Resolver.resolve` | `if self.host_is_ip(host): return canonicalize_ip(host) or host` (resolver.py:431-435) — SYNCHRONOUS fast path, the coroutine never suspends. Only domain hosts reach the `_cached_hosts` lookup (resolver.py:437) / `_fetch_records` → `_race_a_aaaa` Happy-Eyeballs A+AAAA race (resolver.py:512). |
| 18 | `proxy.py:101 Proxy.__init__` | `Resolver.host_is_ip` guard → ValueError (proxy.py:103-107); `port is None` → ValueError (proxy.py:109-110); `int(port) > 65535` → ValueError (proxy.py:112-114); `self.expected_types = set(types) & {...}` (proxy.py:116); `self._geo = Resolver.get_ip_info(self.host)` (proxy.py:133) → resolver.py:94 blocking maxminddb lookup against the module-level `_mmdb_reader` (resolver.py:23). |
| 19 | `api.py:443-444` | `except (ResolveError, ValueError): return` — malformed/unresolvable proxies are silently dropped, no counter, no log at this level. |
| 20 | `api.py:446 / api.py:454 _is_unique / api.py:461 _geo_passed` | `if not self._is_unique(proxy) or not self._geo_passed(proxy): return`. `_is_unique` inserts into `self.unique_proxies[(host, port)]` and returns True on first sight (api.py:455-459). `_geo_passed` rejects when `self._countries and proxy.geo.code not in self._countries` (api.py:462-465). |
| 21 | `api.py:449-452` | `if check: await self._push_to_check(proxy) else: self._push_to_result(proxy)`. The grab path is always the else branch — `_push_to_check`, `_on_check`, and the whole Checker/judge machinery are never touched. |
| 22 | `api.py:490 Broker._push_to_result` | `self._proxies.put_nowait(proxy)` (api.py:492) — unbounded queue, never blocks, zero backpressure onto the grab loop. Then `self._update_limit()` (api.py:493). |
| 23 | `api.py:495 Broker._update_limit` | `self._limit -= 1` (api.py:496); `if self._limit == 0 and not self._server: self._done()` (api.py:497-498). Exact `== 0` test; limit=0 (unlimited) works only because the counter goes negative and never re-tests equal. |
| 24 | `api.py:424-430 end-of-cycle` | `log.debug('Grab cycle is complete')`; `if self._server:` → `await asyncio.sleep(GRAB_PAUSE)` (api.py:427, GRAB_PAUSE=180 at api.py:23) and re-loop (the generator is rebuilt, so ALL providers are re-scraped); `else: break` (api.py:430). In plain grab there is no server → single cycle. |
| 25 | `api.py:431` | `await self._on_check.join()` — NO-OP in the grab path. `_on_check` (api.py:102, `asyncio.Queue(maxsize=max_conn)`) is only ever `put` by `_push_to_check` (api.py:485), which check=False never reaches, so `_unfinished_tasks == 0` and join() returns instantly. |
| 26 | `api.py:432 → api.py:516 Broker._done` | `while self._all_tasks: task = self._all_tasks.pop(); if not task.done(): task.cancel()` (api.py:518-521) — cancels the currently-running `_grab` task itself (Python sets `_must_cancel`, lets the current sync stretch finish, marks the task CANCELLED at return). Then `self._push_to_result(None)` (api.py:522) — THE SENTINEL — then `log.info(f'Done! Total found proxies: {len(self.unique_proxies)}')`. Not idempotent, no done-flag. |
| 27 | `cli.py:392-395 handle` | `while True: proxy = await proxies.get(); if proxy is None: break`. Consumer never calls `task_done()`. Breaking on the sentinel completes `handle`, which completes the cli.py:529 gather, which ends the program. This is the ONLY termination path. |
| 28 | `api.py:500 Broker.stop (SIGINT path)` | Registered at api.py:119-126 via `self._loop.add_signal_handler(signal.SIGINT, self.stop)` (NotImplementedError-guarded for Windows). `stop()` → `self._done()` (api.py:502) → cancels tasks + pushes its own `None`; then removes the signal handler (api.py:507-513). |

**Port risks**

- **HANG (worst risk): `_grab` has no try/except, so a provider exception kills the grab before the sentinel is pushed.** api.py:418-432 wraps nothing. `find_proxies` errors ARE caught (providers.py:119-123) but `_pipe` errors are NOT, and bundled providers can raise: `Spys_ru._pipe` does `re.findall(expSession, page)[0]` (providers.py:551) → IndexError when `get()` returned "" after all 3 tries; `Gatherproxy_com._pipe` does `max([int(n) for n in re.findall(expNumPages, page)])` (providers.py:382) → ValueError on a non-empty page lacking the pattern. `_get` also lets `aiohttp.ClientPayloadError`/`InvalidURL`/`TooManyRedirects` escape (not in the providers.py:151-158 catch list). Any of these propagates through `await task` (api.py:421) → `_grab` dies → `_done()` (api.py:432) never runs → no `None` → `cli.handle()` (cli.py:393) blocks forever, and the chunk's 2 sibling tasks leak with open sessions. A tokio port MUST catch per-provider (`JoinSet` + match on `Err`/`JoinError::is_panic`) and emit the terminator on **every** exit path (scopeguard / `Drop` / explicit `finally`-shaped block), or it reproduces the hang. Rust makes this worse: a panic in a spawned task surfaces as `JoinError` at the join point, exactly where Python's exception surfaces.
- **DROPPED TERMINATOR: `_done()` pushes the sentinel with `put_nowait` (api.py:522 → api.py:492).** Today `self._proxies` is unbounded so it can't fail — but it is **caller-supplied** (`Broker(queue=...)`, api.py:82; cli.py:473-475). Pass a bounded queue and `put_nowait` raises `QueueFull` *inside* `_done()`, losing the sentinel AND aborting the rest of `_done()`. A tokio port that reaches for `mpsc::channel(N)` + `try_send` hits this on every full buffer; `send().await` instead would deadlock `_done()` (it's called from sync context at api.py:498/502). Use `mpsc::unbounded_channel`, or reserve a permit for the terminator up front (`Sender::reserve_owned` at startup), or signal termination out-of-band (channel close / `CancellationToken`) rather than in-band.
- **`_limit` is a signed counter that intentionally underflows; `usize` breaks it.** api.py:496 `self._limit -= 1` runs on *every* push **including the `None` sentinel** (api.py:522 → 493), and api.py:497 tests exactly `== 0`. `limit=0` (unlimited, the default at api.py:186) works *only* because the counter runs to -1, -2, … and never re-tests equal. In Rust: `usize` underflow panics in debug / wraps in release; `checked_sub` returns `None`; and rewriting the guard as `<= 0` changes behavior (an unlimited grab would terminate on the first proxy). Port as `i64`/`isize` with the exact `== 0` test, or restructure to `Option<NonZeroUsize>` with an explicit decrement-and-check — and remember the sentinel itself consumes one decrement.
- **`_done()` cancels the task that is currently executing it — tokio has no equivalent and the ordering is load-bearing.** api.py:518-521 pops `_grab`'s own handle out of `_all_tasks` and calls `task.cancel()` on itself. CPython sets `_must_cancel`, lets the current *synchronous* stretch run to completion — so `_push_to_result(None)` at api.py:522 **still executes** — and only converts the task to CANCELLED at the next suspension/return. In tokio, `JoinHandle::abort()` on your own task is a no-op mid-poll and takes effect at the next `.await`; a port that aborts-then-sends may look fine, but a port that models `_done` as 'abort everything, then notify' in a *different* task will race and can drop the terminator. Rule for the port: **send the terminator first, abort second**, and never rely on abort() to stop the current task's synchronous tail.
- **`_done()` is not idempotent and can push multiple sentinels — a oneshot/close-based port will panic.** Three call sites reach it (api.py:432 normal end, api.py:498 limit hit, api.py:502 `stop()`/SIGINT), with no done-flag. Worse, it's self-re-entrant: `_done()` → `_push_to_result(None)` → `_update_limit()` (api.py:493) → if `_limit` was exactly 1 this hits `== 0` → `_done()` again → a second `None`. (Bounded at one extra level since the next decrement reaches -1.) The CLI consumer breaks on the first `None` so extra sentinels are invisible today. In Rust: a `oneshot::Sender` panics/errors on second send; `mpsc` send-after-close returns Err; `CancellationToken::cancel()` is idempotent (safest). Add an explicit `AtomicBool`/`OnceCell` guard rather than inheriting the accidental bound.
- **Loop starvation: `_handle` never yields for IP-literal hosts, and a naive tokio port makes it worse.** api.py:423 awaits `_handle` per-proxy serially; `Proxy.create` → `resolver.resolve` returns synchronously for IP literals (resolver.py:431-435 — the common case, feeds are IP:port), and `Proxy.__init__` (proxy.py:101) is fully sync including a blocking maxminddb lookup at proxy.py:133 → resolver.py:105. So a 5000-proxy response is one uninterrupted synchronous block; the other 2 in-flight provider tasks are frozen for its duration. In tokio this becomes a **blocked worker thread** (tokio is cooperative — no preemption), stalling unrelated tasks on the same worker. Needs `spawn_blocking`/`block_in_place` for the mmdb batch, or an explicit `tokio::task::yield_now()` cadence. But note: adding yield points changes proxy emission ordering and interleaving vs. Python.
- **Provider chunking is chunk-then-drain, NOT a rolling window of 3 — `JoinSet`/`buffer_unordered` silently changes throughput.** api.py:411-415: chunk N+1's tasks aren't created until every task in chunk N completed *and* was drained by `as_completed` (api.py:420). One slow provider stalls everything behind it — and 'slow' is real: `max_tries=3` × `timeout=20s` per URL (providers.py:42) with no backoff (providers.py:129-133), and `_find_on_pages` can fan out hundreds of URLs. A tokio port using `JoinSet` with 3 permits, or `stream::iter(providers).buffer_unordered(3)`, refills as each finishes — strictly better, but NOT the same program (different throughput, different emission order, different provider hit pattern). If parity matters, port it literally as `chunks(3)` + `join_all` per chunk.
- **`PROVIDERS` is a module-level list of *instances* shared by reference — the idiomatic Rust port silently changes semantics either way.** providers.py:695 instantiates every Provider at import; api.py:116-118 stores them by reference into each Broker. Consequences the port must consciously keep or drop: (a) `Provider._proxies` (providers.py:52) is **never cleared**, so in server mode cycle 2 (api.py:427) `get_proxies()` returns cycle-1 ∪ cycle-2 and every stale proxy is re-`Proxy.create`d, re-geo-looked-up, and re-rejected by `_is_unique` → unbounded memory + O(n²) rework; (b) two Brokers in one process share provider state; (c) `self._session` is rebound per call (providers.py:90) so concurrent `get_proxies()` on one instance clobber each other. A Rust port that constructs `Provider` per run (natural, owned) **fixes the leak and changes behavior**; one that uses `static` + `Arc<Mutex<Provider>>` reproduces it. Pick deliberately and write it down.
- **`get_proxies()` returns the live `set`, not a snapshot — iteration can blow up under concurrency.** providers.py:96 returns `self.proxies` → providers.py:74 returns `self._proxies` itself; `_grab` then iterates it at api.py:422 while the setter (providers.py:79) can still `update()` it from any overlapping `get_proxies()` on the same shared instance (two Brokers, or a server-mode cycle overlap) → `RuntimeError: Set changed size during iteration`. In Rust this is exactly the `&HashSet` guard vs owned `Vec` decision — return an owned snapshot (`Vec<ProxyTuple>`) from the provider task and let the channel carry ownership; do not hand out `MutexGuard<HashSet>`.
- **`Spys_ru.charEqNum` is a class-level dict mutated concurrently — Python is safe only by accident, Rust won't be.** providers.py:517 declares it on the class; `find_proxies` reads and writes it (providers.py:539-543) while `_find_on_pages` (providers.py:112 `asyncio.gather`) decodes multiple pages 'concurrently'. It's race-free today *purely* because `find_proxies` is synchronous with no `await` — GIL + no yield point makes the whole read-modify-write atomic. Port that to an async fn, or let two pages land on different tokio worker threads, and you have a genuine data race (won't compile without a `Mutex`, and with one you get lock contention plus the same cross-page table pollution the Python has). Prefer per-page-local decode tables — but note that *changes results*, since the current code lets one page's table decode the next page's ports.
- **`Resolver._cached_hosts` is a process-global, unlocked, TTL-less class attribute with a read-modify-write across an await.** resolver.py:37; read at resolver.py:437-439, written at resolver.py:460/462 after `await self._fetch_records(...)` (resolver.py:441). Two tasks resolving the same host both miss, both query, both write — benign last-writer-wins in Python. In Rust this needs `Arc<Mutex<HashMap>>`/`DashMap` (changing the duplicate-query profile) or a single-flight map (changing it further). Also unbounded — no eviction, ever. Note this only fires for *domain* hosts; the IP-literal fast path (resolver.py:431) never touches the cache, so it's near-dead in practice for grab.
- **`asyncio.as_completed` does NOT cancel siblings on early exit — `JoinSet` does.** api.py:420: if `await task` (api.py:421) raises or the loop is cancelled, the chunk's other 2 tasks keep running with open aiohttp sessions. They survive in `_all_tasks` and are only reaped by a later `_done()` — which on the exception path never happens (see risk #1). Rust's `JoinSet` aborts all children on drop, and `async` cancellation propagates structurally — safer, but *different*: sessions close earlier, in-flight requests die, and any provider-side effects stop. `FuturesUnordered` matches Python's leak semantics more closely. Decide which you want; don't inherit it by accident.
- **Retry loop: no backoff, and `max_tries=0` is representable.** providers.py:129-133 `for _ in range(self._max_tries): page = await self._get(...)` then `return page` — with `max_tries=0` `page` is unbound → `UnboundLocalError` escaping to risk #1's hang. And zero backoff means a failing host is hammered 3× back-to-back; that no-delay profile is the current de-facto rate limit against providers, so 'improving' it with exponential backoff changes wall-clock and provider-side behavior. Port `max_tries` as `NonZeroU32` so 0 is unrepresentable, and decide explicitly about backoff.
- **SIGINT handler is a *synchronous* callback in Python; tokio's is an async task.** api.py:119-126 registers `loop.add_signal_handler(signal.SIGINT, self.stop)` — so `stop()` → `_done()` (api.py:502) runs to completion *between* await points, atomically w.r.t. the grab task: the abort-all and the sentinel push cannot interleave with a `_push_to_result(proxy)`. In tokio, `signal::ctrl_c()` is awaited in its own task and races the grab task — a proxy can be pushed *after* the sentinel, so the consumer sees a value past its terminator (or the value is lost). Use a `CancellationToken` checked by the producer, and make the terminator the last thing the producer itself sends, rather than sending it from the signal task.
- **Construction-outside-runtime tolerance differs.** `Broker.__init__` builds `asyncio.Queue` (api.py:82, 102) and `Provider.__init__` builds `asyncio.Semaphore` (providers.py:54) at **import** time for `PROVIDERS` — legal only on Python ≥3.10 where these are loop-agnostic; `_resolve_loop` (api.py:128-135), `Resolver.__init__` (resolver.py:55-63) and `Provider.__init__` (providers.py:55-59) all catch `RuntimeError` to survive it, and aiodns construction is deferred to first use (resolver.py:547-549). Only `grab()` (api.py:196, `create_task`) actually requires a running loop. In tokio, `Semaphore::new`/`mpsc::channel` are runtime-free (fine), but anything calling `Handle::current()` **panics** outside a runtime — keep `static`/`lazy_static` provider construction free of any runtime handle, and take the `Handle`/spawn only inside `grab()`.

---

### Flow: `Broker.find()` is a fire-and-forget SPAWNER, not a driver: it resolves the host's real external IPs, builds a `Checker`, spawns exactly two tasks (`check_judges` + `_grab`), and returns. All results reach the caller out-of-band on `Broker._proxies`, an asyncio.Queue the user supplied (or an unbounded default), terminated by a single `None` poison pill. The caller's loop is `while True: p = await q.get(); if p is None: break`.

The pipeline is a strict SINGLE-PRODUCER chain. `_grab` (api.py:404) loops provider batches of 3 (`MAX_CONCURRENT_PROVIDERS`), and for each batch does `for task in asyncio.as_completed(tasks): for proxy in await task: await self._handle(proxy)`. Because `_handle` is *awaited inline*, exactly one coroutine ever touches the grab→check boundary — provider fetches overlap (they are tasks), but proxy handling is serialized. This single-producer property is load-bearing for correctness (see port_risks #2).

The grab→check queue (`Broker._on_check`, api.py:102) is NOT a channel — it is a **counting semaphore built out of an asyncio.Queue**, and nothing ever consumes it in a loop. `_push_to_check` does `await self._on_check.put(None)` (api.py:485) to ACQUIRE a permit (blocks when maxsize=max_conn is reached — this is the only backpressure on discovery), then spawns `Checker.check(proxy)` as a task. The permit is RELEASED from the task's *done callback* (`_task_done`, api.py:469) via `get_nowait()`. The same callback separately calls `task_done()`, which drives the queue's independent `_unfinished_tasks` counter that `_grab`'s terminal `await self._on_check.join()` (api.py:431) waits on. I verified empirically that these are two orthogonal counters: `join()` returns while `qsize()` is still non-zero. A Rust port needs BOTH a `Semaphore(max_conn)` and a separate outstanding-checks counter + `Notify`; conflating them hangs or drops work.

`Checker.check` (checker.py:166) first blocks on `Judge.ev[proto].wait()` — process-global `asyncio.Event`s set by the FIRST judge that verifies, concurrently with the grab loop already running. It then walks protocols SEQUENTIALLY, each iteration mutating a single `proxy.ngtr` slot and the proxy's reader/writer. On success the done callback calls `_push_to_result` → `put_nowait(proxy)` + `_update_limit()`; when `_limit` hits exactly 0, `_done()` cancels every task (including the `_grab` task that is calling it — self-cancellation) and pushes the `None` sentinel.

`serve()` (api.py:275) is a SYNC method that `run_until_complete`s a starter which boots `asyncio.start_server` then spawns `find(limit=limit)`. It adds a second backpressure loop: once `_limit <= 0` and the output queue is non-empty, `_push_to_check` parks on `await self._proxies.join()` (api.py:482) — released only when `ProxyPool._import` (server.py:88) pulls proxies and calls `self._proxies.task_done()` (server.py:96). That is the "lazy pause": discovery resumes only when real client traffic drains the pool. Shutdown is `NoProxyError` → `Server.stop()` → `self._loop.stop()` (server.py:248), i.e. killing the event loop from inside a done callback.

> **Concurrency model.** SINGLE event loop, single thread, no executor anywhere (blocking mmdb reads happen inline on the loop). QUEUES (2, both asyncio.Queue, with completely different roles): - `Broker._proxies` (api.py:82) — UNBOUNDED output queue, caller-supplied. Producer: `_push_to_result` via `put_nowait` (api.py:492) from BOTH a coroutine (grab mode, api.py:452) and a done callback (check mode, api.py:475). Consumer: user code, or `ProxyPool._import` (server.py:93). Terminated by a `None` poison pill (api.py:522). In serve mode only, its `join()`/`task_done()` pair is used as a *pause barrier* (api.py:482 <-> server.py:96). - `Broker._on_check` (api.py:102, maxsize=max_conn) — NOT a channel. A counting semaphore + join-counter hybrid. `put(None)` acquires (api.py:485), `get_nowait()` in the done callback releases (api.py:472), `task_done()` in the same callback drives the orthogonal `_unfinished_tasks` counter that `join()` (api.py:431) waits on. Nothing ever consumes it in a loop. Verified empirically: `join()` returns while `qsize()` is still non-zero — the two counters are independent. SEMAPHORES (1 real): `Provider._sem_provider = asyncio.Semaphore(max_conn=4)` (providers.py:54), per-provider, constructed at MODULE IMPORT time for the PROVIDERS singletons, used at providers.py:140. EVENTS: `Judge.ev = {'HTTP'|'HTTPS'|'SMTP': asyncio.Event()}` (judge.py:16-20) — CLASS-LEVEL, constructed at import, outside any loop. Level-triggered: `.set()` on first verified judge (judge.py:142) or on the no-judges-found path (checker.py:111/117/121); `.wait()` at checker.py:173-177 returns instantly if already set. `Judge.clear()` (judge.py:64) resets them per Checker construction. TASKS: `_grab`/`_load` + `check_judges` (api.py:267-272); one per provider, batched 3 at a time (api.py:412); one per proxy check, capped at max_conn by the _on_check permit (api.py:486); two per client relay direction (server.py:399-409); one per client connection (server.py:301); DNS/ext-IP probe tasks (resolver.py:309, 522-523). GATHER: judges (checker.py:94, unbounded), DNSBL (checker.py:161, return_exceptions=True), provider sub-pages (providers.py:112/196/209), relay streams (server.py:411), ext-IP probe cleanup (resolver.py:335). AS_COMPLETED: api.py:420 — with `await self._handle(...)` inline, which is what makes the pipeline single-producer. CONCURRENCY CAPS: 3 providers (api.py:26) x 4 conns each (providers.py:42); max_conn=200 concurrent checks; max_tries retries at 3 layers (provider fetch providers.py:129, proxy check checker.py:231, server request server.py:362). BACKPRESSURE: exactly two points. (1) `_on_check.put` blocks the sole producer when max_conn checks are in flight. (2) serve-mode `_proxies.join()` (api.py:482) parks the producer until real client traffic drains the pool. TERMINATION: three paths, all converging on `_done()` (api.py:516) -> cancel `_all_tasks` -> `put_nowait(None)`. (a) limit reached: `_update_limit` (api.py:497). (b) providers exhausted: `_grab` -> `_on_check.join()` -> `_done()` (api.py:431-432). (c) SIGINT -> `stop()` (api.py:121/500). Server shutdown is separate: None sentinel -> NoProxyError (server.py:99) -> `Server.stop()` -> `self._loop.stop()` (server.py:248). EVENT-LOOP ASSUMPTIONS: `Broker.serve()` (api.py:370) and `Server.stop()` (server.py:243-248) call `run_until_complete`/`loop.stop()` — they assume ownership of a non-running loop and terminate the process's loop as the shutdown mechanism. Every `__init__` has a `try: loop or get_running_loop() except RuntimeError: self._loop = loop` deferral pattern (api.py:128-135, checker.py:75-79, judge.py:32-36, providers.py:55-59, resolver.py:55-59, server.py:204-208).

> **Shared state.** OWNED BY Broker (single-task mutation, no locking needed today because one coroutine touches them): - `Broker.unique_proxies: dict[(host,port) -> Proxy]` (api.py:87, written 456, read 455, read by show_stats 543). UNBOUNDED; holds a permanent strong reference to every Proxy ever seen — in serve mode this grows forever. It is the dedupe authority. - `Broker._all_tasks: list[Task]` (api.py:88) — the cancellation registry. Appended from `_grab` (414), `find` (273), `grab` (197), `_push_to_check` (488); drained destructively in `_done` (518-521). Contains the _grab task itself -> self-cancellation. - `Broker._limit: int` (api.py:92) — decremented in `_update_limit` (496) from BOTH a coroutine and a done callback; read in the serve pause condition (480). Doubles as the termination trigger. - `Broker._countries`, `_checker`, `_server` — set once by find/serve, read by many tasks. PROCESS-GLOBAL (the real Arc/Mutex/static problem): - `Judge.available: dict[str, list[Judge]]` and `Judge.ev: dict[str, asyncio.Event]` (judge.py:15-20) — CLASS attributes. Mutated by `Judge.check` (append 141, set 142) across all judge tasks, wiped by `Judge.clear()` from `Checker.__init__` (checker.py:47), read by `Judge.get_random` (judge.py:62) from every check task. Two Brokers in one process clobber each other. - `Resolver._cached_hosts: dict` (resolver.py:37) — CLASS-level, shared by every Resolver instance (Broker's, Checker's, each Judge's, Server's, every `Proxy.create`). Read 437/468, written 460-462. Unbounded, no single-flight. - `PROVIDERS: list[Provider]` (providers.py:695) — module-level SINGLETONS built at import. Each carries mutable `_proxies: set` (providers.py:52) that ACCUMULATES across grab cycles and across Broker instances (never cleared; updated at providers.py:79), plus `_sem_provider` (54) and `_session` (50, reassigned per get_proxies at 88-90), plus `_cookies` (51). - `_mmdb_reader` (resolver.py:23) — module-global maxminddb reader, called synchronously from `Proxy.__init__` (proxy.py:133). - `server.history: TTLCache(maxsize=10000, ttl=600)` (server.py:26) — module-global, written server.py:390, read 337. THE PROXY OBJECT (shared mutable, crosses every boundary — the key ownership decision): Created in `_handle` (api.py:436), immediately co-owned by `Broker.unique_proxies` (api.py:456) AND handed to the Checker task AND pushed onto the output queue (api.py:492) AND into `ProxyPool._pool`/`_newcomers` (server.py:123/127). Mutated by: Checker (`proxy.ngtr` slot proxy.py:284, `proxy.types[...]` checker.py:260, `is_working` checker.py:193, and `_types_passed` DELETES type entries at checker.py:149); Proxy itself (`stat['requests']` proxy.py:400, `stat['errors']` proxy.py:333, `_runtimes` proxy.py:335, `_log` proxy.py:331, `_reader`/`_writer` dicts proxy.py:138-139/424-425, `_closed` proxy.py:398/406, `_schemes` cache proxy.py:186); Server (`proxy.log` server.py:444, connect/close 371/445). So Broker, the Checker task, the consumer, and the ProxyPool all alias the SAME mutable object concurrently. In Rust: split immutable identity (host/port/geo -> `Arc<ProxyId>`) from the mutable stats/log (`Mutex<Stats>`) and from the exclusively-owned connection state (`&mut` during a check, moved into the pool afterwards). CHECKER (shared, mutated after readers start): `Checker._ngtrs: set` (checker.py:88) and `_req_http_proto`/`_req_https_proto`/`_req_smtp_proto` (82-86) are mutated by `check_judges` (109/115/120/125) WHILE concurrently-running `check` tasks read them (172-182). `Checker._judges` rebound at checker.py:98. SERVER: `Server._connections: dict[Task -> (reader, writer)]` (server.py:215) — inserted 303, popped in the done callback 287, ITERATED in stop() 239. `ProxyPool._pool: list` (heapq) and `_newcomers: list` (server.py:45-46) — mutated by every client task via get/put/remove.

| # | Where | What |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `api.py:67 Broker.__init__` | Builds the two queues that define the whole pipeline: `self._proxies = queue or asyncio.Queue()` (api.py:82, UNBOUNDED output queue, caller-supplied) and `self._on_check = asyncio.Queue(maxsize=max_conn)` (api.py:102, the grab->check backpressure semaphore, default 200). Also creates Resolver (api.py:83), normalizes providers to Provider objects (api.py:116), and registers SIGINT->self.stop (api.py:121, NotImplementedError-guarded for Windows). |
| 2 | `api.py:199 Broker.find` | Entry point. NOT a driver — it spawns and returns. Caller consumes results from the queue passed in step 1. |
| 3 | `api.py:246 -> resolver.py:307-340 Resolver.get_real_ext_ips` | `ips = await self._resolver.get_real_ext_ips()`. Spawns one `_probe_family` task per routable family (resolver.py:309), waits for first success (`_wait_for_first_success`, resolver.py:322/343), then gives the loser the REMAINING timeout budget (`_drain_with_budget`, resolver.py:326). try/finally cancels stragglers + `gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)` (resolver.py:332-335) so aiohttp connectors close. Returns a frozenset (dual-stack aware). |
| 4 | `api.py:247 -> api.py:612 _update_types` | Normalizes `types` list into a `{proto: levels}` dict. `find` raises ValueError if empty (api.py:249-250). |
| 5 | `api.py:252 -> checker.py:32 Checker.__init__` | Constructs the Checker. Side effect: `Judge.clear()` (checker.py:47) WIPES process-global class state `Judge.available` / `Judge.ev`. Builds judge list (checker.py:48), computes `_req_http_proto`/`_req_https_proto`/`_req_smtp_proto` gate flags (checker.py:82-86) and `self._ngtrs` (checker.py:88). |
| 6 | `api.py:267-273 Broker.find` | Spawns exactly TWO concurrent tasks: `create_task(self._checker.check_judges())` and `create_task(self._grab(types, check=True))` (or `self._load(data, check=True)` at api.py:269 when raw data was passed). Both appended to `self._all_tasks` — the cancellation registry. find() then RETURNS; no awaiting of results. |
| 7 | `checker.py:90 Checker.check_judges` | `await asyncio.gather(*[j.check(real_ext_ips=...) for j in self._judges])` (checker.py:94) — ALL judges probed in parallel, unbounded. Runs CONCURRENTLY with _grab, so proxy checks can begin before this finishes. |
| 8 | `judge.py:73 Judge.check` | Resolves judge host (judge.py:95), HTTP-GETs it, and on verification appends self to global `Judge.available[scheme]` (judge.py:141) and calls `Judge.ev[scheme].set()` (judge.py:142). The event fires on the FIRST verified judge, not after all complete — this is the gate that releases waiting check tasks. |
| 9 | `checker.py:98-137 Checker.check_judges` | AFTER the gather: prunes `self._judges` to working ones (98), and for each protocol with zero judges sets the event anyway to unblock waiters (checker.py:111/117/121), flips `_req_*_proto` to False (109/115/120), and REMOVES protos from `self._ngtrs` (checker.py:124-125). If no judges at all: raises RuntimeError (checker.py:137) inside a task nobody awaits. |
| 10 | `api.py:404 Broker._grab` | `while True:` outer loop (api.py:418). In find() mode it `break`s after one cycle (api.py:430); in serve() mode it sleeps GRAB_PAUSE=180s (api.py:427) and re-grabs FOREVER. |
| 11 | `api.py:405-415 _grab._get_tasks` | Generator filtering providers by `not types or not pr.proto or bool(pr.proto & types.keys())` (api.py:409), then yielding batches of MAX_CONCURRENT_PROVIDERS=3 as `create_task(pr.get_proxies())` (api.py:412), each appended to `_all_tasks` (414). Tasks for batch N+1 are only created after batch N is fully handled — so provider concurrency is capped at 3. |
| 12 | `api.py:419-423 _grab` | `for tasks in _get_tasks(): for task in asyncio.as_completed(tasks): proxies = await task; for proxy in proxies: await self._handle(proxy, check=check)`. The inline `await self._handle` SERIALIZES handling (single producer) while the other 2 provider tasks keep fetching. |
| 13 | `providers.py:81 Provider.get_proxies` | Opens one `aiohttp.ClientSession` per call assigned to `self._session` (providers.py:88-90, an INSTANCE attr — clobbered by concurrent calls on the same Provider), runs `self._pipe()` (91), returns `self.proxies` — the ACCUMULATED set, never cleared between grab cycles. |
| 14 | `providers.py:135 Provider._get` | `async with (self._sem_provider, self._session.request(...))` — per-provider `asyncio.Semaphore(max_conn=4)` created in __init__ (providers.py:54), i.e. at MODULE IMPORT time for the PROVIDERS singletons. Retried up to _max_tries by `get` (providers.py:128-133). All transport errors swallowed to `page = ''` (providers.py:159). |
| 15 | `providers.py:163 Provider.find_proxies -> providers.py:76 proxies setter` | Regex-extracts (host, port); the setter stamps provider proto onto each: `[(host, port, self.proto) for host, port in new if port]` (providers.py:78) and `self._proxies.update(new)` (79) — monotonic accumulation into the singleton. |
| 16 | `api.py:434 Broker._handle` | Per raw (host, port, proto) tuple. `Proxy.create(*proxy, timeout, resolver, verify_ssl, loop)` (api.py:436); ResolveError/ValueError -> silently return (api.py:443-444). |
| 17 | `proxy.py:72 Proxy.create -> proxy.py:101 Proxy.__init__` | Awaits `resolver.resolve(host)` (proxy.py:89), which hits the CLASS-LEVEL shared cache `Resolver._cached_hosts` (resolver.py:37/437/460) with no in-flight dedup. __init__ then does a BLOCKING mmdb geo lookup on the event loop: `self._geo = Resolver.get_ip_info(self.host)` (proxy.py:133 -> module-global `_mmdb_reader`, resolver.py:23). Also computes `expected_types = set(types) & {valid protos}` (proxy.py:116). |
| 18 | `api.py:446 -> api.py:454 _is_unique / api.py:461 _geo_passed` | Dedupe against `self.unique_proxies` dict keyed (host, port) (api.py:455-456) — the dict grows UNBOUNDED and holds a permanent reference to every Proxy ever seen. Then country filter. |
| 19 | `api.py:468 Broker._push_to_check` | THE grab->check boundary. Serve-mode pause first: `if self._server and not self._proxies.empty() and self._limit <= 0: await self._proxies.join()` (api.py:480-482) — parks the sole producer until the Server drains the output queue. |
| 20 | `api.py:485 _push_to_check` | `await self._on_check.put(None)` — ACQUIRES a permit; blocks when maxsize=max_conn reached. This is the ONLY backpressure on proxy discovery. The `None` is a dummy payload; the queue is used purely as a semaphore + a join() counter. Nothing ever consumes it in a loop. |
| 21 | `api.py:486-488 _push_to_check` | `task = asyncio.create_task(self._checker.check(proxy))`; `task.add_done_callback(partial(_task_done, proxy))`; `self._all_tasks.append(task)`. Up to max_conn of these run concurrently. |
| 22 | `checker.py:166 Checker.check` | Optional DNSBL fan-out (`_in_DNSBL`, checker.py:155, gather over spam domains). Then the GATE: `await Judge.ev['HTTP'].wait()` / `['HTTPS']` / `['SMTP']` (checker.py:172-177) — level-triggered; returns instantly if already set. Then `ngtrs = proxy.expected_types & self._ngtrs` (checker.py:180) reading state step 9 may still be mutating. |
| 23 | `checker.py:185-190 Checker.check` | SEQUENTIAL `for proto in ngtrs:` loop -> `_check_conn_25` (checker.py:199) or `_check` (227). Sequential is LOAD-BEARING: each iteration mutates the single `proxy.ngtr` slot and the shared reader/writer dicts. |
| 24 | `checker.py:227 Checker._check` | `Judge.get_random(proto)` (228, secrets.choice over the globally-mutating available list). Retry loop `for _ in range(self._max_tries)` (231): `proxy.ngtr = proto` (233 -> proxy.py:283-284 constructs NGTRS[proto](self)), `await proxy.connect()` (234 -> proxy.py:347, wait_for(open_connection, timeout)), `negotiate` (235), `_send_test_request` (236 -> checker.py:284). ProxyTimeoutError -> `continue` (retry); other proxy errors -> `break` (give up). `finally: proxy.close()` (263 -> proxy.py:403) EVERY iteration. On success: `_check_test_response` (252/329), `_get_anonymity_lvl` (254/345), `proxy.types[proxy.ngtr.name] = lvl` (260). |
| 25 | `checker.py:192-197 Checker.check` | `working = any(results)`; `proxy.is_working = working` (193). `_types_passed(proxy)` (195 -> checker.py:139) which in strict mode DELETES entries from `proxy.types` (checker.py:149). Returns bool. |
| 26 | `api.py:469-478 _push_to_check._task_done (done callback, SYNC context)` | THE critical mechanism. `self._on_check.task_done()` (470) decrements the queue's `_unfinished_tasks` counter that `join()` waits on. Then `if not self._on_check.empty(): self._on_check.get_nowait()` (471-472) RELEASES the capacity permit and wakes a blocked putter. Two INDEPENDENT counters (verified: join() returns with qsize() still non-zero). Then `if f.result(): self._push_to_result(proxy)` (474-475); CancelledError swallowed (477-478). |
| 27 | `api.py:490 Broker._push_to_result` | `self._proxies.put_nowait(proxy)` (api.py:492) — NON-blocking, called from the sync done callback; raises QueueFull if the caller supplied a bounded queue. Then `_update_limit()` (493). |
| 28 | `api.py:495 Broker._update_limit` | `self._limit -= 1`; `if self._limit == 0 and not self._server: self._done()` (497-498). Note `== 0` not `<= 0`, so limit=0 ('unlimited') counts into negatives and never fires. Also fires on the None sentinel push (see step 30). |
| 29 | `api.py:431-432 _grab (terminal) / api.py:401-402 _load (terminal)` | `await self._on_check.join()` — blocks until every spawned check task has run its done callback (unfinished counter hits 0), THEN `self._done()`. This is the only thing preventing in-flight checks from being cancelled at shutdown. |
| 30 | `api.py:516 Broker._done` | TERMINATION. `while self._all_tasks: task = self._all_tasks.pop(); if not task.done(): task.cancel()` (518-521) — cancels the CURRENTLY RUNNING _grab task (self-cancellation; CancelledError lands at the next await). Then `self._push_to_result(None)` (522) — the poison pill. Because _push_to_result calls _update_limit, _done can RE-ENTER itself exactly once (pushing two Nones) when _limit was 1. |
| 31 | `cli.py:387-407 handle (the consumer)` | `while True: proxy = await proxies.get(); if proxy is None: break`. Never calls task_done() — safe only because the `_proxies.join()` pause (step 19) is guarded by `if self._server`. |
| 32 | `api.py:275 Broker.serve` | SYNC method. Validates limit > 0 (api.py:349). Builds `Server(..., proxies=self._proxies, ...)` (356). Defines `async def run_server(): await self._server.start(); asyncio.create_task(self.find(limit=limit, **kwargs))` (366-368), then `self._loop.run_until_complete(run_server())` (370) — requires a NON-running loop; the caller then run_forever()s (cli.py:533). |
| 33 | `server.py:228 Server.start` | `await asyncio.start_server(self._accept, host, port, backlog=self._backlog)`. |
| 34 | `server.py:285 Server._accept -> server.py:301` | Per client: `f = asyncio.create_task(self._handle(...))`; `f.add_done_callback(_on_completion)`; `self._connections[f] = (reader, writer)` (303). Unbounded connection tasks. |
| 35 | `server.py:364 -> server.py:59 ProxyPool.get` | `proxy = await self._proxy_pool.get(scheme)`. If `len(_pool) + len(_newcomers) < _min_queue` -> `_import` (61-62); else pop a newcomer (63-64); else pop from the `heapq` best-pool matching scheme (70-77), falling back to `_import` (84). |
| 36 | `server.py:88 ProxyPool._import` | THE consumer of the output queue and the RELEASE for the step-19 pause: `proxy = await asyncio.wait_for(self._proxies.get(), timeout=self._import_timeout)` (93-94) then `self._proxies.task_done()` (96) — which is what lets `_proxies.join()` return. `if not proxy: raise NoProxyError` (98-99) — the None sentinel becomes the server's shutdown signal. Wrong-scheme proxies are `self.put(proxy)`-ed into the LOCAL pool, not re-queued (101). Timeout -> NoProxyError (106-109). |
| 37 | `server.py:370-446 Server._handle` | Retry loop `for attempt in range(self._max_tries)` (362). `await proxy.connect()` (371), negotiate (381), then bidirectional relay: two `create_task(self._stream(...))` (399-409) awaited via `asyncio.gather(*stream)` (411). ErrorOnStream cancels both stream tasks (430-432). `finally: proxy.log(...); proxy.close(); self._proxy_pool.put(proxy)` (443-446) — returns the proxy to the heap with updated stats. |
| 38 | `server.py:115 ProxyPool.put -> server.py:127` | `heapq.heappush(self._pool, (proxy.avg_resp_time, proxy))`. Proxies below _min_req_proxy go to `_newcomers` (123); proxies exceeding max_error_rate/max_resp_time are DROPPED (124-125). |
| 39 | `server.py:286-299 Server._accept._on_completion -> server.py:236 Server.stop` | SHUTDOWN. `NoProxyError` -> `self.stop()` (296-297). stop() cancels connections (239-241), closes the server, and calls `self._loop.stop()` (248) — killing the whole event loop from inside a done callback. That is how `cli.py:533 loop.run_forever()` returns. |

**Port risks**

- _on_check IS NOT A CHANNEL — porting it as `mpsc::channel(max_conn)` with a receiver task SILENTLY REMOVES ALL BACKPRESSURE. Nothing in Python ever consumes it in a loop: `put(None)` (api.py:485) acquires a permit, `get_nowait()` in the *done callback* (api.py:472) releases it. A tokio receiver task would drain instantly, `send()` would never block, and the broker would spawn unbounded check tasks (200 -> tens of thousands), exhausting FDs. Correct port: `Arc<Semaphore>` with `acquire_owned()` before spawn, permit moved into and dropped by the check task.
- task_done() and get_nowait() drive TWO INDEPENDENT COUNTERS — I verified `join()` returns while `qsize()` is still non-zero. `task_done()` (api.py:470) decrements `_unfinished_tasks` (what `join()` at api.py:431 waits on); `get_nowait()` (api.py:472) frees a capacity slot. A Rust port needs BOTH: `Semaphore(max_conn)` for capacity AND a separate outstanding-checks `AtomicUsize` + `Notify` (or a `JoinSet`) for the join barrier. Implement only the semaphore and `_grab` returns before checks finish -> `_done()` cancels every in-flight check -> proxies silently dropped. Implement only the counter and discovery runs unbounded.
- `if not self._on_check.empty()` (api.py:471) is only safe because there is EXACTLY ONE producer. `_grab` awaits `_handle` inline (api.py:423), so `put` is never concurrent. A tokio port that spawns per-provider or per-proxy handler tasks (the natural `JoinSet` idiom) breaks this invariant: two callbacks can race the empty() check, one skips its `get_nowait()`, and a permit is PERMANENTLY LEAKED. After max_conn leaks the grab loop blocks forever on `put` with zero checks running — a silent, unrecoverable stall. Either preserve single-producer or use a real `Semaphore` where the permit is an RAII guard (which fixes this by construction).
- `_done()` (api.py:516) IS RE-ENTRANT and a naive `Mutex<DoneState>` DEADLOCKS. Path: `_done` -> `_push_to_result(None)` (522) -> `_update_limit` (493) -> `if self._limit == 0` (497) -> `_done()` again. It self-limits because the second call sees `_limit == -1`, but it pushes TWO `None` sentinels when `_done()` fires while `_limit == 1`. tokio/std `Mutex` are NOT reentrant -> instant self-deadlock. Port `_done` as an idempotent `AtomicBool::swap(true)` guard, and decide deliberately whether to emit one sentinel or two (consumers that break on the first None leave the second orphaned in the queue).
- `_done()` CANCELS THE TASK THAT IS CALLING IT. `_all_tasks` (api.py:518-521) contains the `_grab` task, which calls `_done()` from api.py:432. In Python `task.cancel()` on the running task only sets `_must_cancel`; the sync remainder of `_done()` — crucially `_push_to_result(None)` at 522 — still completes, THEN the CancelledError lands. A tokio port using `AbortHandle::abort()` on the current task, or dropping a `JoinSet` that owns the current future, can lose the sentinel push -> every consumer blocks on `recv()` FOREVER. Push the sentinel BEFORE any abort, and exclude the current task from the abort set.
- `_push_to_result` uses `put_nowait` (api.py:492) and is called FROM A SYNC DONE CALLBACK (api.py:475). Two consequences: (a) there is no async context, so a tokio port cannot `.await` a `send()` here — it must `try_send()`; (b) if the user passes a bounded queue (`Broker(queue=asyncio.Queue(maxsize=N))`), `put_nowait` raises QueueFull inside a callback, where it is swallowed by the loop exception handler and the proxy is SILENTLY DROPPED. A Rust port that makes the output channel bounded and awaits the send inverts the design: it would apply backpressure the Python never had, deadlocking against the serve-mode `_proxies.join()` pause (api.py:482) whose release requires the consumer to keep pulling.
- The serve-mode pause `await self._proxies.join()` (api.py:482) has NO tokio equivalent and is NOT a capacity bound — it is an ALL-ITEMS-CONSUMED barrier released by `ProxyPool._import`'s `task_done()` (server.py:96). It is intentionally unbounded-wait: if no client ever connects, the grab loop parks FOREVER (the documented 'lazy pause'). A port that models it as a bounded channel resumes discovery as soon as ONE slot frees (wrong: should wait for FULL drain), and a port that adds a timeout or a drain task defeats `limit` entirely and grabs unboundedly.
- Subtle: `ProxyPool._import` calls `task_done()` (server.py:96) BEFORE deciding the proxy's fate, and wrong-scheme proxies are `self.put(proxy)`-ed into the LOCAL heap (server.py:101), never re-queued. So the `_proxies.join()` barrier is satisfied by proxies the pool merely INSPECTED. A Rust port that 'fixes' this by re-queuing mismatched proxies makes the barrier never reach zero -> the grab loop deadlocks permanently in serve mode.
- `Judge.ev[proto]` is a LEVEL-TRIGGERED asyncio.Event; `tokio::sync::Notify` IS NOT. `checker.py:173-177` awaits `.wait()` from every one of up to max_conn check tasks, and the event was already `.set()` by the first verified judge (judge.py:142) — those waits return INSTANTLY. `Notify::notified()` waits for the NEXT notify, so every check task spawned after the judges verify would HANG FOREVER (a permit-holding hang -> the whole pipeline stalls). Use `watch::channel(bool)` + `wait_for(|v| *v)`, or a `Semaphore` with `add_permits(usize::MAX)`, or `Notify::notify_waiters()` plus a separate `AtomicBool` fast path.
- The judge gate is set on the FIRST verified judge (judge.py:142), but `check_judges` prunes `Checker._ngtrs` (checker.py:124-125) and flips `_req_*_proto` (109/115/120) AFTER the gather completes — i.e. AFTER check tasks are already reading them at checker.py:172-182. This is a benign-under-the-GIL data race that Rust will not compile: `Checker` is shared `&self` across tasks. Fixing it 'properly' (compute `_ngtrs` before spawning any check) is a BEHAVIOR CHANGE — early proxies would attempt a different protocol set than they do today.
- All-judges-fail is a load-bearing non-crash path: `check_judges` sets all three events then RAISES RuntimeError (checker.py:137) inside a task whose exception is NEVER retrieved. The pipeline continues with `_ngtrs` empty -> `results = []` -> `any([]) is False` -> every proxy fails the check, but termination still works normally via `_on_check.join()` -> `_done()`. A tokio port using `JoinSet` propagates that error on `join_next()`; if you `?` it you abort the grab and never push the None sentinel -> consumers hang instead of getting a clean empty result.
- The per-proxy protocol loop MUST STAY SEQUENTIAL (checker.py:185-190). Each iteration sets `proxy.ngtr = proto` (checker.py:233 -> proxy.py:284, a SINGLE `_ngtr` slot) and mutates the shared `_reader`/`_writer` dicts (proxy.py:138-139) and `_closed` flag, with `finally: proxy.close()` (checker.py:263) tearing the connection down each pass. Parallelizing with `join_all`/`FuturesUnordered` — the obvious 'optimization' a Rust port reaches for — corrupts the negotiator slot and the live socket, and races the `stat`/`_runtimes`/`_log` accumulators. Model as `&mut Proxy` held exclusively for the duration of `check`.
- `heapq.heappush(self._pool, (proxy.avg_resp_time, proxy))` (server.py:127) is a LATENT PANIC: on equal `avg_resp_time` Python falls through to comparing the second tuple element, and `Proxy` defines no `__lt__` -> `TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'Proxy'`, raised inside a client task. Rust's `BinaryHeap<(F, T)>` REQUIRES `Ord` on `T`, forcing you to confront it at compile time. Add an explicit tie-break (insertion seq) — but note this is a deliberate behavior change from an upstream crash.
- PROVIDERS are module-level SINGLETONS (providers.py:695) constructed at IMPORT time, and `Provider._proxies` is a set that ACCUMULATES FOREVER (providers.py:52/79, never cleared). `get_proxies()` returns the whole accumulated set every cycle (providers.py:96), so in serve mode's 180s re-grab loop (api.py:418-427) cycle N re-emits every proxy from cycles 1..N-1, and ONLY `Broker._is_unique` (api.py:454) suppresses them. A Rust port with per-run provider state (correct) makes each cycle emit only NEW proxies — which is fine — but if you also drop the unbounded `unique_proxies` dict (api.py:87) to fix the memory leak, you BREAK dedupe across cycles and re-check every proxy forever. The leak and the dedupe are the same data structure.
- `Provider._session` (providers.py:50) is an instance attribute reassigned per `get_proxies()` call (providers.py:88-90), and `_sem_provider` (providers.py:54) is shared by the singleton. Two concurrent `get_proxies()` on the same Provider clobber the session mid-flight. `_grab` never does this (one task per provider per batch, api.py:412), but the serve-mode 180s loop plus a second Broker in-process does. In Rust these singletons become `static` -> `Arc<Mutex<...>>` or a compile error; prefer per-run `Provider` instances owning their own client.
- `proto=("SOCKS4")` (providers.py:706) and `proto=("SOCKS5")` (710) are STRINGS, not tuples — missing commas. I verified: `'SOCKS4' & {'SOCKS4': None}.keys()` == `set()` (falsy), because dict_keys.__rand__ iterates the string CHARACTER BY CHARACTER -> {'S','O','C','K','4'}. Two live consequences: (a) `_grab`'s filter `bool(pr.proto & types.keys())` (api.py:409) is False, so both proxyscrape SOCKS providers are SILENTLY SKIPPED whenever `types` is given — i.e. on every `find()` call; (b) if they did run, `Provider.proxies` (providers.py:78) stamps `types='SOCKS4'` -> `Proxy.__init__`'s `set(types) & {valid protos}` (proxy.py:116) -> empty `expected_types` -> the proxy is checked against ALL protocols. A Rust port with `&[Proto]` 'fixes' both silently: two providers re-enter rotation and protocol filtering starts applying. Decide this deliberately rather than discovering it as a behavior diff.
- `asyncio.as_completed(tasks)` with `await self._handle(...)` INSIDE the loop (api.py:420-423) has precise semantics: handling is serialized against the batch, but while `_handle` is parked on `_on_check.put` backpressure (485) or `_proxies.join()` (482), the other 2 provider fetch tasks KEEP MAKING PROGRESS. `try_join_all` + handle-after loses that overlap (slower, more provider timeouts); `JoinSet` + `while let Some(r) = set.join_next()` reproduces it correctly. Do not spawn `_handle` per proxy — that breaks the single-producer invariant (see risk #3).
- `Broker.serve()` (api.py:275) is a SYNC method calling `self._loop.run_until_complete(run_server())` (370) — it requires a NON-running loop and the caller then `run_forever()`s (cli.py:533). Shutdown is `Server.stop()` -> `self._loop.stop()` (server.py:248), i.e. KILLING THE EVENT LOOP FROM INSIDE A DONE CALLBACK (server.py:296-297). tokio has no `Runtime::shutdown()` callable from within a task (it panics). Restructure around a `CancellationToken`/oneshot that the main task `select!`s on, and make `serve()` async.
- `ProxyPool._import`'s 5s `wait_for` (server.py:93-94, import_timeout=5.0) raises NoProxyError on timeout (106-109) -> `_on_completion` -> `self.stop()` -> loop dies. So a 5-second gap in proxy supply KILLS THE ENTIRE SERVER. Combined with the 180s GRAB_PAUSE (api.py:23) and the `_proxies.join()` pause, this is easy to hit. A tokio port must reproduce it exactly (surprising but load-bearing for the CLI's exit behavior) or deliberately change it — but the None sentinel ALSO routes through NoProxyError (server.py:98-99), so the two shutdown causes are indistinguishable at the catch site (server.py:296).
- `Server.stop()` ITERATES `self._connections` (server.py:239) while the done callback `_on_completion` POPS from it (server.py:287). Safe in CPython only because callbacks are deferred to the next loop tick. In Rust this is a straight borrow-checker error (iterate + remove on a HashMap), forcing a restructure — collect the handles into a Vec first, then abort. Same shape at api.py:518-521 where `_done` destructively pops `_all_tasks` while done callbacks may append to it (api.py:488).
- `Resolver._cached_hosts` (resolver.py:37) is CLASS-level, shared by every Resolver instance across Broker/Checker/Judge/Server/every `Proxy.create`, with NO in-flight dedup: N concurrent `Proxy.create` calls for the same host issue N DNS queries and all write the cache (resolver.py:460-462). Free under the GIL; in Rust it's `Arc<Mutex<HashMap>>`/`DashMap`. Adding single-flight (the obvious improvement) is a real behavior change — dramatically fewer DNS queries, different timing, and the `_cache_compatible` family check (resolver.py:471) interacts with it.
- `Resolver.get_ip_info` (resolver.py, via module-global `_mmdb_reader` at resolver.py:23) is called SYNCHRONOUSLY from `Proxy.__init__` (proxy.py:133), i.e. a blocking mmap read on the event loop for EVERY grabbed proxy, inside `_handle`'s await chain. In tokio this blocks a worker thread. `spawn_blocking` is the reflex, but it makes `Proxy::new` async and reorders the pipeline; the reader must also be `Arc<maxminddb::Reader<Mmap>>` (Send+Sync). Consider keeping it sync — mmap reads are ~microseconds — rather than restructuring construction.
- SIGINT handling: `self._loop.add_signal_handler(signal.SIGINT, self.stop)` (api.py:121) runs `_done()` SYNCHRONOUSLY in the signal callback — cancelling tasks and `put_nowait(None)` from a non-async context (NotImplementedError-guarded for Windows, api.py:125). `tokio::signal::ctrl_c()` is async and lives in a `select!` arm, so the port's `stop()` becomes async. Ensure the sentinel is still pushed EXACTLY ONCE across all three termination paths (limit-reached api.py:497, providers-exhausted api.py:432, SIGINT api.py:500) — they can race, and today only the `_done` re-entrancy accident (risk #4) bounds the duplicate count.
- `_update_limit` uses `if self._limit == 0` (api.py:497), NOT `<= 0`. With `limit=0` ('not limited', api.py:92) it counts down into negatives and never fires — correct by accident. But the None sentinel push ALSO decrements it (via `_push_to_result` -> `_update_limit`, api.py:493), which is what produces the double-sentinel in risk #4. A Rust port using `saturating_sub` or `usize` changes both behaviors: `usize` underflow-panics on the unlimited path, and `<= 0` would make `limit=0` terminate immediately with zero proxies.

---

### Flow: Checker.check(proxy) validates ONE proxy across a set of protocols, sequentially, reusing one Proxy object as a mutable connection handle.

Driver context: Broker.find() (api.py:246) discovers the host's real external IPs, constructs the Checker (api.py:252), then spawns TWO concurrent tasks — check_judges() (api.py:267) and _grab()/_load() (api.py:269-271). Judge probing and proxy grabbing run in parallel; check() coroutines rendezvous with judge readiness via three class-level asyncio.Events.

Per-proxy path: _handle (api.py:434) constructs the Proxy (which does the geo lookup synchronously in Proxy.__init__, proxy.py:133), dedups (api.py:454), geo-filters (api.py:461), then _push_to_check (api.py:468) acquires a concurrency slot by `await self._on_check.put(None)` against a maxsize=max_conn Queue — the Queue IS the semaphore — and spawns an independent task per proxy.

Inside check() (checker.py:166): optional DNSBL gather → block on per-scheme judge Events → intersect proxy.expected_types with the (mutating) self._ngtrs → for each proto sequentially: pick ONE random judge, then loop up to max_tries { set negotiator → TCP connect → protocol negotiate → send HTTP probe → recv } retrying ONLY on timeout, breaking on any other proxy error. On success: decompress, verify the response echoes a per-request random marker + Referer + Cookie + some IP, and (HTTP only) classify anonymity Transparent/Anonymous/High by set-intersecting the host's real ext-IPs against IPs found in the page. Result is written into proxy.types[negotiator_name] = level.

Aggregate: working = any(results) (checker.py:192); _types_passed (checker.py:139) then filters/prunes proxy.types against requested types+levels. The task's done-callback (api.py:469) releases the slot and pushes passing proxies to the result queue. Timing is a side effect: Proxy.log (proxy.py:322) accumulates per-operation runtimes into _runtimes, and avg_resp_time (proxy.py:237) averages them — EXCLUDING any operation whose log message contains the substring "timeout".

Geo is NOT determined in check(): it is computed eagerly in Proxy.__init__ via a blocking maxminddb read and only filtered pre-check by Broker._geo_passed.

> **Concurrency model.** EVENT LOOP: single-threaded asyncio throughout. Every "safe" unsynchronized mutation below is safe ONLY because no preemption occurs between an await-free read and write. This assumption does not survive a multi-threaded tokio runtime. QUEUES (3, all with different semantics): 1. `Broker._on_check = asyncio.Queue(maxsize=max_conn)` (api.py:102) — NOT a work queue. It is a hand-rolled SEMAPHORE + WaitGroup. Acquire = `await put(None)` (api.py:485, blocks at maxsize -> backpressure on the grab loop). Release = in the done-callback: `task_done()` (decrements the unfinished counter that put() incremented) then `get_nowait()` (frees the maxsize slot). Nothing ever `get()`s to consume work. `await self._on_check.join()` (api.py:401, 431) = "all in-flight checks finished" barrier. put/task_done are 1:1 so join() balances; get_nowait is purely slot reclamation and is guarded by `not empty()`. 2. `Broker._proxies` = unbounded asyncio.Queue (api.py:82, user-suppliable). Producers `put_nowait` (never blocks). Termination via `None` poison sentinel pushed by `_done()` (api.py:522). 3. Serve-mode pause (api.py:480-483): `await self._proxies.join()` — backpressure keyed on the CONSUMER calling task_done() on every result, not on queue depth. EVENTS: `Judge.ev = {'HTTP','HTTPS','SMTP'} -> asyncio.Event` (judge.py:16-20), CLASS-LEVEL, constructed at import. Set by the FIRST successful Judge.check for that scheme (judge.py:142) OR by check_judges for a scheme with zero working judges (checker.py:111/117/121). Awaited unbounded by every check() (checker.py:172-177). This is the only rendezvous between judge probing and proxy checking, and it has no timeout and no error path. GATHER (2 uses, different failure policies): check_judges uses BARE `gather` (checker.py:94) — first exception propagates and kills the task. _in_DNSBL uses `gather(..., return_exceptions=True)` (checker.py:161) — exception OBJECTS land in the result list and are then truthiness-tested. TASKS: `asyncio.create_task` per judge-check batch, per provider (api.py:412, MAX_CONCURRENT_PROVIDERS=3 at a time via as_completed), and ONE PER PROXY for checking (api.py:486). All appended to `Broker._all_tasks`, which `_done()` drains-and-cancels (api.py:518-521). Cancellation is the ONLY way the infinite `while True` grab loop (api.py:418) exits in serve mode. NO Semaphore object is used for check concurrency despite the deprecated `max_concurrent_conn` kwarg accepting one (api.py:153-156) — it just reads `._value` off it. WITHIN check(): protocols run SEQUENTIALLY (checker.py:185-190), retries run SEQUENTIALLY (checker.py:231). So max_conn bounds PROXIES in flight, and each proxy holds at most ONE socket at a time. Concurrency is entirely at the proxy level. RESOLVER: Happy-Eyeballs A/AAAA race via `asyncio.wait(FIRST_COMPLETED)` + cancel-loser (resolver.py:512-544); ext-IP discovery races both families with a first-success + remaining-budget grace window and a try/finally that cancels+gathers stragglers (resolver.py:321-335).

> **Shared state.** CLASS-LEVEL / PROCESS-GLOBAL (the dangerous tier — these must NOT become `static` in Rust): - `Judge.available: {scheme -> [Judge]}` (judge.py:15). Mutated by Judge.check (append, judge.py:101/141), by Judge.clear (judge.py:65-67) which Checker.__init__ calls (checker.py:47). Read by Judge.get_random (judge.py:62) from every check task. Owner: nominally Checker, actually the Judge class. - `Judge.ev: {scheme -> asyncio.Event}` (judge.py:16). Set by Judge.check / check_judges; cleared by Judge.clear. Awaited by every check(). - `Resolver._cached_hosts: {hostname -> ip|list}` (resolver.py:37). Unbounded, no TTL, no locking, no in-flight dedup; shared by EVERY Resolver instance process-wide. Written in Resolver.resolve (resolver.py:460-462). - `_mmdb_reader` (resolver.py:23), module-level maxminddb handle, read by Resolver.get_ip_info from Proxy.__init__. => Constructing two Checkers/Brokers in one process silently clobbers judge state. CHECKER-OWNED, MUTATED CONCURRENTLY WITH READERS: - `Checker._ngtrs: set` — written by check_judges (checker.py:125) while check() tasks read it (checker.py:180-182). - `Checker._req_http_proto / _req_https_proto / _req_smtp_proto: bool` — written by check_judges (checker.py:109/115/120), read by check() (checker.py:172-177). - `Checker._judges: list` — rebound by check_judges (checker.py:98). - `Checker._real_ext_ips: frozenset` — immutable after __init__; safe to share (Arc<frozenset> / Arc<HashSet>). JUDGE-INSTANCE, SHARED ACROSS CHECK TASKS: - `judge.marks{'via','proxy'}: dict` (judge.py:29) — written by Judge.check (judge.py:138-139), read by _get_anonymity_lvl (checker.py:369) from many concurrent check tasks. Because ev fires on the FIRST good judge, a still-running Judge.check can mutate marks while readers read. Benign under the GIL; a real race in threaded Rust. - `judge.ip`, `judge.is_working` — same pattern. PROXY-INSTANCE (single-writer: exactly one check task owns a Proxy for the duration — the natural `&mut` / owned-value case in Rust): - `Proxy._types: dict` (proxy.py:129) — written by _check/_check_conn_25 (checker.py:220/260), MUTATED IN-PLACE (entries deleted) by _types_passed under strict (checker.py:149). - `Proxy._log: list`, `Proxy._runtimes: list`, `Proxy.stat{'requests', 'errors': Counter}` — appended by Proxy.log/connect (proxy.py:331-335, 400). - `Proxy._ngtr` — rebound per attempt (proxy.py:284), nulled by close() (proxy.py:426). - `Proxy._reader/_writer: {'conn','ssl'}`, `Proxy._closed`, `Proxy._schemes` cache. - Aliasing caveat: `Broker.unique_proxies` (api.py:87) retains a reference to EVERY Proxy forever, and show_stats (api.py:543-609) reads their logs/stats later — so Proxy is single-writer but multi-reader-after-the-fact, and the map grows unboundedly. BROKER-OWNED: - `Broker.unique_proxies: {(host,port) -> Proxy}` (api.py:87) — dedup set + permanent retention. Mutated by _is_unique (api.py:456). - `Broker._limit: int` (api.py:93) — decremented by _update_limit (api.py:496) from _push_to_result; drives termination. - `Broker._all_tasks: list` (api.py:88) — appended everywhere, drained+cancelled by _done (api.py:518). - `Broker._checker`, `_server`, `_countries`, `_signal_handler_registered`.

| # | Where | What |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `api.py:246 Broker.find` | `ips = await self._resolver.get_real_ext_ips()` -> frozenset of canonical ext-IPs (v4 and/or v6 on dual-stack). Racing per-family probes, first-success + remaining-budget grace window (resolver.py:267-340). |
| 2 | `api.py:252 Broker.find` | Construct Checker(real_ext_ips=ips, types=..., max_tries, timeout, post, strict, dnsbl). Checker.__init__ calls Judge.clear() (checker.py:47) which WIPES the CLASS-GLOBAL Judge.available lists and Judge.ev events. |
| 3 | `checker.py:82-88 Checker.__init__` | Derive `_req_http_proto`/`_req_https_proto`/`_req_smtp_proto` (which judge schemes must be ready) and `_ngtrs` = the set of negotiator keys to try (e.g. {'HTTP','SOCKS5','CONNECT:25'}). Note _ngtrs holds NEGOTIATOR keys, not judge SCHEME names. |
| 4 | `api.py:267-273 Broker.find` | Spawn `check_judges()` and `_grab()/_load()` as two concurrent tasks. No ordering barrier between them — check() tasks can be created before any judge is verified. |
| 5 | `checker.py:94 Checker.check_judges` | `await asyncio.gather(*[j.check(real_ext_ips=...) for j in self._judges])` — BARE gather, no return_exceptions. All judges probed concurrently, unbounded. |
| 6 | `judge.py:73-149 Judge.check` | Resolve judge host; SMTP judges pass trivially on resolve success (judge.py:99-103). HTTP/HTTPS judges: aiohttp GET, require status 200 AND a real ext-IP visible in page AND the random marker present. On success append to Judge.available[scheme], record marks{'via','proxy'} = page counts, and SET Judge.ev[scheme] — i.e. the event fires on the FIRST good judge, not after all are probed. |
| 7 | `checker.py:106-125 Checker.check_judges` | For each scheme with ZERO working judges: set its Event anyway (unblock waiters), flip the _req_*_proto flag off, and remove the disabled protocols from self._ngtrs. HTTP disables ['HTTP','CONNECT:80','SOCKS4','SOCKS5']; HTTPS disables ['HTTPS']; SMTP appends 'SMTP' — which matches NO key in _ngtrs (see port_risks). |
| 8 | `api.py:485-488 Broker._push_to_check` | `await self._on_check.put(None)` blocks once max_conn checks are in flight = BACKPRESSURE. Then `asyncio.create_task(self._checker.check(proxy))` + `task.add_done_callback(partial(_task_done, proxy))`. One task per proxy; there is NO worker pool. |
| 9 | `checker.py:167-170 Checker.check` | If dnsbl configured: `await self._in_DNSBL(proxy.host)` -> reversed-IP + domain queries via gather(return_exceptions=True) (checker.py:155-164). If listed, log and return False (no connection attempted). |
| 10 | `checker.py:172-177 Checker.check` | RENDEZVOUS: `await Judge.ev['HTTP'\|'HTTPS'\|'SMTP'].wait()` for each required scheme. Unbounded wait — no timeout. This is where every check task parks until judges are ready. |
| 11 | `checker.py:179-182 Checker.check` | `ngtrs = proxy.expected_types & self._ngtrs` if the proxy declared expected types, else the full self._ngtrs. Read AFTER the event waits, so it observes check_judges' mutations. |
| 12 | `checker.py:185-190 Checker.check` | SEQUENTIAL loop `for proto in ngtrs:` — one protocol at a time, awaited. 'CONNECT:25' -> _check_conn_25, everything else -> _check. Iteration order is Python-set arbitrary. |
| 13 | `checker.py:228 Checker._check / judge.py:52 Judge.get_random` | JUDGE SELECTION: `secrets.choice(Judge.available[scheme])` where scheme = HTTPS for 'HTTPS', SMTP for 'CONNECT:25', else HTTP. Called ONCE per protocol, OUTSIDE the retry loop — all max_tries attempts hit the SAME judge. Raises IndexError on an empty list. |
| 14 | `checker.py:231-235 Checker._check` | Retry loop `for _ in range(self._max_tries)`: `proxy.ngtr = proto` (proxy.py:282 instantiates a FRESH negotiator from NGTRS), `await proxy.connect()` (proxy.py:347, asyncio.open_connection wrapped in wait_for(timeout)), `await proxy.ngtr.negotiate(host=judge.host, ip=judge.ip)`. |
| 15 | `negotiators.py:73-227` | NEGOTIATION per protocol: HttpNgtr (226) = no-op, sets check_anon_lvl=True + use_full_path=True. Socks5Ngtr (73) = greeting, then ATYP-dispatched CONNECT, reads 4-byte reply header then variable BND.ADDR. Socks4Ngtr (140) = v4-only, rejects IPv6 with BadResponseError. Connect80/25Ngtr (175/189) = CONNECT authority-form (IPv6 bracketed); :25 additionally expects SMTP 220. HttpsNgtr (209) = CONNECT :443 then `proxy.connect(ssl=True)` in-place start_tls upgrade (proxy.py:353-378). |
| 16 | `checker.py:236 -> checker.py:284 _send_test_request` | Build request via _request (checker.py:267): GET/POST with Host, Connection: close, and headers carrying a per-request random marker `rv` (utils.py:52-66), absolute-URI path iff ngtr.use_full_path. `await proxy.send(request)` (proxy.py:428) then `await proxy.recv()` (proxy.py:441). Non-200 -> BadStatusError; missing CRLFCRLF split -> ValueError -> BadResponseError. |
| 17 | `checker.py:239-249 Checker._check` | ERROR POLICY: `except ProxyTimeoutError: continue` (RETRY) vs `except (ProxyConnError, ProxyRecvError, ProxySendError, ProxyEmptyRecvError, BadStatusError, BadResponseError): break` (NO retry). Success also breaks. So max_tries only ever applies to timeouts. |
| 18 | `checker.py:262-263 Checker._check` | `finally: proxy.close()` runs on EVERY attempt (proxy.py:403). close() is sync, idempotent via self._closed, closes ssl writer then conn writer, and NULLS self._ngtr. The else-block reads proxy.ngtr.name BEFORE finally runs — order matters. |
| 19 | `checker.py:251-252 -> checker.py:311, 329` | _decompress_content (311): de-chunk if Transfer-Encoding: chunked, zlib.decompress with MAX_WBITS\|32, decode utf-8/ignore. _check_test_response (329): require ALL of [marker rv in body, some IP found, Referer echoed, Cookie echoed] -> True. |
| 20 | `checker.py:254-260 -> checker.py:345 _get_anonymity_lvl` | ANONYMITY (only when ngtr.check_anon_lvl, i.e. HTTP): lowercase body, get_all_ip -> canonical set. Transparent iff real_ext_ips & foundIP is non-empty (set intersection — passes for EITHER family on dual-stack); else Anonymous iff body's 'via'/'proxy' counts EXCEED the judge's baseline judge.marks; else High. Result stored as `proxy.types[proxy.ngtr.name] = lvl` (None for non-HTTP). |
| 21 | `checker.py:192-197 Checker.check` | `working = any(results)`; `proxy.is_working = working`. Then `_types_passed` (checker.py:139): non-strict = any requested proto/level match wins; strict = DELETES non-matching entries from proxy.types in-place and requires a survivor. Returns the final bool. |
| 22 | `proxy.py:322-335 Proxy.log / proxy.py:237 avg_resp_time` | TIMING: each connect/recv logs `runtime = time.time() - stime` and appends to self._runtimes ONLY IF `runtime and 'timeout' not in msg` — a SUBSTRING test on the already-60-char-TRUNCATED message. avg_resp_time = mean(_runtimes). Errors counted into self.stat['errors'][err.errmsg]; stat['requests'] incremented per connect (proxy.py:400). |
| 23 | `api.py:469-478 Broker._push_to_check._task_done` | TERMINATION per check: done-callback calls `self._on_check.task_done()` (releases join() accounting) then `get_nowait()` if non-empty (frees the maxsize slot), then `f.result()` — pushing to the result queue if True. Only asyncio.CancelledError is caught; any other exception escapes into the loop exception handler. |
| 24 | `api.py:490-498 -> api.py:516 Broker._done` | _push_to_result puts the proxy on the unbounded result Queue and decrements _limit; on `_limit == 0` (exact equality) calls _done(), which CANCELS every task in _all_tasks and pushes a `None` POISON SENTINEL to the result queue. _grab/_load also `await self._on_check.join()` then _done() (api.py:401-402, 431-432). |

**Port risks**

- CONFIRMED DEADLOCK (probed): check_judges uses a BARE `asyncio.gather` (checker.py:94) with no return_exceptions, while Judge.check (judge.py:119-124) catches ONLY TimeoutError/ClientOSError/ClientResponseError/ServerDisconnectedError. Any other error (aiohttp.ClientPayloadError, UnicodeDecodeError from resp.text(), a bug) propagates out of gather -> check_judges task dies BEFORE reaching the event-setting code at checker.py:106-121 -> Judge.ev is never set -> every check() blocks forever on `ev.wait()` (checker.py:172-177, no timeout) -> `_on_check.join()` never returns -> Broker hangs with zero output. I reproduced this exactly. A tokio port using Notify/watch must fire the readiness signal on EVERY exit path (or model it as a `watch::Sender` whose DROP closes the channel and wakes waiters with an Err) — the natural `try_join_all` + `?` translation reproduces the hang, and a `Notify` dropped without notify_waiters() hangs permanently with no diagnostic.
- CONFIRMED BUG (probed): the SMTP disable path is a no-op. checker.py:118-121 appends the judge SCHEME name 'SMTP' to disable_protocols, but self._ngtrs holds NEGOTIATOR keys, so 'CONNECT:25' is never removed (checker.py:123-125). With CONNECT:25 requested and both smtp:// judges failing DNS, check() reaches _check_conn_25 -> `Judge.get_random('CONNECT:25')` -> `secrets.choice([])` -> IndexError, raised OUTSIDE the try (checker.py:200/228). In Python this escapes check(), the done-callback's `f.result()` re-raises it (only CancelledError is caught, api.py:477), it lands in the loop exception handler, and the proxy is silently dropped — the run continues. The HTTP/HTTPS branches use matching key names so only SMTP is affected. In Rust, `choose()` returns Option: `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` PANICS the task; if the port uses a JoinSet that propagates panics or `abort_on_panic`, one dead SMTP judge takes down the whole run instead of dropping one proxy. Port the empty case explicitly as skip-this-protocol (and fix the scheme/negotiator key mismatch).
- `_on_check` is a Queue used as a semaphore, not a work queue (api.py:102, 485, 471-473). Acquire = `put(None)`, release = `task_done()` + `get_nowait()` in the DONE-CALLBACK, and `join()` is the drain barrier. A tokio port that reads this as an mpsc + worker pool changes the model fundamentally: here EVERY proxy immediately gets its OWN task once a slot is free (no pool, no fairness, no ordering). Correct mapping is `Arc<Semaphore>` (permit held for the task's lifetime, released on Drop = RAII) + a JoinSet/AtomicUsize+Notify to reproduce `join()`. Do not use a bounded mpsc + N workers — that serializes differently and changes how max_conn relates to in-flight sockets.
- Release-on-completion happens in a DONE-CALLBACK that runs for EVERY terminal state including cancellation (api.py:469-478): task_done() is called BEFORE `f.result()`, so a raising check still frees its slot (I verified qsize=0/unfinished=0 after an IndexError — no leak, no hang). A Rust port that releases the permit only on the Ok path, or that `?`-propagates the error before releasing, leaks permits and eventually deadlocks at max_conn. RAII permits get this right for free — but only if the permit is MOVED into the spawned task, not held by the spawner.
- Judge.available and Judge.ev are CLASS-LEVEL globals (judge.py:15-20) that Checker.__init__ RESETS via Judge.clear() (checker.py:47). Two Brokers/Checkers in one process clobber each other's judge state. The idiomatic Rust translation (`static`/`OnceLock`/`lazy_static`) faithfully reproduces this bug and makes it worse (real threads). Put judge state in an `Arc<JudgeRegistry>` owned by the Checker instead — this is a deliberate behavior FIX, so flag it rather than porting the global.
- Judge readiness fires on the FIRST successful judge (judge.py:142), NOT after all judges are probed. So check() tasks start while Judge.available is still being appended to and while a still-running Judge.check mutates `judge.marks` (judge.py:138-139) that _get_anonymity_lvl concurrently READS (checker.py:369). Safe only because of the GIL + single-threaded loop with no await between read and write. On a multi-threaded tokio runtime this is a genuine data race on shared judge state. Either snapshot judges into an immutable Arc once check_judges completes (changes timing: checks start later), or wrap marks in a lock (keeps timing, adds contention).
- Checker._ngtrs and _req_*_proto are MUTATED by check_judges (checker.py:109-125) while check() tasks READ them (checker.py:172-182). check() re-reads _ngtrs AFTER the event wait, so it deliberately observes the post-disable set. In Rust this needs Arc + interior mutability (RwLock) or — better — compute the final set inside check_judges and publish it once via `watch`/`OnceLock`, so readers get an immutable snapshot. A naive `&self` port won't compile; a naive `clone-per-task` port silently reads the PRE-disable set and tries protocols with no judges (-> the IndexError above).
- _in_DNSBL truthiness bug (checker.py:161-163): `gather(..., return_exceptions=True)` then `any([r for r in responses if not isinstance(r, ResolveError)])`. Exception OBJECTS other than ResolveError are truthy and are counted as 'listed in DNSBL' -> the proxy is rejected. A ResolveError means 'not listed'; a successful resolve means 'listed'.
