Class-level asyncio.Event per scheme, **constructed at IMPORT time (outside any running loop)**, signalled by `check` when a judge for that scheme verifies, cleared by `clear`, and awaited by checker.py (`await Judge.ev[proto].wait()`) to gate proxy checks until judges exist. **Set-once latch semantics: many waiters, level-triggered.**<br>**→ Rust:** `tokio::sync::watch::channel(bool)` per scheme (**level-triggered, matches Event's already-set-passes-immediately semantics**) — **NOT `Notify`, whose `notified()` is edge-triggered and would deadlock a waiter that arrives after the set.** `clear()` maps to sending `false`. Construction at import time is a Python-only hazard that disappears in Rust. |
| `Judge.__init__` | method | `__init__(self, url, timeout=8, verify_ssl=False, loop=None)`. Parses url with `urlparse` into **uppercase** scheme and netloc host; derives `path` via the **fragile `url.split(self.host)[-1]`**; inits `ip=None`, `_is_working=False`, `marks={'via':0,'proxy':0}`. Resolves the loop via `loop or asyncio.get_running_loop()`, swallowing RuntimeError to leave `_loop=loop` (possibly None). **SIDE EFFECT: constructs a NEW per-instance `Resolver(loop=self._loop)`, so every Judge carries its own independent DNS cache.**<br>**→ Rust:** `fn new(url: &str) -> Result<Judge, JudgeError>` — urlparse never raises but `Url::parse` does, so **construction becomes fallible.** Replace the `split(host)[-1]` path hack with `url.path()` + `url.query()`. Drop `loop` entirely. **Take `resolver: Arc<Resolver>` as a ctor param instead of building one per judge — the current per-instance Resolver is almost certainly an unintended cache-fragmentation bug worth fixing in the port.** Use a builder for the timeout/verify_ssl optionals. |
| `Judge.__repr__` | method | Returns `f"<Judge [{self.scheme}] {self.host}>"`. Pure. Used in every log line in `check` and in checker.py's `proxy.log(f"Selected judge: {judge}")`.<br>**→ Rust:** `impl fmt::Display for Judge` (and derive `Debug`). **The exact `<Judge [HTTP] host>` string leaks into user-visible logs — keep it byte-identical to avoid breaking log scrapers.** |
| `Judge.is_working` | property | Returns `self._is_working`. Pure getter, no validation, no side effects. Read by checker.py's `[j for j in self._judges if j.is_working]` filter.<br>**→ Rust:** plain `pub is_working: bool` field, or `fn is_working(&self) -> bool`. **The property wrapper carries zero behavior — do not port the getter/setter pair as a trait.** |
| `Judge.is_working` (setter) | property | Assigns `self._is_working = val`. **No validation, no coercion, no side effects — a pure passthrough.** Called only from within `check`.<br>**→ Rust:** collapses into direct field assignment behind `&mut self`. No setter needed. |
| `Judge.get_random` | classmethod | `@classmethod get_random(cls, proto)`. Maps proto → scheme (`'HTTPS'`→HTTPS, `'CONNECT:25'`→SMTP, everything else→HTTP) and returns `secrets.choice(cls.available[scheme])`. **ERROR PATH: raises IndexError (from `secrets.choice` on an empty sequence) if no judge for that scheme has verified** — callers in checker.py rely on `Judge.ev[proto].wait()` having been awaited first to guarantee non-emptiness. **Reads global mutable state.**<br>**→ Rust:** `fn get_random(&self, proto: Proto) -> Option<Arc<Judge>>` on the registry — **return `Option`/`Result<_, JudgeError::NoJudges>` rather than panicking, since the IndexError is a latent crash the port should close.** Proto→scheme is a `match` on a `Proto` enum. **`secrets.choice` (CSPRNG) is only used to appease SonarCloud S2245; the comment states selection is not security-sensitive, so `rand::rng().random_range()` suffices** — reach for `OsRng` only if the lint equivalent matters. |
| `Judge.clear` | classmethod | `@classmethod clear(cls)`. Empties all three `available` lists in-place and clears all three `ev` events, resetting the global judge registry. **Called by `Checker.__init__` — so constructing a second Checker WIPES the first one's verified judges (global-state hazard).** Returns None.<br>**→ Rust:** `fn clear(&self)` on `JudgeRegistry`: truncate each Vec and `watch::Sender::send(false)` each latch. **Once the registry is an owned `Arc<JudgeRegistry>` per Checker, this cross-Checker clobbering bug evaporates** — each Checker constructs a fresh registry instead of resetting a global. |
| `Judge.check` | async method | `async check(self, real_ext_ips=None, real_ext_ip=None)`. Probes the judge and verifies it echoes one of the host's real external IPs. **Normalises legacy single-string `real_ext_ip` (and a bare str passed to `real_ext_ips`) into a frozenset.** Resolves `self.host` → **MUTATES `self.ip`**; returns None silently on `ResolveError`. **SMTP short-circuit: marks working, appends to `available[SMTP]`, sets `ev[SMTP]`, returns without any network probe.** Otherwise GETs `self.url` over a **force_close TCPConnector** with a marker-bearing User-Agent, `allow_redirects=False`; on `TimeoutError`/`ClientOSError`/`ClientResponseError`/`ServerDisconnectedError` logs debug and returns None (leaving `is_working` False). On success lowercases the body, extracts all IPs via `get_all_ip`, canonicalizes the real IPs, and **if status==200 AND any real IP is visible AND the random marker `rv` is echoed**, MUTATES `self.marks['via']`/`['proxy']` to `page.count('via')`/`count('proxy')`, sets `is_working=True`, **appends self to the class-global `available[scheme]` and sets the class-global `ev[scheme]`**; else logs the failure with the full response body. Returns None in every path.<br>**→ Rust:** `async fn check(&mut self, real_ext_ips: &HashSet<IpAddr>, registry: &JudgeRegistry) -> Result<(), JudgeError>` — **drop the legacy `real_ext_ip` param** (or model both via a `RealIps` enum / builder) rather than replicating the runtime `isinstance(str)` sniffing, which is pure duck-typing defence. **CRITICAL: `resp.status` is read at line 137 AFTER the `async with` closed the response — in reqwest the `Response` is MOVED by `.text()`, so capture `let status = resp.status();` BEFORE awaiting the body.** The `page = False` init at line 105 is vestigial — do not port the falsy sentinel; use the Result type. Store real IPs as `IpAddr` end-to-end so canonicalization is structural, not a `canonicalize_ip(ip) or ip` string fallback. Errors: `thiserror` enum `JudgeError { Resolve, Timeout, Transport(reqwest::Error) }`; the four aiohttp arms all collapse into `reqwest::Error` + `is_timeout()`. |
| `get_judges` | function | `get_judges(judges=None, timeout=8, verify_ssl=False)`. Factory: **falls back to a hardcoded 10-URL default list when `judges` is None OR empty/falsy** (so `get_judges([])` silently returns the 10 defaults — confirmed intentional by `tests/test_checker.py`). For each entry, passes Judge instances through untouched and wraps plain strings in `Judge(j)`; **then MUTATES `j.timeout` and `j.verify_ssl` on every element — including on caller-owned Judge objects passed in.** Returns a new list. No I/O, no network. **Only caller: `Checker.__init__`.**<br>**→ Rust:** `fn get_judges(judges: Option<Vec<JudgeSpec>>, timeout: Duration, verify_ssl: bool) -> Result<Vec<Judge>, JudgeError>`, where `enum JudgeSpec { Url(String), Built(Judge) }` replaces the `isinstance` duck-typing. **The post-construction field mutation of caller-supplied Judges is a hidden side effect** — fold timeout/verify_ssl into a `JudgeBuilder` so they're set at construction. `judges or [...]` conflates None and empty-vec: **preserve deliberately (tests depend on it)** but make it explicit via `.filter(|v| !v.is_empty()).unwrap_or_else(default_judges)`. **The default list is built fresh per call, so this is NOT the classic mutable-default-arg bug** — it becomes a `const`/`LazyLock<Vec<&str>>`. |

**Private symbols**

<details>
<summary>None — judge.py has no private symbols; all state is class-level (see <code>available</code>/<code>ev</code> above), which is precisely the problem.</summary>

The module exposes everything publicly. The two class attributes `Judge.available` and `Judge.ev` are documented in the public table because they are read and mutated directly by `checker.py`, making them de-facto public API despite looking like internals.

</details>

**Internal deps:** `proxybroker.errors` (`ResolveError`), `proxybroker.resolver` (`Resolver`), `proxybroker.utils` (`canonicalize_ip`, `get_all_ip`, `get_headers`, `log`).

**External deps:** `asyncio` (`Event`, `TimeoutError`), `secrets` (`choice`), `urllib.parse` (`urlparse`), `aiohttp` (`TCPConnector`, `ClientSession`, `ClientTimeout`, `ClientOSError`, `ClientResponseError`, `ServerDisconnectedError`).

**Port risks**

- **Class-level mutable global state:** `Judge.available` (dict of lists) and `Judge.ev` (dict of asyncio.Event) are class attributes mutated in-place from instance methods (`self.available[self.scheme].append(self)` **resolves through the instance to the CLASS dict**), from classmethods, and directly from checker.py. **Every Judge, Checker, and Broker in the process shares one registry — `Checker.__init__` calls `Judge.clear()`, so a second Checker wipes the first's verified judges.** Rust has no equivalent of a mutable class attribute reachable via `self`. Modelling it literally requires a `static` with interior mutability, which **imports the exact aliasing/test-pollution bug the Python has** and makes `check(&self)` need `&mut` access to shared state across tasks. **Approach:** do NOT port as `static`. Introduce `struct JudgeRegistry { available: Mutex<HashMap<JudgeScheme, Vec<Arc<Judge>>>>, ready: HashMap<JudgeScheme, watch::Sender<bool>> }`, construct one per `Checker`, thread `Arc<JudgeRegistry>` into `Judge::check`. Use `parking_lot::Mutex` for the Vec (short non-async critical section) or `tokio::sync::Mutex` if held across an await. If a global is genuinely required for API-compat, `std::sync::LazyLock<Arc<JudgeRegistry>>` — **but the per-Checker registry is strictly better and fixes the clobbering.**
- **`asyncio.Event` set-once latch awaited by many tasks** (`await Judge.ev["HTTP"].wait()` in `checker.py:173-177`), constructed at class-definition/import time with no running loop, and reset via `.clear()`. **asyncio.Event is level-triggered: a waiter arriving AFTER `set()` returns immediately.** The obvious Rust reach — `tokio::sync::Notify` — **is edge-triggered: `notified()` registered after `notify_waiters()` blocks forever, silently deadlocking every proxy check that starts after the judges verified.** Import-time construction is also loop-affine on Python <3.10. **Approach:** `tokio::sync::watch::channel(false)` per scheme: `set()` → `send(true)`, `clear()` → `send(false)`, `wait()` → `rx.wait_for(|v| *v).await`, **which returns immediately when already true.** Correct level-triggered semantics and multi-waiter broadcast. Alternative: `tokio::sync::Semaphore` with `add_permits`, but `watch` matches clear/reset cleanly.
- **`resp` is read after its `async with` block exits:** `page = await resp.text()` inside the block, then `resp.status` at line 137 outside it. Python leaves the name bound in function scope and aiohttp keeps `.status` readable on a released response. **In reqwest, `Response::text(self)` takes `self` by value — the response is MOVED and cannot be touched afterwards.** A literal transliteration will not compile, and a careless fix (reading status inside the block only on some paths) changes which failures get logged with a status. **Approach:** capture eagerly before consuming the body: `let status = resp.status(); let page = resp.text().await?;`. **This is the borrow checker surfacing a genuine Python smell (reading a released aiohttp response), not an obstacle — the fix is strictly clearer.**
- **Duck-typed / overloaded parameters.** `check(real_ext_ips=None, real_ext_ip=None)` accepts a set, an iterable, a bare str (**defensively re-wrapped so it isn't iterated into characters**), or the legacy single-IP kwarg — with a documented precedence rule. `get_judges` accepts a list mixing `str` and `Judge` and dispatches on `isinstance`. `tests/utils.py` additionally passes `MagicMock(spec=Judge)` duck-typed stand-ins. Rust has no runtime type sniffing, no optional-kwarg overloading, no structural mock substitution. **The `isinstance(real_ext_ips, str)` guard exists purely because Python silently iterates strings into chars — a failure mode that cannot occur in Rust.** **Approach:** replace the `check()` overload with a single `&HashSet<IpAddr>` (callers normalize; **the whole shim is what the line-82 TODO asks to delete**) or an explicit `enum RealIps { One(IpAddr), Many(HashSet<IpAddr>) }`. Replace `get_judges`' isinstance dispatch with `enum JudgeSpec { Url(String), Built(Judge) }` or a blanket `impl TryFrom<T> for Judge`. Replace `MagicMock(spec=Judge)` with a `trait JudgeLike` (or `#[cfg_attr(test, automock)]` via `mockall`) so checker tests can inject fakes without runtime patching.
- **aiohttp-specific connector and exception internals:** `TCPConnector(loop=..., ssl=self.verify_ssl, force_close=True)`, `ClientTimeout(total=...)`, `allow_redirects=False`, a fresh `ClientSession` per check, and a **4-arm except catching `ClientOSError`/`ClientResponseError`/`ServerDisconnectedError`/`asyncio.TimeoutError` while letting generic `aiohttp.ClientError`, `UnicodeDecodeError`, and `CancelledError` propagate.** reqwest has no `force_close` (it pools by default), folds all transport failures into one opaque `reqwest::Error`, and applies redirect policy at client-build time, not per-request. **The aiohttp `ssl=False` argument means "do not verify" (not "no TLS") — a wrong mapping here silently disables or silently enables certificate verification.** `ssl=self.verify_ssl` also passes a bool into a param that accepts SSLContext/bool/Fingerprint, an overload with no Rust analogue. **Approach:** `reqwest::Client::builder().danger_accept_invalid_certs(!verify_ssl).redirect(redirect::Policy::none()).timeout(Duration).pool_max_idle_per_host(0)` — the last reproduces force_close. **Build ONE client per Checker and clone it** (`Client` is `Arc` inside) instead of a session per check. Discriminate errors with `e.is_timeout()`/`e.is_connect()`/`e.is_decode()` into a `thiserror` enum; **decide deliberately whether previously-propagating errors (generic ClientError, decode failures) should now be caught, since Rust's `?` will otherwise change the failure surface.** Note reqwest auto-decompresses gzip/deflate (feature-gated) matching the Accept-Encoding header sent.
- **Per-instance `Resolver` construction:** `__init__` builds `Resolver(loop=self._loop)` for every Judge, so **10 default judges create 10 independent resolvers with 10 separate DNS caches** — while Checker holds its own Resolver for `get_real_ext_ips()`. A literal port replicates the cache fragmentation and forces `Judge` to own a `Resolver`, complicating ownership (each Judge needs its own aiodns channel / socket state) and defeating any shared-cache design. It's cheap in Python only because Resolver construction is lazy. **Approach:** inject a shared `Arc<Resolver>` through the constructor (`Judge::new(url, resolver: Arc<Resolver>)`) or the `JudgeBuilder`. If the resolver is hickory's `TokioAsyncResolver`, it is internally `Arc`-shared and cheaply cloneable with a shared cache — one instance per Broker. **Flag this as a behavior change (shared DNS cache across judges) in the port notes, since it is a fix, not a transliteration.**
- **`self.path = url.split(self.host)[-1]`** — derives the request path by string-splitting the URL on the host substring and taking the LAST fragment. `self.scheme = urlparse(url).scheme.upper()` then keys the `available`/`ev` dicts by that **raw uppercased string.** The split is fragile in ways Rust's `Url` makes structurally impossible to reproduce: if the host substring recurs in the path or query (`http://azenv.net/?ref=azenv.net`) the last fragment is wrong, and **for `smtp://smtp.gmail.com` the path is `""` (empty, not `"/"`)** — `checker.py:289` feeds `judge.path` straight into the outgoing test request, so **the empty/wrong value is load-bearing.** The stringly-typed scheme also means an unexpected scheme (e.g. `ftp://`) produces a **KeyError on `available[scheme]` at runtime** rather than a parse-time rejection. **Approach:** `url::Url::parse` + `url.path()` / `url.query()`, and `enum JudgeScheme { Http, Https, Smtp }` with `FromStr` rejecting anything else at construction (`Judge::new -> Result`). **Verify against the real judge list whether any URL currently yields a path differing from `url.path()` — preserve bug-for-bug ONLY if a checker test depends on the empty-path SMTP case**; otherwise the typed version is correct and the KeyError becomes a compile-time-exhaustive match.
- **Marker/leak detection is substring- and count-based over a lowercased response body:** `rv in page`, `page.count("via")`, `page.count("proxy")`, plus `get_all_ip(page)` regex extraction and a `canonicalize_ip(ip) or ip` string fallback. **`page.count("via")` counts substring occurrences including inside unrelated words ("trivial", "via" in a URL)**, and the whole predicate is stringly-typed over an arbitrarily large body. **The `or ip` fallback silently keeps an uncanonicalizable string in a set that is then intersected with canonical forms — a comparison that can never match, failing open into a "judge failed" log rather than an error.** **Approach:** `page.matches("via").count()` reproduces the Python count exactly (both are non-overlapping substring counts) — **use it to stay bug-compatible, and flag the false-positive counting as a separate defect for the checker port, since `marks` feeds anonymity-level classification.** Keep real IPs as `HashSet<IpAddr>` from `Resolver` onward so canonicalization is structural and the `or ip` fallback disappears; parse extracted page IPs with `IpAddr::from_str` and intersect typed sets. Body regex extraction ports to `regex` + `once_cell`/`LazyLock`; the existing patterns are documented ReDoS-free and `regex` guarantees linear time regardless.

---

### proxybroker/checker.py

**Purpose:** Validates candidate proxies by negotiating each requested protocol through a randomly-selected judge, verifying the echoed test request, and classifying the resulting anonymity level (Transparent/Anonymous/High).

**LOC:** 390

**Tags:**

- **HOT:** 41 commits touching `checker.py` — **the highest-churn surface in the package**; expect the Rust port to keep absorbing changes, so **favor testable pure functions over one monolithic `check()`.**
- **COMPLEX:** no single function exceeds 80 lines (largest is `__init__` at ~50, `check_judges` at ~48), but **`Checker._check` and `Checker._check_conn_25` are deeply nested** — for-loop wrapping `try/except/else/finally` with **continue-on-timeout, break-on-protocol-error, and a `finally` that must run `proxy.close()` on every path including the loop-control paths.** Rust has no `else`/`finally`, so **this is the module's highest-risk restructuring.**
- **UNTESTED (partial):** `tests/test_checker.py` covers `Checker.__init__` (config/kwarg contracts), `_get_anonymity_lvl` (9 cases incl. dual-stack, uppercase/expanded IPv6, legacy-str) and `_check_test_response` (3 cases). **NO test references `Checker.check_judges`, `Checker.check`, `Checker._check`, `Checker._check_conn_25`, `Checker._in_DNSBL`, `Checker._types_passed`, `_request`, `_send_test_request`, `_decompress_content`, or `ProxyChecker`** — i.e. **every network path and every retry/error branch is unverified.** Port these behind a trait-mocked transport and write the tests the Python side never had.
- **STALE:** `ProxyChecker` has been deprecated since the rename recorded in `CHANGELOG.md:306/312`, is absent from `proxybroker/__init__.py` `__all__`, and has **zero callers anywhere in the repo** outside its own definition. Also dead: the `headers` parameter of `_check_test_response` is never read, and `Checker._real_ext_ip` (legacy single-string accessor) has no in-repo consumer.
- **TODO:** `checker.py:91` — literal `# TODO: need refactoring` on `Checker.check_judges`, which mixes judge health-checking, global protocol-flag mutation, event signalling, warning emission and error raising in one method.

**Public symbols**

| Name | Kind | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `Checker` | class | Stateful proxy checker holding judge list, retry/timeout config, requested type filter, DNSBL list and the host's real external IP set; **mutates global Judge class state via `Judge.clear()` at construction.**<br>**→ Rust:** `struct Checker { judges: Vec<Judge>, method: Method, max_tries: u32, real_ext_ips: HashSet<IpAddr>, strict: bool, dnsbl: Vec<String>, types: HashMap<Protocol, HashSet<AnonLvl>>, ngtrs: HashSet<Protocol>, resolver: Arc<Resolver>, req_http/https/smtp_proto: bool }`. **Do NOT reproduce `Judge.clear()` global reset — inject an `Arc<JudgePool>` so instances are isolated** (see port risks). |
| `Checker.__init__` | method | `__init__(self, judges, max_tries=3, timeout=8, verify_ssl=False, strict=False, dnsbl=None, real_ext_ip=None, types=None, post=False, loop=None, *, real_ext_ips=None)`. **Side effect: calls `Judge.clear()` wiping the GLOBAL `Judge.available` registry**, then builds judges via `get_judges(judges, timeout, verify_ssl)`; normalizes `real_ext_ip` (str) / `real_ext_ips` (iterable, **keyword-only, wins if both given**) into a frozenset, **defensively wrapping a bare str so it isn't iterated per-character**; sets legacy `self._real_ext_ip` to an arbitrary member; captures `asyncio.get_running_loop()` falling back to `self._loop = loop` (possibly None) on RuntimeError; constructs Resolver; derives `_req_http_proto`/`_req_https_proto`/`_req_smtp_proto` by **set-intersecting protocol tuples with `types.keys()`** (all True when types is falsy); `_ngtrs` = set of keys of types or `NGTRS`.<br>**→ Rust:** builder pattern (`CheckerBuilder`) — **11 params with 10 defaults and a keyword-only arg is exactly the builder case.** `real_ext_ip`/`real_ext_ips` collapse into one `.real_ext_ips(impl IntoIterator<Item = IpAddr>)`; the str-vs-iterable defensive branch vanishes under the type system. `timeout`/`verify_ssl` are pass-through to `get_judges` only — keep them on the judge builder, not on Checker. **Drop `loop` entirely.** |
| `Checker.check_judges` | async method | Concurrently checks all judges via `asyncio.gather` (**no `return_exceptions` — a single judge raising aborts the whole gather**); retains only `j.is_working`; for each of HTTP/HTTPS/SMTP with zero entries in the GLOBAL `Judge.available`, **disables the corresponding `_req_*_proto` flag, sets `Judge.ev[proto]` to unblock already-waiting `check()` coroutines**, and removes disabled protocols from `self._ngtrs`; emits a `UserWarning` naming missing judges; **raises `RuntimeError('Not found judges')` if no judge survives.** Carries an in-source `# TODO: need refactoring`. Mutates `self._judges`, `self._ngtrs`, `self._req_*_proto` and global Judge state.<br>**→ Rust:** `async fn check_judges(&mut self) -> Result<(), CheckerError>`. Use `futures::future::join_all` (**NOT `try_join_all`**) only if you intend to keep abort-on-first-error — **current `gather()` semantics propagate the first exception, so `try_join_all` is the faithful port.** Note **SMTP is disabled without being added to `nojudges`**, so the warning never names SMTP while still listing it in disable_protocols — preserve or fix deliberately. `warnings.warn` -> `tracing::warn!`. RuntimeError -> `CheckerError::NoJudges`. |
| `Checker.check` | async method | `async check(self, proxy)`. **Main entry point.** Returns False early (logging `'Found in DNSBL'`) if DNSBL configured and host listed. **Awaits the global `Judge.ev[HTTP/HTTPS/SMTP]` events for each required protocol (blocks indefinitely until `check_judges` sets them).** Intersects `proxy.expected_types` with `self._ngtrs` (falling back to all `_ngtrs`). **Sequentially (NOT concurrently)** checks each protocol, dispatching `CONNECT:25` to `_check_conn_25` and everything else to `_check`. Sets `proxy.is_working = any(results)` (mutation), returns True only if working AND `_types_passed(proxy)`.<br>**→ Rust:** `async fn check(&self, proxy: &mut Proxy) -> bool`. **The per-protocol loop is sequential and must stay so (each iteration reuses the single proxy connection).** `Judge.ev` waits -> `tokio::sync::watch::Receiver<bool>::wait_for(\|v\| *v)` — **sticky semantics are required** (see port risks). Prefer `Result<CheckOutcome, CheckError>` over bare bool so DNSBL-reject and all-protocols-failed are distinguishable. |
| `ProxyChecker` | class | `class ProxyChecker(Checker)`. **Deprecated backward-compat alias subclass**, adding no behavior. Not exported from `__init__.py`'s `__all__` and has zero in-repo callers.<br>**→ Rust:** omit from the port, or emit `#[deprecated(note = "use Checker")] pub type ProxyChecker = Checker;`. |
| `ProxyChecker.__init__` | method | Emits a `DeprecationWarning` (stacklevel=2) then forwards all args/kwargs unchanged to `Checker.__init__`.<br>**→ Rust:** no equivalent — Rust deprecation is compile-time via `#[deprecated]`, not a runtime warning. **If dropped entirely, note it as a breaking change in the port's migration notes.** |

**Private symbols**

<details>
<summary>3 private methods + 5 module-level functions</summary>

- **`Checker._types_passed`** — method. `(proxy)`. Returns True immediately if no type filter configured. Iterates **a copy of `proxy.types`**: in non-strict mode returns True on the first protocol whose level matches (or whose required-level set is empty); **in strict mode DELETES non-matching protocols from `proxy.types` (mutating the proxy)** and returns True only if any survive. On failure logs `'Protocol or the level of anonymity differs from the requested'` to the proxy and returns False.
  **→ Rust:** `fn types_passed(&self, proxy: &mut Proxy) -> bool` — **needs `&mut` because the strict path prunes `proxy.types`.** The `.copy()`-then-del dance becomes `proxy.types.retain(|proto, lvl| ...)` in the strict branch; the non-strict branch is a short-circuiting `.any()`. **Split the two modes into separate branches rather than one interleaved loop** — the Python loop conflates them and the paths never actually share the body.
- **`Checker._in_DNSBL`** — async method. `(host)`. Reverses the dotted host, appends each configured DNSBL domain, resolves all queries concurrently with `return_exceptions=True` (logging suppressed), and returns True if any response is **both truthy and not a ResolveError instance.** **Quirk: a non-ResolveError exception (OSError/timeout) is a truthy object and therefore counts as a DNSBL hit; an empty-but-successful resolve result is falsy and is discarded.** Returns False otherwise. Network I/O via Resolver.
  **→ Rust:** `async fn in_dnsbl(&self, host: &str) -> bool`. `join_all` -> `Vec<Result<Vec<IpAddr>, ResolveError>>`; faithful predicate is `.any(|r| match r { Ok(ips) => !ips.is_empty(), Err(ResolveError) => false, Err(_) => true })`. **The IPv4-only dotted-reverse breaks for IPv6 hosts — decide explicitly.** Consider `hickory-resolver`.
- **`Checker._check_conn_25`** — async method. `(proxy, proto)`. **SMTP-only path.** Picks a random judge, retries up to `_max_tries`: assigns `proxy.ngtr = proto` (string -> negotiator instance via setter), connects, negotiates to `judge.host`/`judge.ip`. **`ProxyTimeoutError` -> `continue` (retry); `ProxyConnError`/`ProxyRecvError`/`ProxySendError`/`ProxyEmptyRecvError`/`BadStatusError`/`BadResponseError` -> `break` (give up)**; success -> sets `proxy.types[ngtr.name] = None` (no anonymity level for SMTP), result True, break. **`proxy.close()` runs in `finally` on EVERY path including continue/break.** Returns bool. Network I/O; mutates `proxy.types`, `proxy.ngtr`.
  **→ Rust:** `async fn check_conn_25(&self, proxy: &mut Proxy, proto: Protocol) -> bool`. **No else/finally in Rust:** run the fallible body in an inner async block, bind the Result, call `proxy.close()` unconditionally, THEN match to decide continue/break — or use `scopeguard::defer`. Errors -> `matches!(e, CheckError::Timeout) => continue, _ => break`. The `None` level maps to `Option<AnonLvl>::None`, so `proxy.types` is `HashMap<Protocol, Option<AnonLvl>>`.
- **`Checker._check`** — async method. `(proxy, proto)`. **Non-SMTP path.** Same retry/connect/negotiate skeleton as `_check_conn_25`, then sends the test request via `_send_test_request`, decompresses the body, validates the echo via `_check_test_response`, and on success **computes the anonymity level via `_get_anonymity_lvl` only when `proxy.ngtr.check_anon_lvl` is set (True only for HttpNgtr) else None**, storing it in `proxy.types[ngtr.name]`. Same timeout->continue / protocol-error->break policy; `proxy.close()` in `finally` on every path. Returns bool.
  **→ Rust:** `async fn check(&self, proxy: &mut Proxy, proto: Protocol) -> bool`. **Deepest control flow in the module** (for + try/except/else/finally + nested if) — restructure per `_check_conn_25`'s note. **`check_anon_lvl`/`use_full_path` are per-negotiator constants: model as associated consts on a Negotiator trait or a match on the Protocol enum, not runtime attribute lookup.**
- **`_request`** — function. `(method, host, path, fullpath=False, data="")`. **Pure.** Builds a raw HTTP/1.1 request as bytes plus the random verification marker `rv` from `get_headers(rv=True)`; sets Host, `Connection: close`, Content-Length (as an int, stringified later by the f-string), and `Content-Type: application/octet-stream` for POST; uses an absolute URI as the path when `fullpath` is set. Returns `(bytes, rv_str)`. No I/O.
  **→ Rust:** `fn build_request(method: Method, host: &str, path: &str, fullpath: bool, data: &[u8]) -> (Vec<u8>, String)`. **Hand-rolled serialization — do NOT swap in `http`/`hyper`: the byte layout and header ORDER are load-bearing because `_check_test_response` greps the echoed page for these exact strings.** Keep an `IndexMap` or a fixed-order array to preserve insertion order (Python dicts are ordered; HashMap is not).
- **`_send_test_request`** — async function. `(method, proxy, judge)`. Sends the built request through the proxy and reads the response. **Raises `BadStatusError` (the CLASS, not an instance)** if `get_status_code(resp) != 200`; raises `BadResponseError` from a `ValueError` when the response cannot be split on the `b'\r\n\r\n'` header/body boundary. **The `finally` block logs `'Get: success/failed'` to the proxy and debug-logs judge URL, rv and the full response on every path (including the raising ones) before the exception propagates.** Returns `(headers_bytes, content_bytes, rv)`. Network I/O.
  **→ Rust:** `async fn send_test_request(method, proxy: &mut Proxy, judge: &Judge) -> Result<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>, String), CheckError>`. `resp.split(b'\r\n\r\n', maxsplit=1)` + starred unpack -> `resp.split_once(b"\r\n\r\n").ok_or(CheckError::BadResponse)?` — **with maxsplit=1 the `*_` is always empty, so the ValueError only ever fires on too-few values.** The finally-logs-then-reraises pattern **needs an explicit log call on both arms of the match** (or `scopeguard::defer`), since `?` skips any trailing code.
- **`_decompress_content`** — function. `(headers, content)`. **Pure.** Parses headers; if Content-Encoding is gzip/deflate, first de-chunks when Transfer-Encoding is chunked by taking a **naive stride-2 slice** of the `b'\r\n'` split (`content.split(b'\r\n')[1::2]`), then `zlib.decompress` with **`MAX_WBITS|32` (auto gzip/zlib detect)**, swallowing `zlib.error` into `b''`. **Always returns a str via `content.decode('utf-8', 'ignore')` — invalid bytes are DROPPED, not replaced.** No I/O.
  **→ Rust:** `fn decompress_content(headers: &[u8], content: &[u8]) -> String`. **Two faithfulness traps:** (1) flate2 has no `MAX_WBITS|32` auto-detect — sniff the `0x1f 0x8b` magic to pick `GzDecoder` vs `ZlibDecoder`, and map any decode error to an empty body; (2) **`String::from_utf8_lossy` inserts U+FFFD whereas Python's `errors='ignore'` DROPS the bytes** — use `encoding_rs` or a manual `std::str::from_utf8` loop that skips invalid sequences, **or the echo-marker greps in `_check_test_response` can diverge.**
- **`_check_test_response`** — function. `(proxy, headers, content, rv)`. **Pure except for `proxy.log` side effects.** Returns True only if all four hold: the `rv` marker is echoed in the body, `get_headers()['Referer']` is echoed, `get_headers()['Cookie']` is echoed, and `get_all_ip(content)` is non-empty. Logs `'Response: correct'` or a per-flag breakdown and returns False otherwise. **Note `headers` is accepted but never used.**
  **→ Rust:** `fn check_test_response(proxy: &Proxy, content: &str, rv: &str) -> bool` — **drop the dead `headers` param.** Depends on `get_headers()` being PURE for Referer/Cookie (verified: only User-Agent varies, via rv); **if the Rust port randomizes any other header, this comparison silently breaks.** Hoist Referer/Cookie to `const`.
- **`_get_anonymity_lvl`** — function. `(real_ext_ips, proxy, judge, content)`. **Pure except for `proxy.log`.** Lowercases the body, extracts all IPs, and normalizes `real_ext_ips` accepting None (-> empty, Transparent disabled), a single str (legacy contract, canonicalized with fallback to the raw string), or an iterable of strings. Returns `'Transparent'` if ANY real ext-IP (v4 or v6) intersects the found IPs, else `'Anonymous'` if the body's `'via'`/`'proxy'` counts **exceed the judge's baseline `judge.marks`**, else `'High'`. Logs the verdict to the proxy.
  **→ Rust:** `fn anonymity_lvl(real_ext_ips: &HashSet<IpAddr>, proxy: &Proxy, judge: &Judge, content: &str) -> AnonLvl`. Model as `enum AnonLvl { Transparent, Anonymous, High }` (Display for the log's `lvl[:4]` truncation). The str-or-iterable polymorphism is a Python-only compat shim — collapse to one typed param. Parsing to `IpAddr` makes canonicalize_ip's normalization structural and free. **NB: `content.lower()` is applied before IP extraction, which is what makes uppercase IPv6 leaks match.**

</details>

**Internal deps:** `proxybroker.errors` (`BadResponseError`, `BadStatusError`, `ProxyConnError`, `ProxyEmptyRecvError`, `ProxyRecvError`, `ProxySendError`, `ProxyTimeoutError`, `ResolveError`), `proxybroker.judge` (`Judge`, `get_judges`), `proxybroker.negotiators` (`NGTRS`), `proxybroker.resolver` (`Resolver`), `proxybroker.utils` (`canonicalize_ip`, `get_all_ip`, `get_headers`, `get_status_code`, `log`, `parse_headers`).

**External deps:** `asyncio` (`gather`, `get_running_loop`, `Event` via `Judge.ev`), `time` (judge-check runtime measurement only), `warnings` (`UserWarning` for missing judges, `DeprecationWarning` for `ProxyChecker`), `zlib` (`decompress`, `MAX_WBITS`, `error`).

**Port risks**

- **`Judge.available` / `Judge.ev` are class-level mutable dicts acting as a global registry, and `Checker.__init__` calls `Judge.clear()` which wipes them for every other live Checker.** Python class attributes give free cross-instance shared mutable state. Rust has no equivalent: a `static` needs Mutex/OnceLock, which **reproduces the exact test-isolation bug (constructing a second Checker nukes the first one's judges)** and forces lock-ordering discipline across the whole check path. **Approach:** do not port the global. Introduce `struct JudgePool { available: HashMap<Protocol, Vec<Arc<Judge>>>, ready: HashMap<Protocol, watch::Sender<bool>> }` and inject `Arc<JudgePool>` into Checker. **Drop `Judge.clear()` entirely — pool lifetime replaces it.** Use `arc-swap` or `tokio::sync::RwLock` for the available lists.
- **asyncio.Event semantics in `Judge.ev`:** `check_judges` calls `.set()` specifically "for coroutines, which is already waiting", and `Checker.check` awaits events that **may have been set BEFORE the await is reached.** asyncio.Event is **sticky** — set() latches, so a later wait() returns immediately. **`tokio::sync::Notify` is NOT sticky** (notify_waiters drops the signal if nobody is parked yet), so a naive swap introduces a **rare deadlock where `check()` hangs forever on a protocol whose judges were already disabled.** This is a real missed-wakeup race, not a style issue. **Approach:** `tokio::sync::watch::channel(false)` per protocol and `rx.wait_for(|ready| *ready).await` — latched and multi-consumer, matching asyncio.Event exactly. `event-listener::Event` is also non-sticky; `tokio::sync::Semaphore::add_permits` with `acquire` is a workable alternative. **Never `Notify`.**
- **`proxy.ngtr = proto` assigns a STRING and the property setter instantiates a negotiator class looked up in the `NGTRS` dict at runtime**; the code then reads `proxy.ngtr.name` / `.check_anon_lvl` / `.use_full_path` / `.negotiate()` by duck typing. Runtime class registration plus dynamic dispatch through a string key — **the protocol set is open at runtime and there is no compile-time guarantee a key exists (a bad proto is a KeyError deep inside the retry loop).** **Approach:** `enum Protocol { Http, Https, Connect80, Connect25, Socks4, Socks5 }` (FromStr + Display for the wire/config boundary), with negotiation behind a `trait Negotiator { const CHECK_ANON_LVL: bool; const USE_FULL_PATH: bool; async fn negotiate(&mut self, host: &str, ip: IpAddr) -> Result<(), CheckError>; }`. Use `enum_dispatch` or a match returning `Box<dyn Negotiator>` to avoid the dyn-async-trait overhead; **the `NGTRS` dict becomes an exhaustive match the compiler checks.**
- **try/except/else/finally inside a retry for-loop, where `finally: proxy.close()` executes on the continue path, the break path, and the success path alike.** Rust has neither `else` nor `finally`, and **`?` early-returns past any trailing cleanup. A direct transliteration silently leaks the connection on the error paths — the exact class of bug that makes a proxy checker report false negatives under load.** **Approach:** `for _ in 0..max_tries { let r: Result<_, CheckError> = async { ... }.await; proxy.close(); match r { Err(e) if e.is_timeout() => continue, Err(_) => break, Ok(v) => { ...; break } } }`. **Bind the Result before cleanup so `?` can't skip it.** `scopeguard::defer!` is the alternative if the body needs `?` internally; prefer the explicit form here since the cleanup is one call.
- **`err = BadStatusError; raise err` raises the exception CLASS rather than an instance**, and the `finally` block reads that same `err` binding for logging before the exception propagates. Raising a class (Python auto-instantiates it) combined with a finally-block that inspects a local set by the try-block is a **control-flow/data-flow entanglement with no Rust analogue**; error identity is also used for the continue-vs-break policy in the callers. **Approach:** `#[derive(thiserror::Error)] enum CheckError { Timeout, Conn, Recv, Send, EmptyRecv, BadStatus(u16), BadResponse }` with an `is_timeout()` helper driving the retry match. **Log explicitly on both match arms instead of relying on `finally`;** `anyhow` only at the binary's edges.
- **`content.decode('utf-8', 'ignore')` in `_decompress_content` DROPS invalid bytes, and `zlib.decompress(content, zlib.MAX_WBITS | 32)` auto-detects gzip vs zlib framing.** Rust's idiomatic equivalents differ behaviorally on both counts: **`String::from_utf8_lossy` INSERTS U+FFFD replacement characters (changing byte offsets and potentially breaking the rv/Referer/Cookie substring greps that decide whether a proxy passes)**, and flate2 has no auto-detect wbits mode. **Approach:** decode with `encoding_rs` (UTF-8 decoder configured to skip, not replace) or a manual `std::str::from_utf8` chunk loop that discards invalid sequences. For decompression, **sniff the `0x1f 0x8b` gzip magic** and pick `flate2::read::GzDecoder`, else `ZlibDecoder`, mapping any error to an empty body to mirror the `except zlib.error: content = b''` swallow.
- **Chunked-body handling is `content.split(b'\r\n')[1::2]`** — a naive stride-2 slice, not a chunked-transfer parser, **and it only runs when the body is ALSO compressed.** The Python code is subtly wrong (it corrupts any chunk whose payload contains `\r\n`, and never de-chunks uncompressed chunked bodies). **A "correct" Rust port using a real parser changes which proxies pass the check — a silent behavioral divergence that will look like a port regression in the field.** **Approach:** decide explicitly and document it. *Faithful:* replicate the stride-2 slice. *Correct:* use `httparse` or hyper's chunked decoder and de-chunk regardless of Content-Encoding. **Recommend shipping the faithful version first, gated behind a `strict_chunked` feature flag, so the port can be diffed against Python 1:1 before fixing.**
- **`_in_DNSBL`'s `any([r for r in responses if not isinstance(r, ResolveError)])` conflates exception objects with successful results under Python truthiness.** Duck-typed truthiness means **a non-ResolveError exception (OSError, timeout) is a truthy object and therefore votes "proxy is blacklisted", while a successful-but-empty resolve result is falsy and is silently discarded.** Rust's Result forces this ambiguity to be resolved at the type level, so **the porter must consciously choose a semantic.** **Approach:** `join_all` -> `Vec<Result<Vec<IpAddr>, ResolveError>>`, then `.iter().any(|r| match r { Ok(ips) => !ips.is_empty(), Err(ResolveError::NotFound) => false, Err(_) => true })` to preserve current behavior — **and add a comment that treating transport errors as a DNSBL hit is inherited, likely unintended.** `hickory-resolver` for the resolver itself.
- **`_check_test_response` re-derives its expected values by calling `get_headers()` a second time instead of receiving what `_request` actually sent.** **An implicit purity contract across module boundaries:** it works today only because `get_headers()` randomizes User-Agent alone (via rv) and returns constant Referer/Cookie. **Any future randomization of those headers breaks proxy validation silently, with no test catching it** (no test covers `_request` or `_send_test_request`). **Approach:** make the coupling explicit — have `build_request` return a `TestMarkers { rv: String, referer: &'static str, cookie: &'static str }` and thread it into `check_test_response`. Hoist the constant headers to `const REFERER: &str` / `const COOKIE: &str`. **This removes the hidden invariant entirely rather than documenting it.**
- **Ordered-dict dependence in `_request`:** headers are serialized in insertion order and **the resulting byte layout is what the echo-greps later search for.** Python dicts preserve insertion order as a language guarantee; **Rust's HashMap explicitly randomizes iteration order per process (SipHash with a random seed), so a HashMap-based port produces a different request on every run.** **Approach:** use `indexmap::IndexMap`, a `Vec<(&str, String)>`, or a fixed const array of header names. **Avoid the `http::HeaderMap`/hyper serializer — it normalizes casing and ordering, and this code hand-builds the raw request deliberately.**
- **`asyncio.get_running_loop()` captured in `__init__` with a RuntimeError fallback that leaves `self._loop = None`**, plus a `loop` parameter threaded to Resolver. Explicit event-loop handles are an asyncio-ism with no tokio counterpart; a half-initialized `_loop = None` object is a latent AttributeError the type system should eliminate rather than reproduce. **Approach:** delete the concept. Tokio resolves the runtime ambiently at await points; if a handle is genuinely needed use `tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()`. **Removing `loop` from the public constructor is a breaking API change — record it in the port's migration notes alongside the `ProxyChecker` removal.**
- **`Checker.check` mutates the proxy it is handed** (`proxy.is_working`, `proxy.types`, `proxy.ngtr`) **while `_types_passed` concurrently deletes entries from `proxy.types` during iteration of a `.copy()`.** Python's shared-object aliasing lets `check()`, `_check()` and `_types_passed()` all mutate one Proxy freely with no ownership discipline; **the `.copy()` exists purely to dodge a mutation-during-iteration RuntimeError.** Rust's borrow checker rejects the whole shape, and the Broker layer likely wants these proxies across tasks. **Approach:** thread `&mut Proxy` through `check`/`_check`/`_check_conn_25`/`_types_passed` (single owner, no aliasing — **the `.copy()` disappears in favor of `HashMap::retain`**). If the Broker needs concurrent access, use `Arc<Mutex<Proxy>>` at the Broker boundary and **lock once per `check()` call, not per mutation**, to keep the check path free of interleaving.

---

### proxybroker/providers.py

**Purpose:** Defines the `Provider` scraper base class (HTTP fetch → regex/JS-deobfuscation → (host, port) extraction), 28 site-specific subclasses, and the module-level `PROVIDERS` registry of pre-instantiated providers that `api.Broker` fans out over to harvest free public proxy lists.

**LOC:** 806

**Tags:**

- **HOT:** **94 name-only entries** in `git log -- proxybroker/providers.py` out of 662 total repo commits — **the single most-churned module in the package** (api.py 83, resolver.py 83, proxy.py 73, server.py 67, utils.py 53, provider_utils.py 27). Churn is inherent: every provider site that changes its markup forces an edit here.
- **COMPLEX:** strictly, **NO function exceeds 80 lines** (longest is `Provider._get` at 27, then `Aliveproxy_com._pipe` 21, `Gatherproxy_com._pipe` 19, `Provider.__init__` 19, `Provider.find_proxies` 18 — 48 functions total, measured via AST). **Tag applied on the density/nesting axis instead:** `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.find_proxies` is a 4-stage deobfuscator (sqrt→banker's-round→per-index XOR over code points→unquote→html-unescape→regex), and `Spys_ru.find_proxies` **emulates a JS XOR-variable expression evaluator via a `re.sub` callback reading order-dependent mutable class state.** Also the **112-line `PROVIDERS` literal (lines 695-806)** is the largest single top-level symbol.
- **STALE (heavy):** (1) **5 provider classes are fully defined but COMMENTED OUT of `PROVIDERS`** — `Proxyb_net`, `Proxz_com`, `Proxynova_com`, `_50kproxies_com`, `Free_proxy_cz`. (2) ~20 more lines of commented-out dead-site entries (proxydb.net, cybersyndrome, ip-adress.com, marcosbl, go4free, blackstarsecurity, get-proxy.net). (3) Large commented-out logic blocks: `Free_proxy_cz._pipe` lines 596-619 (abandoned rate-limit batching), `Gatherproxy_com.find_proxies` lines 360-365 (abandoned gp.dep branch), `Spys_ru._pipe` lines 563-567 (abandoned country crawl), `Aliveproxy_com` line 283, `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base` lines 402-403. (4) **`Blogspot_com_base._cookies={'NCR':1}` is unreachable dead code** — shadowed by `self._cookies={}` in `Provider.__init__`. (5) **`Provider._loop` is assigned and never read.** (6) Many registered domains are long defunct (gatherproxy.com ~2018, freeproxylists.com, xseo.in, nntime.com, aliveproxy.com, foxtools.ru, maxiproxies.com). (7) `Proxylist_me._pipe` carries an inline 'Pre-existing master bug' comment.
- **UNTESTED:** `tests/test_providers.py` covers **ONLY the `Provider` base class** (construction, the proxies setter, `find_proxies` incl. the IPv6 masking cases, and `_find_on_pages([])`). Grep of `tests/` finds **ZERO references to any of the 28 concrete subclasses, to `PROVIDERS`, or to `ProxyProvider`.** Specifically untested: every `_pipe` override, every `find_proxies` override, every `char_js_port_to_num`, all base64/hex/XOR decode paths, `Provider.get` retry logic, `Provider._get` error handling, and `get_proxies` session lifecycle. The only other find_proxies test (`tests/test_custom_provider_examples.py`) exercises `examples/`, not this module.
- **TODO:** no literal TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK markers. **Tag applied for the equivalent recorded-but-unfixed work:** the 'Pre-existing master bug' note at line 338, the `# # Bad...` disabled-provider block at line 785, and the abandoned commented implementations listed under STALE — **each is an unresolved decision the port must explicitly accept or discard.**

**Public symbols — base class**

| Name | Kind | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `Provider` | class | **Template-method base for all scrapers**; holds url/domain/proto/session/cookies/`_proxies` set and a per-provider `asyncio.Semaphore`; subclasses override `_pipe`, `find_proxies`, and/or the `_pattern` class attribute. **`domain` is set in `__init__` only when url is truthy**, otherwise subclasses supply it as a class attr (a bare `Provider()` has NO `domain` and `get_proxies` would raise AttributeError on its first log line).<br>**→ Rust:** `trait Provider: Send + Sync` with `#[async_trait]`; default `pipe()`/`find_proxies()` impls. **Shared fields → a `ProviderCore` struct each impl owns (composition, not inheritance, since Rust has no attribute inheritance).** Registry as `Vec<Box<dyn Provider>>`. |
| `Provider.__init__` | method | `(url=None, proto=(), max_conn=4, max_tries=3, timeout=20, loop=None)`. Sets `self.domain = urlparse(url).netloc` only if url truthy; stores url/proto/`_max_tries`/`_timeout`; inits `_session=None`, **`_cookies={}` (this instance attr SHADOWS and kills `Blogspot_com_base._cookies={'NCR':1}`)**, `_proxies=set()`; constructs `asyncio.Semaphore(max_conn)`; resolves `self._loop = loop or asyncio.get_running_loop()`, swallowing RuntimeError to None. **`self._loop` is assigned and then NEVER read anywhere in the module — dead field.** Because `PROVIDERS` is built at import time (no running loop), `_loop` is always None for bundled providers.<br>**→ Rust:** builder pattern — 6 params, 5 optional. **`tokio::sync::Semaphore` has no runtime affinity, so the get_running_loop/RuntimeError dance disappears entirely. Drop `_loop`.** `proto` → `Vec<Proto>` enum, not stringly-typed. |
| `Provider.proxies` | property | Returns the internal `_proxies` set **directly (no copy)** — callers can mutate provider state through the returned reference. Elements are `(host, port, proto)` tuples.<br>**→ Rust:** `fn proxies(&self) -> &HashSet<(String, String, Vec<Proto>)>`. The tuple is a nominal type — prefer `struct ProxyEntry { host, port, proto }` with Hash+Eq. Returning `&` reproduces the no-copy semantics safely. |
| `Provider.proxies` (setter) | method | **MISLEADING NAME: assignment APPENDS, never replaces.** Rebuilds each item as `(host, port, self.proto)`, **silently DROPS entries whose port is falsy** (`''`/None/0), then `self._proxies.update(new)` for set dedup. Unpacks `for host, port in new` — **so any 2-element iterable works** (`Proxy_list_org` feeds it 2-element *lists*). Mutates `_proxies`; returns nothing; **raises ValueError if any element does not unpack to exactly 2.**<br>**→ Rust:** Rust has no property setters and `x = y` meaning `x.extend(y)` is a footgun. Expose `fn add_proxies(&mut self, new: impl IntoIterator<Item = (String, String)>)`. Filter empty ports with `.filter(\|(_, p)\| !p.is_empty())`. The "any 2-element iterable" duck typing collapses to the `(String, String)` tuple type. |
| `Provider.get_proxies` | async method | **Public entry point.** Logs debug, opens `aiohttp.ClientSession(headers=get_headers(), cookies=self._cookies)` bound to `self._session` via `async with ... as self._session`, awaits `self._pipe()`, closes the session on exit (**leaving `self._session` as a CLOSED session object, not None**), logs count, returns `self.proxies`. **Does NOT reset `_proxies` — repeat calls accumulate.** Exceptions raised by `_pipe` **propagate to the caller** (api.py); only `find_proxies` errors are swallowed downstream.<br>**→ Rust:** `async fn get_proxies(&mut self) -> Result<&HashSet<ProxyEntry>, ProviderError>`. Build a `reqwest::Client` (with default headers + `Arc<Jar>` cookie store) per call, or better hoist a shared client into the broker — reqwest clients are pooled and cheaply cloneable, unlike ClientSession. |
| `Provider.get` | async method | `(url, data=None, headers=None, method="GET")`. Retry wrapper: loops up to `self._max_tries`, calling `self._get`, breaking on the first truthy page. Returns the last page (possibly `''`). **No backoff/sleep between attempts. LATENT BUG: `page` is only bound inside the loop, so `max_tries=0` raises UnboundLocalError.**<br>**→ Rust:** `async fn get(&self, req: &PageRequest) -> String` returning empty string on total failure (preserve the `''` sentinel, or better `Option<String>`). Rust's scoping makes the UnboundLocalError impossible — init `let mut page = String::new()` before the loop. Consider `backoff` crate if adding sleep, **but note the original has none.** |
| `Provider.find_proxies` | method | **Public parse hook (18 lines).** Masks every `[v6]:port` span with **equal-length spaces** BEFORE the IPv4 pass so `[::ffff:192.0.2.1]:8080` cannot also emit a phantom `192.0.2.1:8080`; runs `self._find_proxies(masked)`; then appends `(canonicalize_ip(raw_v6), port)` for each bracketed v6 match, dropping any that fail canonicalization (None). Returns `list[tuple[str,str]]`. Pure — no I/O, no mutation. **Overridden by 8 subclasses which BYPASS the IPv6 path entirely.**<br>**→ Rust:** `fn find_proxies(&self, page: &str) -> Result<Vec<(String,String)>, ParseError>` as a default trait method. Equal-length space masking must be byte-safe — use `Regex::replace_all` with a closure emitting `" ".repeat(m.as_str().len())`, keep everything in `&str`/char boundaries. `canonicalize_ip` → `std::net::IpAddr::from_str(s).ok().map(\|a\| a.to_string())`. |
| `ProxyProvider` | class | Deprecated backwards-compat alias for `Provider`. Not in `__init__.__all__` but importable and part of the historical public API.<br>**→ Rust:** `#[deprecated(since = "...", note = "use `Provider` instead")] pub type ProxyProvider = Provider;` — compile-time warning, no runtime emission and no stacklevel analog. |
| `ProxyProvider.__init__` | method | Emits `DeprecationWarning` via `warnings.warn(stacklevel=2)` then delegates to `super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)`. Side effect: a runtime warning (which `__init__.py` configures as `simplefilter('once', DeprecationWarning)`).<br>**→ Rust:** no runtime-warning equivalent. `*args/**kwargs` passthrough has no analog either — with a builder, the deprecated alias just re-exports the builder. If runtime parity is required, `log::warn!` once via `std::sync::Once`. |
| `PROVIDERS` | constant | `list[Provider]` — **112 lines (695-806).** Module-level list of **38 ALREADY-INSTANTIATED provider objects, built at IMPORT time**: 18 plain `Provider(url=..., proto=...)` entries plus 20 subclass instances. Imported by api.py, which does `list(PROVIDERS)` — **a shallow copy, so every Broker shares the SAME mutable provider instances** (accumulated `_proxies` sets and closed `_session`s leak across Broker lifetimes). ~20 further lines of commented-out 'Bad...' providers document dead sites plus the 5 defined-but-disabled classes. **BUG: the two proxyscrape socks entries pass `proto=("SOCKS4")` and `proto=("SOCKS5")` — parens WITHOUT a trailing comma, so these are STRINGS, not tuples**; the proxies setter stores the bare string as the proto field and any downstream iteration over it yields individual CHARACTERS ('S','O','C','K','S','4').<br>**→ Rust:** **Do NOT port as a global.** Import-time instantiation of I/O-stateful objects + shared-mutable-singleton semantics is un-expressible without `Mutex`/`OnceLock` and is a bug anyway. Use `pub fn default_providers() -> Vec<Box<dyn Provider>>` returning **FRESH instances per call** (kills the cross-Broker leak). **The `("SOCKS4")`-is-a-string bug becomes a compile error in Rust — `Vec<Proto>` forces the fix; note the behavior change in the port log.** Consider driving the 18 plain entries from a static TOML/RON table instead of code. |

**Public symbols — the 28 concrete provider classes**

| Name | Kind | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `Freeproxylists_com` | class | `domain = 'freeproxylists.com'`. Two-stage scraper: index pages → timestamped `load_{type}_{uts}.html` endpoints. **Site is defunct.**<br>**→ Rust:** unit struct + `ProviderCore`; impl Provider with only `pipe` overridden. |
| `Freeproxylists_com._pipe` | async method | gathers 3 index pages (socks/elite/anonymous .html), **concatenates them**, regex-extracts (type, 10-digit-uts) pairs, formats `http://www.freeproxylists.com/load_{t}_{uts}.html`, then `_find_on_pages`. Empty regex result → empty urls → `_find_on_pages` no-ops.<br>**→ Rust:** `join_all` over 3 gets, `concat()`, `fancy-regex` captures_iter. Straightforward. |
| `Blogspot_com_base` | class | Shared blogspot crawler. Declares `_cookies = {'NCR': 1}` (force no-country-redirect) — **DEAD CODE: `Provider.__init__` unconditionally sets `self._cookies = {}`, shadowing it, so the NCR cookie is never sent.** Expects subclasses to define a `domains` list.<br>**→ Rust:** base-class attribute shadowed by instance init is a Python-only hazard; **make cookies an explicit builder field so the bug cannot recur silently. Decide whether to fix (send NCR) or preserve.** |
| `Blogspot_com_base._pipe` | async method | gathers `http://{d}/` for every d in `self.domains` (**attribute defined only on subclasses — AttributeError if instantiated directly**), regex-extracts dated post links (`.../YYYY/MM/...`), `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** `domains` becomes a required trait method `fn domains(&self) -> &[&str]` — **makes the "abstract base" explicit at compile time instead of failing at runtime.** |
| `Blogspot_com` | class | `domain='blogspot.com'`; domains = sslproxies24, proxyserverlist-24, freeschoolproxy, googleproxies24 (.blogspot.com). Registered with HTTP/CONNECT:80/HTTPS/CONNECT:25.<br>**→ Rust:** const `&[&str]` for domains. |
| `Blogspot_com_socks` | class | `domain='blogspot.com^socks'` (**the `^socks` suffix is a synthetic key so two providers can share a real domain without colliding in Broker's unique-provider dict**); domains = `['www.socks24.org']`. Registered with SOCKS4/SOCKS5.<br>**→ Rust:** the `^socks` sentinel-in-a-string is a smell — model as `struct ProviderId { domain: String, variant: Option<Variant> }` or a newtype key with Hash/Eq. |
| `Webanetlabs_net` | class | `domain='webanetlabs.net'`. Index → `proxylist_at_*` paths.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Webanetlabs_net._pipe` | async method | GETs `https://webanetlabs.net/publ/24`, regex-extracts hrefs containing `proxylist_at_`, prefixes host, `_find_on_pages`. Empty page → no urls → no-op.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Checkerproxy_net` | class | `domain='checkerproxy.net'`. **Registered in PROVIDERS with NO proto (defaults to `()`), so every proxy it yields carries an empty protocol tuple.**<br>**→ Rust:** empty proto tuple is meaningful downstream — keep `Vec<Proto>` and let it be empty rather than defaulting to all. |
| `Checkerproxy_net._pipe` | async method | GETs `https://checkerproxy.net/`, regex-extracts `/archive/YYYY-MM-DD` hrefs, **rewrites them to `/api/archive/...` JSON endpoints**, `_find_on_pages`. Parsed by the default IPv4 regex over raw JSON.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial; **note it regexes JSON rather than parsing it — preserve, don't "improve" to serde_json** (the API shape is unverified/dead). |
| `Proxz_com` | class | `domain='proxz.com'`. **COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS** (dead registration, ~443 proxies historically).<br>**→ Rust:** port but gate behind a feature flag / disabled-by-default list. |
| `Proxz_com.find_proxies` | method | URL-decodes the page then runs `self._find_proxies` — **overriding the base means it SKIPS all IPv6 bracketed extraction and the v6 masking.** Pure.<br>**→ Rust:** `percent_encoding::percent_decode_str(page).decode_utf8_lossy()` (Python's `unquote` defaults to utf-8/errors='replace' — lossy matches). |
| `Proxz_com._pipe` | async method | GETs `proxy_list_high_anonymous_0.html`, regex-extracts sibling `proxy_list_high_anonymous_*` hrefs, **appends the seed url**, `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Proxy_list_org` | class | `domain='proxy-list.org'`. **ACTIVE** in PROVIDERS (HTTP/CONNECT:80/HTTPS/CONNECT:25). Base64-obfuscated host:port inside `Proxy('...')` JS calls.<br>**→ Rust:** needs the **single-group findall shape (`Vec<String>`, not `Vec<(String,String)>`).** |
| `Proxy_list_org._pattern` | constant | `re.compile(r"""Proxy\('([\w=]+)'\)""")`. **SINGLE capture group → `_find_proxies` returns `list[str]` here, not `list[tuple]`.** Class-attr override of `Provider._pattern`.<br>**→ Rust:** no lookahead — plain `regex` is fine. **But this is the concrete case proving `_find_proxies` needs two typed variants in Rust.** |
| `Proxy_list_org.find_proxies` | method | `[b64decode(hp).decode().split(':') for hp in self._find_proxies(page)]` — **returns `list[LIST[str]]`, NOT `list[tuple]`**, relying on the setter's duck-typed 2-unpack. Raises `binascii.Error` on bad b64, `UnicodeDecodeError` on non-utf8 payload, and **ValueError in the SETTER if a decoded payload has ≠1 colon — the setter runs OUTSIDE the try, so a 3-part split raises ValueError that ESCAPES to `_pipe`.** Skips IPv6 handling.<br>**→ Rust:** `base64` crate with a **lenient engine** (Python's `b64decode` default `validate=False` DISCARDS non-alphabet chars). `split_once(':').ok_or(ParseError::Shape)?` — makes the escaping-ValueError path explicit instead of latent. |
| `Proxy_list_org._pipe` | async method | GETs `english/index.php?p=1`, regex-extracts `./index.php?p=N` hrefs, prefixes, appends seed, `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Aliveproxy_com` | class | `domain='aliveproxy.com'`. Registered with **NO proto** (empty tuple).<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Aliveproxy_com._pipe` | async method | **No discovery** — hardcodes 16 list paths (socks5-list, high-anonymity/anonymous/fastest, 6 country lists, 5 port-specific lists) and `_find_on_pages` them all concurrently. **21 lines, the longest `_pipe`.**<br>**→ Rust:** `const PATHS: &[&str]` + `join_all`. **Zero logic — pure data. Good candidate to fold into a declarative config table rather than code.** |
| `Maxiproxies_com` | class | `domain='maxiproxies.com'`. Registered with NO proto.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Maxiproxies_com._pipe` | async method | GETs `/category/proxy-lists/`, regex-extracts `<a href>` links containing the literal substring `'example'`, `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `_50kproxies_com` | class | `domain='50kproxies.com'`. **Leading underscore is NOT privacy — Python identifiers cannot start with a digit.** COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS (~822 proxies historically).<br>**→ Rust:** Rust has the same identifier rule — **name it `FiftyKProxiesCom` or `P50kProxiesCom`; do not carry the underscore.** |
| `_50kproxies_com._pipe` | async method | GETs `/category/proxy-list/`, regex-extracts `<a href>` links containing `'-proxy-list-'`, `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Proxylist_me` | class | `domain='proxylist.me'`. **ACTIVE** in PROVIDERS.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Proxylist_me._pipe` | async method | GETs `https://proxylist.me/`, regex-extracts `?page=N` ids, **`max()`es them → UNGUARDED `ValueError('max() arg is an empty sequence')` if the page is empty or the markup changed; this escapes `_pipe` (not covered by `_find_on_page`'s except).** Then fetches pages `1..lastId`. **Inline comment records that master's `range(lastId)` was a pre-existing bug** (hit `?page=0` → 404, never fetched the last page); this file fixes it to `range(1, lastId+1)`.<br>**→ Rust:** `.max().ok_or(ProviderError::NoPagination)?` — **the empty-max panic must become a Result. Keep the 1-indexed fix.** |
| `Foxtools_ru` | class | `domain='foxtools.ru'`. Registered with **`max_conn=1`** (the only rate-limited entry besides `My_proxy_com`'s `max_conn=2`).<br>**→ Rust:** `Semaphore::new(1)`; **preserve, the site 429s otherwise.** |
| `Foxtools_ru._pipe` | async method | No discovery — hardcodes `http://api.foxtools.ru/v2/Proxy.txt?page={1..5}` and `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial; `(1..6).map(...)`. |
| `Gatherproxy_com` | class | `domain='gatherproxy.com'`. **ACTIVE** in PROVIDERS. Hex-encoded ports embedded in JS. **Site has been defunct since ~2018.**<br>**→ Rust:** port for parity, but flag as dead-on-arrival. |
| `Gatherproxy_com._pattern_h` | constant | `re.compile(<ip>(?=.*?(<ip>\|'(?P<port>[\d\w]+)')), flags=re.DOTALL)`. **Two-group pattern with a lookahead alternation** pairing each IPv4 with the next quoted hex port. **Note it is `_pattern_h`, NOT `_pattern` — `_find_proxies` is bypassed; `find_proxies` calls `self._pattern_h.findall` directly.**<br>**→ Rust:** `(?=...)` lookahead → **`fancy-regex` mandatory.** The `[\d\w]+` port class admits non-hex chars, so `from_str_radix(16)` must return Option, not unwrap. |
| `Gatherproxy_com.find_proxies` | method | `[(host, str(int(port, 16))) for host, port in self._pattern_h.findall(page) if port]` — **hex→decimal port conversion**, dropping empty ports. Raises ValueError on non-hex port text (swallowed by `_find_on_page`). **6 lines of commented-out dead code above it show an abandoned gp.dep http-vs-socks branch.**<br>**→ Rust:** `u32::from_str_radix(port, 16).ok().map(\|p\| (host, p.to_string()))` + `filter_map`. **Delete the commented branch — it is not a spec.** |
| `Gatherproxy_com._pipe` | async method | **BUG: builds `urls` inside a `for t in ['anonymous','elite']` loop but REASSIGNS (not extends) it each iteration — the 'anonymous' page set is silently discarded, only 'elite' is ever fetched.** POSTs `{Type, PageIdx:1}` to `/proxylist/anonymity/`, `continue`s on empty page, **`max()`es `href="#N"` ids (unguarded ValueError on empty match)**, then POSTs pages `1..lastPageId` as dict-form entries.<br>**→ Rust:** the reassign-vs-extend bug vanishes with `let mut urls = Vec::new(); ... urls.extend(...)` — **decide explicitly whether to preserve the bug or fix it (fixing doubles the request count).** `.max().ok_or(..)?` for the empty case. |
| `Gatherproxy_com_socks` | class | `domain='gatherproxy.com^socks'`. Registered with SOCKS4/SOCKS5. **Extends `Provider` directly (NOT `Gatherproxy_com`), so it uses the default IPv4 `_pattern`, not the hex-port one.**<br>**→ Rust:** confirms composition-over-inheritance: it shares the domain-ish name but none of the parsing. |
| `Gatherproxy_com_socks._pipe` | async method | Single dict entry: POST `http://www.gatherproxy.com/sockslist/` with no body, via `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial; `PageRequest::Full { method: Method::POST, .. }`. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base` | class | `domain='tools.rosinstrument.com'`. Shared JS-deobfuscation base: **sqrt-derived XOR key + `hideTxt()` payload.**<br>**→ Rust:** **the XOR/charcode logic is the highest-risk parser in the file** — see port risks. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.sqrtPattern` | constant | `re.compile(r"""sqrt\((\d+)\)""")`. Extracts the integer whose square root becomes the XOR key.<br>**→ Rust:** plain `regex` — no lookaround. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.bodyPattern` | constant | `re.compile(r"""hideTxt\(\n*'(.*)'\);""")`. **Greedy `.*` (no DOTALL on this one)** capturing the obfuscated payload passed to `hideTxt()`.<br>**→ Rust:** greedy `.*` without DOTALL = single-line match in both Python and `regex` — **preserve the flag difference exactly (this pattern is NOT DOTALL while `_pattern` IS).** |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base._pattern` | constant | `re.compile(<domainOrIP>(?=.*?(<domain>\|<ip>\|(?P<port>\d{2,5}))), flags=re.DOTALL)`. Override accepting **hostnames OR IPv4 — the only provider pattern in the file that admits DNS names, not just IPs** — paired by lookahead to a 2-5 digit port.<br>**→ Rust:** `(?=...)` → `fancy-regex`. **Note downstream code assumes hosts are IPs elsewhere — a DNS name flowing out of here is a real type-model question for `ProxyEntry.host` (`String`, not `IpAddr`).** |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_base.find_proxies` | method | **4-stage deobfuscator:** (1) `sqrtPattern.findall` → return `[]` if empty; (2) `x = round(sqrt(float(x[0])))` — **Python's `round()` is BANKER'S rounding**; (3) `self.bodyPattern.findall(page)[0]` → **UNGUARDED IndexError if sqrt matched but hideTxt didn't** (caught by `_find_on_page`); (4) `unquote`, then **XOR every ODD-indexed char's code point with x** (`ord(char) ^ (x if i % 2 else 0)`), rebuild via `chr()`, `html.unescape`, then `_find_proxies`. Pure but algorithmically dense; **bypasses IPv6 handling.**<br>**→ Rust:** **four separate divergence risks:** (a) `round()` half-to-even → use `f64::round_ties_even()` NOT `round()`; (b) `chr(n)` accepts surrogates D800-DFFF, `char::from_u32` returns None → operate on `Vec<u32>` or map to REPLACEMENT_CHARACTER deliberately; (c) `html.unescape` = full HTML5 2231-entity lenient table → `html-escape` crate's `decode_html_entities`, **not `htmlescape` (incomplete)**; (d) index-0 access → `.first().ok_or(ParseError::NoBody)?`. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com` | class | `domain='tools.rosinstrument.com'`. Registered with HTTP/CONNECT:80/HTTPS/CONNECT:25.<br>**→ Rust:** shares the parser, differs only in url generation → same trait impl, different `pipe`. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com._pipe` | async method | Generates **102 urls**: `raw_free_db.htm?{pid}&t={t}` for pid in `range(51)` × t in (1,2); all fanned out at once via `_find_on_pages` (**Semaphore(4) is the only throttle**).<br>**→ Rust:** Cartesian product → `(0..51).flat_map(\|pid\| (1..3).map(move \|t\| ...))`. 102 concurrent futures is fine for tokio; the semaphore gates sockets. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_socks` | class | `domain='tools.rosinstrument.com^socks'`. Registered with SOCKS4/SOCKS5.<br>**→ Rust:** same as above; only `t=3`. |
| `Tools_rosinstrument_com_socks._pipe` | async method | Generates **51 urls**: `raw_free_db.htm?{pid}&t=3` for pid in `range(51)`, `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Xseo_in` | class | `domain='xseo.in'`. **ACTIVE** in PROVIDERS. JS char-variable port obfuscation. **Russian site — pages are historically cp1251-encoded.**<br>**→ Rust:** **encoding hazard: `resp.text()` charset sniffing must be replicated** (see port risks). |
| `Xseo_in.charEqNum` | constant | `charEqNum = {}`. **MUTABLE CLASS-LEVEL DICT shared by every `Xseo_in` instance.** `find_proxies` REBINDS `self.charEqNum = {...}` (creating an instance attr), so the class dict stays empty in practice — **but the declaration is a latent shared-state footgun and identical in form to `Spys_ru`'s, which DOES mutate it.**<br>**→ Rust:** class-level mutable default ≈ the mutable-default-arg antipattern. **Make it a local `HashMap<char, char>` built inside the parse fn and threaded to the replace closure — no struct field at all.** |
| `Xseo_in.char_js_port_to_num` | method | `re.sub` callback: takes `group(0)` chars, strips `'+'`, maps each char through `self.charEqNum`, joins into a digit string. **Raises KeyError if a char is absent from the map** (swallowed by `_find_on_page`). Reads mutable self state.<br>**→ Rust:** `Regex::replace_all` takes `impl Fn(&Captures) -> String` — a closure capturing `&map` works. Use `map.get(&ch).copied().unwrap_or_default()` or propagate. **No `&mut self` needed if the map is a local.** |
| `Xseo_in.find_proxies` | method | Builds `self.charEqNum` from `\b(?P<char>[a-z])=(?P<num>\d);` pairs (**rebinds the instance attr each call**), then `re.sub(r'\(""\+(?P<chars>[a-z+]+)\)', self.char_js_port_to_num, page)` to inline the real port digits, then `_find_proxies`. Mutates `self.charEqNum`; **bypasses IPv6 handling.**<br>**→ Rust:** two-pass regex: collect map, then `replace_all` with closure. Bound-method-as-callback → plain closure. **Keep the map local to kill the shared-state field.** |
| `Xseo_in._pipe` | async method | Single `_find_on_page(url='http://xseo.in/proxylist', data={'submit': 1}, method='POST')`. **Note `data` value is an INT — aiohttp urlencodes it as `'1'`.**<br>**→ Rust:** reqwest's `.form()` needs `&str` values — `[("submit", "1")]`. **Python's implicit int→str in urlencode must be done by hand.** |
| `Nntime_com` | class | `domain='nntime.com'`. **ACTIVE** in PROVIDERS. Same JS char-map obfuscation as `Xseo_in` but with a `(":"+chars)` port form and its own IP pattern.<br>**→ Rust:** **near-duplicate of `Xseo_in` — collapse both into one parameterized `JsCharMapParser { port_expr: Regex }`.** |
| `Nntime_com.charEqNum` | constant | `charEqNum = {}`. Mutable class-level dict; rebound (not mutated) per call by `find_proxies`, same as `Xseo_in`.<br>**→ Rust:** local HashMap; no field. |
| `Nntime_com._pattern` | constant | `re.compile(\b<ip>(?=.*?(<ip>\|(?P<port>\d{2,5}))), flags=re.DOTALL)`. Override of the global pattern adding a leading `\b` word boundary; otherwise equivalent to `IPPortPatternGlobal`.<br>**→ Rust:** `(?=...)` → `fancy-regex`. `\b` supported in both. |
| `Nntime_com.char_js_port_to_num` | method | **Byte-identical to `Xseo_in.char_js_port_to_num` (copy-paste duplication)**: strips `'+'`, maps chars through `self.charEqNum`, joins. KeyError on unknown char.<br>**→ Rust:** deduplicate — one free fn `fn js_chars_to_num(chars: &str, map: &HashMap<char, char>) -> String`. |
| `Nntime_com.find_proxies` | method | Same as `Xseo_in.find_proxies` except the port expression is `\(":"\+(?P<chars>[a-z+]+)\)` (colon-prefixed). Mutates `self.charEqNum`; bypasses IPv6 handling.<br>**→ Rust:** same parser, different `port_expr` — parameterize. |
| `Nntime_com._pipe` | async method | Generates 30 urls `proxy-updated-{01..30}.htm` (zero-padded via `{:02}`), `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** `format!("...{:02}.htm", n)` — Rust has the identical width/fill spec. |
| `Proxynova_com` | class | `domain='proxynova.com'`. **COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS** (~818 proxies historically).<br>**→ Rust:** port, disabled by default. |
| `Proxynova_com._pipe` | async method | GETs the proxy-server-list index, regex-extracts 2-letter ISO codes from `"xx"` literals, excludes `'en'`, builds per-country urls, `_find_on_pages`. **The regex is crude enough to catch any 2-letter quoted token in the page (JS, CSS, anything).**<br>**→ Rust:** trivial; **keep the crude regex for parity (tightening it changes the url set).** |
| `Spys_ru` | class | `domain='spys.ru'`. **ACTIVE** in PROVIDERS. **The most complex obfuscation: XOR-composed JS variables.** Russian site, cp1251 encoding risk. **Note `_pipe` actually targets spys.ONE, not spys.ru — `self.domain` and the fetched host disagree.**<br>**→ Rust:** **highest-effort parser to port.** Also the domain/url mismatch means the `domain` field is a display key, not a URL source — model accordingly. |
| `Spys_ru.charEqNum` | constant | `charEqNum = {}`. **MUTABLE CLASS-LEVEL DICT that IS mutated in place (`self.charEqNum[char] = int(num)`) and NEVER rebound** — so **state accumulates across every page, every call, and every `Spys_ru` instance process-wide.** Under `_find_on_pages`' concurrent gather, two pages' variable maps interleave into one shared dict; the XOR resolution path (`self.charEqNum[tochar]`) reads whatever a sibling page last wrote. **Benign under the GIL only because `find_proxies` contains no await; genuinely racy under real threads.**<br>**→ Rust:** **the port's sharpest edge. Do NOT translate to a struct field** — build a fresh `HashMap<String, i64>` per `find_proxies` call and pass `&mut` through. If parity with the accumulating behavior is required (some pages may rely on a prior page's vars), it needs `Arc<Mutex<HashMap>>` and **the race becomes explicit and lock-ordered — Rust will not let you paper over it.** |
| `Spys_ru.char_js_port_to_num` | method | `re.sub` callback **emulating a JS expression**: splits `group(0)` on `'+'`, drops the leading `''` element, and for each `(var1^var2)` term strips parens, splits on `'^'`, XORs the two mapped ints, appends the digit. Raises KeyError on unknown var, ValueError if a term lacks exactly one `'^'`. Reads `self.charEqNum`.<br>**→ Rust:** closure over `&HashMap<String, i64>`; `split_once('^').ok_or(..)`; XOR on i64. **Errors must be collected out-of-band since `replace_all`'s closure returns String (no Result)** — use a `Cell<Option<Error>>` or pre-validate. |
| `Spys_ru.find_proxies` | method | Extracts `[>;](?P<char>[a-z\d]{4,})=(?P<num>[a-z\d\^]+)` var assignments; for each, if the value contains `'^'` it resolves `int(digit) ^ self.charEqNum[tochar]` **against the ACCUMULATED map (order-dependent — a forward reference raises KeyError)**, else `int(num)`; **writes into the shared class dict.** Then `re.sub` of `(?P<js_port_code>(?:\+\([a-z0-9^+]+\))+)` through `char_js_port_to_num`, then `_find_proxies`. Mutates shared class state; bypasses IPv6 handling. Two dead commented-out `expCharNum`/`charEqNum` lines above.<br>**→ Rust:** **sequential order-dependence means no parallel iteration over the var list.** `Vec<(String,String)>` in match order, fold into a HashMap. `int(digit)` → `i64::from_str`. **The KeyError-on-forward-reference path must become `ok_or(ParseError::UnresolvedVar)`.** |
| `Spys_ru._pipe` | async method | GETs `http://spys.one/proxies/`, extracts a 32-hex session id via `re.findall(expSession, page)[0]` → **UNGUARDED IndexError that ESCAPES `_pipe` (no `_find_on_page` wrapper here) and aborts the provider whenever the page is empty/changed — the most likely live failure in the module.** Then POSTs the same url twice with `{xf0: session, xpp: 3 (200/page), xf1: 3|4 (ANM|HIA)}`. 5 lines of dead commented country-crawl code follow.<br>**→ Rust:** `.first().ok_or(ProviderError::NoSession)?`. **Form values are ints and a literal `None` in the template dict (overwritten before use)** — `reqwest.form()` needs `&[(&str,&str)]`, so serialize by hand. **This session→POST handshake is stateful: model as a two-step `async fn` returning `Vec<PageRequest>`.** |
| `My_proxy_com` | class | `domain='my-proxy.com'`. Registered with **`max_conn=2`** and NO proto (empty tuple).<br>**→ Rust:** `Semaphore::new(2)`. |
| `My_proxy_com._pipe` | async method | GETs `free-proxy-list.html`, regex-extracts `free-*` hrefs, prefixes host, **appends the seed url**, `_find_on_pages`.<br>**→ Rust:** trivial. |
| `Free_proxy_cz` | class | `domain='free-proxy.cz'`. **COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS** (~420 proxies). **Base64 host AND port.**<br>**→ Rust:** port, disabled by default. |
| `Free_proxy_cz._pattern` | constant | `re.compile(r"""decode\("([\w=]+)".*?\("([\w=]+)"\)""", flags=re.DOTALL)`. **Two groups (b64 host, b64 port) joined by a lazy DOTALL `.*?` — can pair a host with a port from an adjacent row if the markup shifts.**<br>**→ Rust:** no lookaround → plain `regex` with `(?s)` inline flag works. |
| `Free_proxy_cz.find_proxies` | method | `[(b64decode(h).decode(), b64decode(p).decode()) for h, p in self._find_proxies(page)]`. Raises `binascii.Error`/`UnicodeDecodeError` on malformed payloads (swallowed by `_find_on_page`). Bypasses IPv6 handling.<br>**→ Rust:** `base64` lenient engine + `String::from_utf8`; **`filter_map` over Results to drop bad rows instead of aborting the batch (Python aborts the WHOLE page on one bad row — decide parity).** |
| `Free_proxy_cz._pipe` | async method | Generates 14 urls `proxylist/main/date/{1..14}`, `_find_on_pages`. **Followed by ~24 lines of commented-out rate-limit-batching (15 urls then sleep 61s) and lastPageId discovery — abandoned, the site 429s without it.**<br>**→ Rust:** if re-enabled, **the commented batching is a real requirement:** `tokio::time::sleep` + chunked `join_all`, or `governor` crate for a proper rate limiter. |
| `Proxyb_net` | class | `domain='proxyb.net'`. **COMMENTED OUT of PROVIDERS** (~857 proxies). AJAX JSON-ish endpoint with b64 ports.<br>**→ Rust:** port, disabled by default. |
| `Proxyb_net._port_pattern_b64` | constant | `re.compile(r"stats\('([\w=]+)'\)")`. Single group: base64 blob per port.<br>**→ Rust:** plain `regex`. |
